parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML configuration file to load. Defaults to config.yaml in the current directory.

functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML configuration file from the specified path. Opens the file in read mode with UTF-8 encoding, uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary, and returns the parsed configuration dictionary. This function provides the primary interface for loading the framework's configuration settings from config.yaml. If the file does not exist or contains invalid YAML syntax, it will raise an exception.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Configuration dictionary loaded from config.yaml containing the printers section

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Printer description/state to filter by (e.g., 'subscribed', 'unsubscribed', 'out_of_trial_period', 'initiate_unsubscribe', 'subscribed_no_pens')

functions.what_it_does: Filters and returns printers from the configuration that match the specified description/state. First retrieves the 'printers' list from the config dictionary using config.get('printers', []). Then searches for the 'pie' key in the printers list using next() with a generator expression. Handles two cases: Case 1 - if pie_printers is a single dictionary, checks if its description matches and returns a list containing that printer's model and serial_number. Case 2 - if pie_printers is a list of dictionaries, iterates through all printers and returns a list of dictionaries containing model and serial_number for all printers where the description matches the specified value. Returns an empty list if no printers match or if the pie key is not found. This function enables data-driven testing by allowing test scripts to dynamically select printers based on their subscription state.

Session-scoped pytest fixture that configures logging for the entire test session. Automatically runs before any tests execute due to autouse=True. First removes any existing log handlers that pytest has already attached by iterating through logging.root.handlers and calling removeHandler() for each. Then configures logging using basicConfig() with INFO level, a timestamp format string showing asctime, levelname, and message. Sets up two handlers: FileHandler writing to the log file path specified in config['log_file'], and StreamHandler writing to sys.stdout for console output. This ensures all test logs are captured both in a file and displayed in the console during test execution.
functions.usage_example: # This fixture runs automatically, no explicit call needed

parameters.name: parser
parameters.description: Pytest command-line parser object for adding custom options

functions.docstring: This hook adds a new command-line option for specifying the YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Pytest hook function that adds a custom command-line option --yaml-file to the pytest command. Calls parser.addoption() with action='store' to store the provided value, default=None indicating the option is optional, and a help string explaining the option's purpose. This allows test execution to be driven by external YAML files containing test definitions and data. Tests can be run with 'pytest --yaml-file=path/to/test.yaml' to specify which YAML test definition to execute.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML file to load and parse

functions.docstring: Load and parse YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML file from the specified file path. Opens the file in read mode with UTF-8 encoding using a context manager. Uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary. Returns the parsed dictionary, or an empty dictionary if the file is empty or contains only null. Includes error handling: raises FileNotFoundError with a descriptive message if the file does not exist, and raises ValueError with the YAML parsing error details if the file contains invalid YAML syntax. This function is used internally by other functions to load both main test YAML files and imported component YAML files.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Parsed YAML data dictionary

functions.docstring: If the YAML contains flow/steps, return that value. Otherwise return the whole document.
functions.what_it_does: Extracts the flow steps from a YAML data structure. Attempts to access data['flow']['steps'] using nested dictionary access. If successful, returns the steps list. If a KeyError or TypeError occurs (meaning the flow or steps keys do not exist, or data is not a dictionary), catches the exception and returns the entire data object unchanged. This allows the function to handle both structured YAML files with a flow/steps hierarchy and simpler YAML files that contain steps directly at the root level. Used during YAML import expansion to extract the relevant test steps from imported component files.

parameters.name: base_path
parameters.description: Base directory path for resolving relative import file paths

parameters.name: node
parameters.description: YAML node to process - can be a list, dict, or scalar value

functions.docstring: Recursively expand any list items containing a 'file' key by loading the referenced YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Recursively processes a YAML data structure to expand file imports into inline content. Handles three cases: Case 1 - if node is a list, iterates through each item. If an item is a dictionary containing a 'file' key, constructs the full file path by joining config['common_test_components'] with the file value, prints the file path for debugging, loads the referenced YAML file using load_yaml_file(), extracts its flow steps using extract_flow_steps(), and extends the expanded list with those steps. If an item does not have a 'file' key, recursively processes it and appends the result. Case 2 - if node is a dictionary, recursively processes each value and returns a new dictionary with the same keys and expanded values. Case 3 - if node is a scalar value (string, number, boolean, null), returns it unchanged. This enables modular test definitions where common test components can be defined in separate YAML files and imported into main test files using {file: 'component.yaml'} syntax.

parameters.name: config_path
parameters.description: Path to the main YAML test configuration file

functions.what_it_does: Parses a YAML test flow file with support for multi-phase test definitions and file imports. First determines the base directory of the config file using os.path.dirname() and os.path.abspath(). Loads the root YAML file using load_yaml_file(). Extracts the phases dictionary from root['flow']['phases']. Initializes an empty all_steps list. Iterates through all phase values in the phases dictionary. For each phase's steps list, calls expand_imports() to recursively expand any file imports, then extends all_steps with the expanded steps. Returns a dictionary with structure {'flow': {'steps': all_steps}} containing all test steps from all phases flattened into a single list with all imports expanded. This function is the main entry point for loading YAML-driven test definitions and is called by the yaml_data fixture.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Pytest request object providing access to test context and command-line options

functions.docstring: This fixture reads the YAML file specified by the --yaml-file option and returns its content as a Python dictionary.
functions.what_it_does: Pytest fixture that provides parsed YAML test data to test functions. Retrieves the YAML file path from the command-line option using request.config.getoption('--yaml-file'). If no YAML file was provided, skips the test with pytest.skip() and an explanatory message. If a file path was provided, calls parse_flow() to load and parse the YAML file with import expansion. Includes error handling: if the file is not found, fails the test with pytest.fail() and a descriptive message. If YAML parsing fails, fails the test with the parsing error details. Returns the parsed flow dictionary containing all test steps. Test functions can access this data by declaring yaml_data as a parameter.

parameters.name: pytestconfig
parameters.description: Pytest configuration object providing access to command-line options

functions.docstring: Session-scoped fixture that returns the file path passed with --yaml-file.
functions.what_it_does: Session-scoped pytest fixture that provides the YAML file path to test functions. Retrieves the yaml_file option value from pytestconfig using getoption(). If no path was provided (value is None or empty), skips the test with pytest.skip() and a message indicating the option was not provided. If a path was provided, returns it as a string. Being session-scoped means this fixture is evaluated once per test session and the same value is reused across all tests, improving performance. Test functions can access the file path by declaring yaml_file_path as a parameter.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the MultiBrowserManager instance by creating an empty dictionary to store browser instances (self.browsers), setting the current active browser to None (self.current_browser), and detecting the operating system using platform.system() to store in self.os_system for later use in OS-specific window management operations. This constructor sets up the foundational data structures needed to track and manage multiple browser sessions.

functions.docstring: Create browser with debugger address (existing Chrome instance)
functions.what_it_does: Creates a Selenium WebDriver connection to an existing Chrome browser instance that is running with remote debugging enabled on the specified port. Configures Chrome options with debugger_address pointing to localhost:remote_port, disables logging and dev-shm-usage, adds no-sandbox flag, and excludes automation switches to avoid detection. Creates a Chrome WebDriver with these options, stores it in the browsers dictionary with metadata including driver reference, type as 'debug', profile name, remote port, a copy of original window handles, and current tab index set to 0. Prints success message with the port number and returns the driver instance. If any exception occurs during connection, prints error message and returns None.

functions.docstring: Create new incognito browser instance
functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Chrome browser instance in incognito mode. Initializes Chrome Options, adds the --incognito argument to launch in private browsing mode. If user_data_dir is provided, adds --user-data-dir argument to specify a custom profile directory. Also adds --no-default-browser-check and --no-first-run arguments to suppress Chrome startup dialogs. Creates a Chrome WebDriver with these options, stores it in the browsers dictionary with metadata including driver, type as 'incognito', profile name, user data directory path, original window handles, and current tab index. Prints success message with checkmark emoji and profile name. Returns the driver instance on success. If any exception occurs, prints error message with X emoji and returns None.

functions.docstring: Create new incognito browser instance
functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Chrome browser instance in normal (non-incognito) mode with extensive automation detection avoidance. Initializes Chrome Options and if user_data_dir is provided, adds --user-data-dir argument. Adds multiple arguments including --no-default-browser-check, --no-first-run, --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled to avoid automation detection, --no-service-autorun, --password-store=basic, --disable-infobars, and --disable-notifications. Adds experimental options to exclude enable-automation switch and disable useAutomationExtension. Configures preferences to disable credential service, password manager, and notifications. Creates Chrome WebDriver with these options, stores it in browsers dictionary with metadata (note: docstring says incognito but type is set as 'incognito' which appears to be a copy-paste error, should likely be 'normal'). Prints success message and returns driver. On exception, prints error and returns None.

functions.method_signature: def bring_to_foreground(self, browser_name: str, force_restore: bool = True):

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: browser_name
parameters.description: Name of the browser to bring to foreground

parameters.name: force_restore
parameters.description: Whether to force window restoration from minimized state

functions.docstring: Bring specific browser window to foreground
functions.what_it_does: Brings a specific browser window to the foreground using multiple methods. First checks if browser exists in registry, returns False if not found. If force_restore is True, checks current window size to detect if minimized (width or height <= 10 pixels), and if so attempts to maximize the window with a 0.5 second delay, otherwise just ensures maximized with 0.3 second delay. Then uses OS-specific commands: on Windows runs PowerShell with AppActivate to bring Chrome to front, on macOS uses osascript to activate Google Chrome, on Linux uses wmctrl. After OS commands, performs additional Selenium restoration by checking window size again, and if size is 0x0 sets window size to 1920x1080 at position 0,0. Executes JavaScript window.focus() to ensure page is active, then maximizes window again. Sleeps for 1 second to allow window state to settle. Sets this browser as current_browser and prints success message. Returns True on success. If any exception occurs, prints error and returns False.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: browser_name
parameters.description: Name of the browser to check

functions.docstring: Check if browser window is minimized or hidden
functions.what_it_does: Checks the current state of a browser window and returns a string indicating its visibility state. Returns 'not_found' if browser_name is not in the browsers registry. Gets the driver for the browser and retrieves window size and position using get_window_size() and get_window_position(). If width or height is 10 pixels or less, returns 'minimized'. If x or y position is less than -1000, returns 'off_screen'. Attempts to execute JavaScript to get document.visibilityState, and if it equals 'hidden', returns 'hidden'. If none of these conditions match, returns 'visible'. If any exception occurs during state checking, prints warning message and returns 'unknown'.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: browser_name
parameters.description: Name of the browser to ensure visibility

functions.docstring: Ensure window is visible before bringing to foreground
functions.what_it_does: Ensures a browser window is visible by first checking its current state using check_window_state() and printing the state. If the state is 'minimized', 'hidden', or 'off_screen', prints a restoration message and calls bring_to_foreground() with force_restore=True. If the state is 'visible', calls bring_to_foreground() with force_restore=False to avoid unnecessary restoration operations. For any other state (like 'unknown'), defaults to calling bring_to_foreground() with force_restore=True as a safety measure. Returns the boolean result from bring_to_foreground().

functions.docstring: Switch context to specific browser
functions.what_it_does: Switches the active browser context to the specified browser. First checks if browser_name exists in the browsers registry, and if not found prints error message and returns None. If bring_to_focus parameter is True, calls bring_to_foreground() to make the browser window visible and active. Sets self.current_browser to the browser_name to track the active browser. Returns the driver instance from the browsers dictionary for the specified browser.

functions.docstring: Load URL in specific browser
functions.what_it_does: Loads a URL in the specified browser, either in the current tab or a new tab. First checks if browser_name exists in browsers registry, prints error and returns False if not found. Gets the driver for the browser. If new_tab is True, executes JavaScript window.open() to open the URL in a new tab with '_blank' target, then switches to the new tab using driver.switch_to.window() with the last window handle, and updates the browser's current_tab_index metadata to the index of the new tab. If new_tab is False, loads the URL in the current tab using driver.get(). After loading, maximizes the window and refreshes the page. Prints success message with checkmark emoji showing the URL and browser name. Returns True on success. If any exception occurs, prints error message with X emoji and returns False.

parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.what_it_does: Returns the Selenium WebDriver instance for the currently active browser. Checks if self.current_browser is set (not None), and if so returns the 'driver' value from the browsers dictionary for that browser. If no browser is currently active (current_browser is None), returns None.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.docstring: List all managed browsers
functions.what_it_does: Prints a formatted list of all managed browsers with their current status and details. Prints a header '=== Managed Browsers ==='. Iterates through all browsers in the browsers dictionary. For each browser, gets the driver, retrieves the current URL using driver.current_url, counts the number of tabs using len(driver.window_handles). Determines status as 'ACTIVE' with fire emoji if the browser name matches current_browser, otherwise 'INACTIVE' with sleeping emoji. Prints the status, browser name, and type on one line. Prints the current URL indented on the next line. Prints the tab count indented on the third line. This provides a console-based overview of all browser sessions being managed.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of top windows to return

functions.docstring: Get top N windows across all browsers with their URLs
functions.what_it_does: Collects information about all windows across all managed browsers and returns the top N windows. Initializes an empty list all_windows. Iterates through all browsers in the browsers dictionary. For each browser, gets the current window handle, then iterates through all window handles. For each window, switches to it using driver.switch_to.window(), retrieves the URL and title, and creates a window_info dictionary containing browser_name, browser_type, window_handle, window_index, url, title, and is_active flag (True if this is the current window). Appends each window_info to all_windows. Handles exceptions for individual windows by printing warning and continuing. After processing all windows, restores the original window. Sorts all_windows by browser_name and window_index. Slices the list to get the top count windows. Prints a formatted list showing each window's status (ACTIVE or INACTIVE), browser name, type, tab index, URL, and truncated title. Returns the list of top window dictionaries.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.docstring: Get all URLs organized by browser
functions.what_it_does: Collects and organizes all window URLs grouped by browser. Initializes an empty dictionary browser_urls. Iterates through all browsers in the browsers dictionary. For each browser, gets the current window handle and initializes a dictionary entry with the browser type and an empty windows list. Iterates through all window handles for the browser. For each window, switches to it, retrieves the URL and title, and appends a dictionary to the windows list containing index, handle, url, title, and is_current flag. If an exception occurs for a window, appends an error entry with 'ERROR' in the url field and 'Could not access' as title. After processing all windows, restores the original window. Returns the browser_urls dictionary with complete window information organized by browser name.

functions.method_signature: def switch_to_window_by_url(self, url_part: str, exact_match: bool = False):

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: url_part
parameters.description: URL or URL substring to search for

parameters.name: exact_match
parameters.description: Whether to require exact URL match or allow partial match

functions.docstring: Switch to first window containing specific URL
functions.what_it_does: Searches for and switches to the first window across all browsers that matches the specified URL. Iterates through all browsers in the browsers dictionary. For each browser, saves the current window handle, then iterates through all window handles. For each window, switches to it and gets the current URL. If exact_match is True, checks if current_url exactly equals url_part. If exact_match is False, checks if url_part (case-insensitive) is contained in current_url (case-insensitive). If a match is found, prints success message with checkmark emoji showing the browser name and URL, sets this browser as current_browser, and returns a dictionary containing browser_name, window_handle, url, and driver. If no match is found in a browser, restores the original window before checking the next browser. If no match is found in any browser, prints error message with X emoji and returns None. Handles exceptions by printing warning and continuing to next browser.

functions.method_signature: def wait_for_new_window(self, timeout: int = 10, check_interval: float = 0.5) -> Tuple[Optional[str], Optional[webdriver.Chrome], Optional[str]]:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time to wait in seconds

parameters.name: check_interval
parameters.description: How often to check for new windows in seconds

functions.docstring:
Wait for and return details of any new window that appears across all browsers
        
        Args:
            timeout: Maximum time to wait in seconds
            check_interval: How often to check for new windows in seconds
            
        Returns:
            Tuple of (profile_name, driver, window_handle) or (None, None, None) if timeout
functions.what_it_does: Waits for a new window to appear in any of the managed browsers within the specified timeout period. Prints a waiting message with the timeout. Captures the initial window state for all browsers by iterating through the browsers dictionary and storing a set of window handles for each browser, printing the count of initial windows. Records the start time. Enters a while loop that continues until timeout is reached. In each iteration, checks each browser for new windows by getting the current set of window handles and computing the difference from the initial set. If new windows are found, gets the first new window handle, switches to it, attempts to get the URL and title (printing success message with details), sets this browser as current_browser, and returns a tuple of (profile_name, driver, new_handle). If no new windows are found, sleeps for check_interval seconds before checking again. If timeout is reached without finding a new window, prints timeout message and returns (None, None, None). Handles exceptions during window checking by printing warnings and continuing.

functions.method_signature: def find_window_by_url(self, target_url: str, partial_match: bool = True) -> Tuple[Optional[str], Optional[webdriver.Chrome], Optional[str]]:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: target_url
parameters.description: URL or URL part to search for

parameters.name: partial_match

functions.docstring:
Find window containing specific URL across all browsers
        
        Args:
            target_url: URL or URL part to search for
            partial_match: If True, match partial URLs; if False, exact match only
            
        Returns:
            Tuple of (profile_name, driver, window_handle) or (None, None, None) if not found
functions.what_it_does: Searches for a window with a specific URL across all managed browsers. Prints a search message indicating whether partial or exact matching is being used. Iterates through all browsers in the browsers dictionary. For each browser, saves the original window handle, then iterates through all window handles. For each window, switches to it and gets the current URL. If partial_match is True, checks if target_url (case-insensitive) is contained in current_url (case-insensitive). If partial_match is False, checks if target_url (case-insensitive) exactly equals current_url (case-insensitive). If a match is found, prints success message with checkmark emoji, sets this browser as current_browser, and returns a tuple of (profile_name, driver, window_handle). If no match is found in a browser, restores the original window before checking the next browser. If an exception occurs, prints warning, attempts to restore original window, and continues. If no match is found in any browser, prints error message with X emoji and returns (None, None, None).

functions.method_signature: def wait_for_window_with_url(self, target_url: str, timeout: int = 10, partial_match: bool = True, check_interval: float = 0.5) -> Tuple[Optional[str], Optional[webdriver.Chrome], Optional[str]]:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: target_url
parameters.description: URL or URL part to wait for

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time to wait in seconds

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, match partial URLs; if False, exact match only

parameters.name: check_interval
parameters.description: How often to check in seconds

functions.docstring:
Wait for a window with specific URL to appear
        
        Args:
            target_url: URL or URL part to wait for
            timeout: Maximum time to wait in seconds
            partial_match: If True, match partial URLs; if False, exact match only
            check_interval: How often to check in seconds
            
        Returns:
            Tuple of (profile_name, driver, window_handle) or (None, None, None) if timeout
functions.what_it_does: Waits for a window with a specific URL to appear within the specified timeout period. Prints a waiting message indicating the target URL and match type. Records the start time. Enters a while loop that continues until timeout is reached. In each iteration, calls find_window_by_url() with the target_url and partial_match parameters. If find_window_by_url() returns a result where the first element (profile_name) is not None, immediately returns that result tuple. If no match is found, sleeps for check_interval seconds before checking again. If timeout is reached without finding a matching window, prints timeout message with hourglass emoji and returns (None, None, None). This method essentially polls find_window_by_url() repeatedly until either a match is found or timeout occurs.
functions.usage_example: profile, driver, handle = manager.wait_for_window_with_url('login', timeout=20, partial_match=True, check_interval=1.0)

If it exists, calls quit() on the driver to close all windows and terminate the browser process, then deletes the browser entry from the browsers dictionary. If the closed browser was the current_browser, sets current_browser to None. Prints success message with checkmark emoji. If any exception occurs during closing, prints error message with X emoji. If the browser_name does not exist in the registry, this method does nothing.

Uses list(self.browsers.keys()) to create a copy of the keys to avoid dictionary size change during iteration issues. This method ensures all browser instances are properly terminated and cleaned up.

functions.what_it_does: Pytest fixture with module scope that creates and manages a browser instance for the entire test module. Sets browser variable to 'instantink', creates a normal browser instance using manager.create_normal_browser() with the browser name and user_data_dir set to None. If browser creation fails (driver is None), logs an error message about checking Chrome profile folder and returns without yielding. If successful, yields the driver instance to the test function. After the test completes, the fixture cleanup code calls driver.quit() to close the browser and clean up resources. This fixture ensures a single browser instance is shared across all tests in the module and is properly cleaned up after all tests complete.

functions.docstring: Test the execution of the flow using the FlowController class.
functions.what_it_does: Main test function that executes a YAML-based test case. Logs 'hp-ai-instant-services' message. Sets printer_description to 'out_of_trial_period' and retrieves the first printer with that description from config using get_printers_by_description(), extracting its serial_number. Logs the printer information. Logs test execution start message. Creates a YamlStepExecutor instance passing manager, driver, METADATA_FILEPATH, and 'instantink/Home' as the initial page. Creates a trace folder path by joining the log file directory with a folder name composed of the YAML filename (without extension) and current timestamp in format 'dd_mm_yyyy_HH_MM_SS'. Creates the trace folder using os.makedirs with exist_ok=True. Calls executor.set_variables() to set global variables including URL, COUNTRY, LANGUAGE, USERNAME, PASSWORD, REFERENCE_IMAGE_PATH, SELECTED_PRINTER (obtained by calling executor.get_selected_printer()), TMP_FOLDER, TRACE_FOLDER, CURRENT_TIME, and LARGE_WAIT. In a try block, logs test execution begin, assigns yaml_data to flow variable, prints the YAML contents using yaml.dump(), constructs flow_result path as a CSV file in the trace folder, and calls executor.execute_flow() with flow and flow_result. If any exception occurs, logs the error with the exception message.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the YamlStepExecutor with browser manager, driver, and metadata configuration. Sets current_page to None, initializes empty execution_log list, stores the manager and driver references. Creates empty control_map dictionary for UI element metadata, stores metadatapath, initializes empty variables and metadata dictionaries. Populates metadata dictionary with module name mappings: 'auth_controls' maps to 'auth_controls', 'hpsmart' to 'hpsmart', 'instantink' to 'instantink', 'oss' and 'emulate' both map to 'oss_emulator'. Defines iframe_modules list containing 'billing' to track which modules use iframes. If metadata_filepath is provided, calls load_json_metadata() with the specified page to load initial UI element definitions.

functions.method_signature: def load_yaml_file(self, file_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to YAML file to load

functions.docstring: Load and parse YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML file from the filesystem. Opens the file at file_path with UTF-8 encoding, uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary, and returns the parsed data or an empty dictionary if the file is empty. If the file is not found, raises FileNotFoundError with a descriptive message. If YAML parsing fails, raises ValueError with the YAML error details.

functions.method_signature: def load_yaml_string(self, yaml_string: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: yaml_string
parameters.description: YAML content as a string

functions.docstring: Load and parse YAML from string.
functions.what_it_does: Parses YAML content from a string. Uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the yaml_string into a Python dictionary. Calls validate_yaml_structure() on the parsed data to ensure it meets expected schema requirements. Returns the validated dictionary. If YAML parsing fails, raises ValueError with the error details.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page identifier in format 'module/page_name' (e.g., 'instantink/Dashboard')

functions.docstring:
Load the JSON metadata file into the control map.
        :param json_file: Path to the JSON file.
functions.what_it_does: Loads UI element metadata from a JSON file into the control_map. If page is None or empty, returns immediately without loading. Splits the page string on '/' to extract base module name and file name. Looks up the folder name in the metadata dictionary using the base module. Constructs the full JSON file path by joining metadatapath, folder name, and file name with '.json' extension. Opens and reads the JSON file, parsing it into the control_map dictionary using json.load(). Logs success message with the file path. If the file is not found, logs error message. If JSON parsing fails, logs decode error message.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Text or label of the UI control to find

functions.docstring:
Get the control ID for the given name from the loaded JSON metadata.
        :param name: The name or text of the control.
        :return: Control ID if found, None otherwise.
functions.what_it_does: Searches for a UI control definition in the loaded control_map by matching the provided name against the 'text' or 'label_text' fields of interactive elements. Iterates through the 'interactive_elements' array in control_map. For each control, checks if the control's 'text' field equals the provided name, or if the control's 'label_text' field equals the provided name. If a match is found, returns the entire control dictionary containing all metadata (id, css_selectors, xpath_selectors, etc.). If no match is found, returns None implicitly.

functions.method_signature: def click_control(self, control, multiple, timeout: int = 10):

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: control
parameters.description: Dictionary with element metadata (id, name, css_selectors, xpath_selectors, etc.)

parameters.name: multiple
parameters.description: Whether to click multiple matching elements or just the first

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time to wait for elements in seconds

functions.docstring:
Attempts to click a button based on metadata.
        Supports id, css, xpath, text, aria-label, name, etc.
        Falls back progressively until click succeeds.
          
        :param control: Dictionary with element metadata (e.g., id, name, css selectors, etc.).  
        :param multiple: Whether to click multiple matching elements or just the first.  
        :param timeout: Maximum time to wait for elements (default: 10 seconds).  
        :return: True if an element was clicked, False otherwise.
functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click UI elements using multiple locator strategies with progressive fallback. Validates that control is a non-empty dictionary, logging error and returning False if invalid. Builds a prioritized list of locator strategies: first all XPath selectors from control['xpath_selectors'], then all CSS selectors from control['css_selectors'], then individual strategies for id, name, aria_label, data_testid, class_name, and partial_link_text. For each strategy, validates the locator is a proper tuple with 2 elements, logging warning and skipping if invalid. Calls do_clicks() with the locator strategy, multiple flag, and timeout. If do_clicks() returns True (element was clicked), logs success message and returns True immediately. If all strategies fail to click any element, logs warning and returns False. This method prioritizes XPath over CSS and tries all available selectors before giving up.

functions.method_signature: def do_clicks(self, strategy, multiple, timeout, delay=0.10):

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: Locator strategy tuple (By.ID, 'element_id')

parameters.name: multiple
parameters.description: Whether to click multiple elements

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Delay in seconds between multiple clicks

functions.docstring:
Attempts to click one or more elements located by the given strategy.  
            
            :param strategy: Locator strategy (e.g., By.ID, By.CLASS_NAME, etc.)  
            :param multiple: Whether multiple elements should be clicked  
            :param timeout: Maximum time to wait for elements to become clickable  
            :return: True if at least one element was clicked, False otherwise
functions.what_it_does: Performs the actual clicking of elements using a given locator strategy. Initializes result to True, clicked_any to False, and clicked_count to 0. Uses WebDriverWait with the specified timeout to wait for presence_of_all_elements_located with the strategy. If no elements are found, logs debug message and returns False. Logs the count of elements found. Iterates through each element, logging its display and enabled state. Calls _try_click_element() to attempt clicking. If click fails, logs warning and continues to next element. If click succeeds, increments clicked_count, sets clicked_any to True, and logs success. If multiple is False, breaks after first successful click. If multiple is True, calls do_sleep() with the delay before clicking next element. After all elements processed, logs total clicked count. Handles TimeoutException by logging warning and setting result to False. Handles other exceptions by logging error and setting result to False. Returns True only if clicked_any is True and result is True.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: WebElement to click

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: Strategy used to locate element (for logging)

functions.docstring:
Attempts to click a single element and handles exceptions.
    
        :param element: The WebElement to click  
        :param strategy: The strategy used to locate the element (for logging purposes)  
        :return: True if the click was successful, False otherwise
functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click a single WebElement with error handling. Checks if the element is_displayed() and is_enabled(). If both conditions are true, calls element.click() and returns True. If StaleElementReferenceException occurs (element no longer attached to DOM), logs warning with strategy name and returns False. If any other exception occurs, logs error with strategy name and exception details, and returns False. This method isolates click attempts to prevent one failed click from stopping the entire click sequence.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: control
parameters.description: Metadata dictionary for the control

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to set in the control

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds

functions.docstring:
Attempts to set a value in a control based on metadata.
        Supports id, css, xpath, text, aria-label, name, etc.
        Falls back progressively until setting value succeeds.
        :param control: The metadata dictionary for the control.
        :param value: The value to set in the control.
        :param timeout: Maximum wait time for locating the control.
functions.what_it_does: Sets a value in a UI control (input field or dropdown) using multiple locator strategies. Validates control is a non-empty dictionary, logging and returning False if not. Builds a list of basic strategies for id, name, aria_label, data_testid, class_name, and partial_link_text. Extends the strategies list with all CSS selectors from control['css_selectors'] and all XPath selectors from control['xpath_selectors']. Filters out strategies with null locators. Iterates through each strategy. Uses WebDriverWait to wait for presence_of_element_located with the strategy. If element is found and its tag_name is 'select', creates a Select object and attempts to select by visible text first, falling back to select by value if that fails, logging the selection method used and returning True. If element is not a select, calls element.clear() to clear existing content, then element.send_keys() to enter the value as a string, and returns True. Handles NoSuchElementException, ElementClickInterceptedException, and TimeoutException by logging error and continuing to next strategy. Handles other exceptions by logging error and continuing. If all strategies fail, logs final error message and returns False.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: kwargs
parameters.description: Variable name-value pairs to set

functions.what_it_does: Sets multiple variables in the executor's variables dictionary for later substitution in test steps. Iterates through all keyword arguments, logging each key-value pair. Updates the self.variables dictionary with all provided kwargs using dict.update(). Returns True. This method is typically called during test setup to define variables like URL, USERNAME, PASSWORD, TRACE_FOLDER, etc. that will be referenced in YAML test steps using ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text containing variable placeholders in ${VAR_NAME} format

functions.docstring: Replace ${variable} with actual values
functions.what_it_does: Performs variable substitution in text strings, replacing ${VARIABLE_NAME} placeholders with actual values from the variables dictionary. If text is not a string, returns it unchanged. Logs the current variables dictionary. Defines an inner function replace_var() that handles the replacement logic. For each match of the pattern ${...}, extracts the variable name. If the variable name contains a dot (e.g., ${temp_id.status}), splits on the dot to get the variable name and key, retrieves the variable value from self.variables, and if it's a dictionary, returns the value for the key, otherwise returns the original match unchanged. If the variable name has no dot, returns the string value from self.variables or the original match if not found. Uses re.sub() with the pattern \$\{([^}]+)\} to find all ${...} patterns and replace them using the replace_var function. Returns the text with all variables substituted. This supports both simple variables (${URL}) and nested dictionary access (${PRINTER.serial_number}).

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Path where screenshot should be saved

functions.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of the current browser page and saves it to the specified filename. Calls self.driver.save_screenshot() with the filename parameter. Returns the filename string. This method is used during verify actions to capture the current page state for AI vision analysis.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: reference
parameters.description: Path to reference image file or None

parameters.name: test
parameters.description: Path to test screenshot image file

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Prompt text for the AI vision model

functions.what_it_does: Performs AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision models. Extracts Azure endpoint, deployment name, API key, and API version from config. Initializes result to None. Creates AzureOpenAI client with the configuration. If reference image path is provided, opens and base64 encodes it, otherwise sets reference_image to None. Opens and base64 encodes the test image. Logs the prompt being used. Constructs messages array for the API call: if no reference image, creates a single user message with the prompt text and test image; if reference image exists, creates a user message with prompt, reference image, and test image. Calls client.chat.completions.create() with the deployment model, messages, max_tokens=6000, temperature=0, top_p=1, frequency_penalty=0, presence_penalty=0, and seed=12345 for deterministic results. Checks if response is streaming or non-streaming. If streaming, calls handle_streaming_response() to collect chunks. If non-streaming, extracts content from response.choices[0].message.content. Strips backticks, 'json', and whitespace from the result. Parses the cleaned result as JSON using json.loads(). Logs the parsed data and returns it. This method enables visual verification of UI states by sending screenshots to GPT-4 Vision with specific validation prompts.
functions.usage_example: result = executor.run_ai_task(None, 'screenshot.png', 'Verify login button is visible')

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: Streaming response object from OpenAI API

functions.docstring: Handle streaming response with try-except for safety
functions.what_it_does: Handles streaming responses from Azure OpenAI API by collecting content chunks. Logs the response object. Initializes empty result string. Iterates through each chunk in the response. For each chunk, wraps access in try-except to handle chunks without content. Checks if chunk.choices exists and chunk.choices[0].delta.content exists. If content is present, appends it to the result string. If IndexError or AttributeError occurs (normal for chunks without content), continues to next chunk. Returns the concatenated result string. This method safely handles the streaming response format where not all chunks contain content.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Configuration dictionary containing printer definitions

parameters.name: stack
parameters.description: Environment stack name (e.g., 'stage', 'prod')

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the serial number of the printer marked as selected for a specific environment stack. Iterates through config['printers'] list. For each item, checks if the stack key exists in the item dictionary. If stack key exists, iterates through the printers in that stack. For each printer, checks if the 'selected_printer' field is True. If found, logs the serial number and returns it. If no selected printer is found in any stack, returns None. This method enables environment-specific printer selection for tests.

functions.method_signature: def execute_click_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing action parameters

functions.what_it_does: Executes a click action on a UI element based on step parameters. Extracts element_id, label, page, text, expand, parent, and multiple from the step dictionary with defaults. Initializes result to True. If page is specified, loads JSON metadata for that page. Converts multiple and expand string values to boolean. Initializes to_click to True. If expand is True, calls navigation_element_expanded() to check if the element is already expanded, and sets to_click to the negation of that result (only click if not already expanded). If to_click is True, calls get_control() with text to find the control. If control is found, checks if it has an iframe definition, and if so finds the iframe element and switches to it using driver.switch_to.frame(). Calls click_control() with the control and multiple flag. If control is not found by text, tries get_control() with label. If found, handles iframe switching if needed and calls click_control(). If neither text nor label finds a control, logs error and sets result to False. If expand is True and element is already expanded, logs that no click is needed. Logs the click action details and calls log_action(). Returns result boolean.

functions.method_signature: def execute_set_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing action parameters

functions.docstring: Execute set/input action.
functions.what_it_does: Executes a set/input action to enter text into a UI control. Extracts element_id, text, value, and page from the step dictionary. Initializes result to True. If page is specified, loads JSON metadata for that page. If page contains 'emulate', logs context switch message and calls manager.switch_to_window_by_url() to switch to the emulator window. Calls substitute_variables() on the value to replace any variable placeholders. Calls get_control() with text to find the control. If control is found, calls set_control_value() with the control and substituted value. If control is not found, logs error and sets result to False. Logs the set action details and calls log_action(). Returns result boolean.

functions.method_signature: def execute_verify_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing verification parameters

functions.docstring: Execute verification action.
functions.what_it_does: Executes AI-powered visual verification of the current page state. Extracts element_id, prompt, text, screenshot, and reference_image from the step dictionary. Initializes result to True. Converts reference_image to None if it's None, 'None', or empty string, otherwise constructs full path by joining REFERENCE_IMAGE_PATH variable with the reference_image filename. Logs the reference image path. If screenshot is specified, constructs temp filename in TMP_FOLDER, calls take_screenshot() to capture current page, constructs destination filename in TRACE_FOLDER using element_id, copies the screenshot to trace folder using shutil.copy(), and logs the paths. Formats the prompt string using format() to substitute CURRENT_TIME variable with formatted time. Calls run_ai_task() with reference_image, test_image, and formatted prompt to perform AI verification. Logs the verification details including element_id, text, and result. Calls log_action(). Returns the result from AI verification.
functions.usage_example: result = executor.execute_verify_action({'action': 'verify', 'id': 'login_page_check', 'prompt': 'Verify login button is visible', 'screenshot': True})

functions.method_signature: def execute_select_printer_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing printer selection parameters

functions.docstring: Execute select_printer action.
functions.what_it_does: Executes a complex printer selection workflow specific to the application's printer selector UI. Logs the step details. Extracts element_id, text, and page from step. Initializes result to True. Waits for the printer selector expand button (data-testid='selector-collapsed') to be clickable with 10 second timeout. Scrolls the expand button into view using JavaScript scrollIntoView. Clicks the expand button to open the printer dropdown. Logs that dropdown is being clicked. Waits for printer categories to load by waiting for elements with class 'selectablePrinters__selectable-printers-container-new-visid' with 15 second timeout. Calls substitute_variables() on text to get the desired serial number. Logs the serial number. Initializes found flag to False. Iterates through each category, extracting the category title. For each category, finds all printer elements. For each printer, waits for printer_info, printer_name_element, and serial_number_element. Extracts printer name and serial number using JavaScript textContent to handle hidden elements. Strips 'S/N: ' prefix from serial number. Logs each printer found. Compares serial number with desired serial number. If match found, logs selection message, scrolls printer into view, waits for element to be clickable, uses ActionChains to move to element and click, clicks the expand button again to close dropdown, sets found to True and breaks. If found, breaks category loop. If not found after all categories, logs error and sets result to False. Handles exceptions by logging errors and setting result to False. Logs the action and returns result.

functions.method_signature: def execute_load_url_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing URL loading parameters

functions.what_it_does: Executes a URL loading action in a specific browser. Extracts element_id, url (from text field), browser (default 'instantink'), new_tab (default 'False'), and page from step. Initializes result to True. Calls substitute_variables() on url to replace variable placeholders. Calls manager.load_url_in_browser() with browser name, url, and new_tab flag to load the URL. Logs the action details including page, element_id, url, browser, and new_tab. Calls log_action(). Returns the result from load_url_in_browser().

functions.method_signature: def execute_vscroll_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing scroll parameters

functions.what_it_does: Executes a vertical scroll action either to a specific element or by page. Extracts element_id, text, and direction from step. Initializes result to True. Finds the body element and clicks it to ensure focus. If text is specified, calls get_control() to find the control, builds strategies list for id, name, css_selectors, xpath_selectors, aria_label, data_testid, class_name, and partial_link_text. Iterates through strategies, skipping null locators. Uses WebDriverWait to find the element with the strategy. Executes JavaScript scrollIntoView with block: 'center' to scroll the element into view. Logs the scroll action and breaks. If text is not specified, sets direction to Keys.PAGE_UP if direction is truthy, otherwise Keys.PAGE_DOWN. Sends the direction key to the body element to scroll by a page. Logs the page scroll. Logs the action details and calls log_action(). Returns result.

functions.method_signature: def execute_refresh_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary

functions.what_it_does: Executes a browser page refresh action. Extracts element_id from step. Initializes result to True. Calls self.driver.refresh() to reload the current page. Logs the refresh action with element_id. Calls log_action(). Returns result.

functions.method_signature: def execute_escape_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary

functions.what_it_does: Executes an escape key press action to close modals or popups. Extracts element_id from step. Initializes result to True. Finds the body element and sends Keys.ESCAPE to it. Logs the escape action with element_id. Calls log_action(). Returns result.

functions.method_signature: def execute_store_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing variable to store

functions.what_it_does: Stores a variable value for later use in the test. Extracts element_id and text from step. Initializes result to True. If text is specified and not equal to '${}'  (empty placeholder), strips '${' and '}' from text to get the variable name. If the cleaned variable name (case-insensitive) equals 'current_time', stores datetime.now() in variables dictionary. Otherwise stores 'unknown' as the value. Logs the stored variable name and value. If text is not recognized, logs error. Logs the action details and calls log_action(). Returns result.

functions.method_signature: def execute_select_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any], timeout=10) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing select parameters

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds

functions.what_it_does: Executes a select action to choose an option from a dropdown or enter text in a field. Extracts element_id, text, label, value, and page from step. Initializes result to True. In a try block, if page is specified, loads JSON metadata for that page. Logs the text and label. Calls get_control() with text, and if not found, tries with label. If control has an iframe, finds the iframe element and switches to it. Uses WebDriverWait to find the element by control['id']. Sends the value as a string to the element using send_keys(). If iframe was used, switches back to default content. Handles exceptions by logging error. Logs the action details and calls log_action(). Returns result.

functions.method_signature: def execute_locale_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing locale parameters

functions.what_it_does: Sets locale variables for country and language. Extracts element_id, page, country (default 'US'), and language (default 'English') from step. Initializes result to True. Comment indicates a lookup in locales.yaml should be done but is not implemented. Sets variables['country'] to the country value and variables['language'] to the language value. Logs the action details and calls log_action(). Returns result.

functions.method_signature: def execute_custom_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary

functions.docstring: Execute custom/unknown actions.
functions.what_it_does: Handles custom or unknown action types that don't match any predefined action handlers. Extracts action from step with default 'unknown'. Initializes result to True. Logs the custom action type and full step data. Calls log_action(). Returns result. This is a fallback handler for extensibility.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Sleep duration in seconds

functions.docstring: Handle sleep/wait with proper timing.
functions.what_it_does: Pauses test execution for a specified duration. If duration is greater than 0, logs a sleep message with the duration and calls time_module.sleep() with the duration. If duration is 0 or negative, does nothing. This method provides controlled delays between test steps.

functions.method_signature: def execute_step(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary containing action and parameters

functions.docstring: Execute a single test step based on its action type.
functions.what_it_does: Main step execution dispatcher that routes steps to appropriate action handlers. Validates step is a dictionary, raising ValueError if not. Initializes result to True. Extracts action (lowercase) and page from step. If page is specified, updates current_page. Logs step execution start with action and current page. Uses if-elif chain to match action type and call corresponding execute method: 'click' calls execute_click_action, 'set' or 'input' calls execute_set_action, 'load_url' calls execute_load_url_action, 'select_printer' calls execute_select_printer_action, 'vscroll' calls execute_vscroll_action, 'escape' calls execute_escape_action, 'verify' calls execute_verify_action, 'refresh' calls execute_refresh_action, 'store' calls execute_store_action, 'locale' calls execute_locale_action, 'select' calls execute_select_action, otherwise calls execute_custom_action. After action execution, extracts sleep_time from step with default 0, calls substitute_variables() on it, converts to int, and logs the sleep time. If sleep_time is an integer, calls do_sleep(). Returns the result from the action handler.

functions.method_signature: def execute_flow(self, yaml_data: Dict[str, Any], flow_result=None) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: yaml_data
parameters.description: Parsed YAML data containing the flow definition

parameters.name: flow_result
parameters.description: Path to CSV file where results should be saved

functions.docstring: Execute the complete test flow.
functions.what_it_does: Executes a complete test flow from YAML data and generates a CSV result report. Validates yaml_data contains 'flow' key, raising ValueError if not. Initializes empty workflow_results list. Extracts flow and steps from yaml_data. If no steps found, logs message and returns. Logs flow execution start with total step count. Initializes result to True. Iterates through steps with enumeration starting at 1. For each step, logs separator line and step number. Records start_time using time_module.time(). Calls execute_step() in a try block, storing the result. If exception occurs, logs error, calls log_error(), and continues to next step. Records end_time and calculates duration. Appends a dictionary to workflow_results containing step_name (from step['id']), start_time (formatted with ctime), duration_seconds (rounded to 4 decimals), and status (the result boolean). After all steps, if flow_result path is provided and workflow_results is not empty, creates a pandas DataFrame from workflow_results and saves it to CSV using to_csv() with index=False. Logs the save location. Logs completion message and calls print_execution_summary().

functions.method_signature: def log_action(self, action_type: str, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: action_type
parameters.description: Type of action executed

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary

functions.docstring: Log executed action for reporting.
functions.what_it_does: Records an executed action in the execution log for reporting purposes. Creates a log_entry dictionary containing timestamp (current datetime in ISO format), action_type, step_data (copy of the step dictionary), and status set to 'success'. Appends the log_entry to the execution_log list.

functions.method_signature: def log_error(self, step_number: int, step: Dict[str, Any], error_message: str) -> None:

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: step_number
parameters.description: Step number that failed

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step dictionary

parameters.name: error_message
parameters.description: Error message

functions.docstring: Log execution error.
functions.what_it_does: Records a failed step execution in the execution log. Creates a log_entry dictionary containing timestamp (current datetime in ISO format), step_number, step_data (copy of the step dictionary), status set to 'error', and error_message. Appends the log_entry to the execution_log list.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.docstring: Print execution summary.
functions.what_it_does: Prints a summary of test execution results. Initializes successful_count and failed_count to 0. Iterates through execution_log, incrementing successful_count for entries with status 'success' and failed_count for entries with status 'error'. Logs execution summary header. Logs total steps executed (length of execution_log). Logs successful steps count. Logs failed steps count. If failed_count is greater than 0, logs 'Failed steps:' header and iterates through execution_log again, logging step number and error message for each failed step.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.docstring: Get the complete execution log.
functions.what_it_does: Returns a copy of the complete execution log as a list of dictionaries. Creates a new list from self.execution_log using list() constructor and returns it. This prevents external modification of the internal execution log.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text of the navigation element to check

functions.what_it_does: Checks if a navigation element is in expanded state using AI vision. Gets TMP_FOLDER from variables. Constructs filename 'expand.png' in the temp folder. Calls take_screenshot() to capture current page. Constructs a prompt asking the AI to focus on the navigation pane, use chevron direction (^ for expanded, v for collapsed) to detect state, and determine if the specified text element is expanded or collapsed. The prompt uses strict validation mode requesting a JSON response with status true or false. Calls run_ai_task() with no reference image, the test screenshot, and the prompt. Logs the result. Returns the 'status' value from the AI result dictionary.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: prompt_lines
parameters.description: List of strings, each representing a line of the prompt

parameters.name: image_base64
parameters.description: Base64 encoded image string

parameters.name: image_url
parameters.description: Image URL

functions.docstring:
Convert prompt lines array into OpenAI message content array format
        
        Args:
            prompt_lines: List of strings, each representing a line of the prompt
            image_base64: Base64 encoded image string (optional)
            image_url: Image URL (optional, use either this or image_base64)
        
        Returns:
            List of content objects for OpenAI API
functions.what_it_does: Converts prompt lines and optional images into OpenAI API message content format. Initializes empty content list. Iterates through prompt_lines, and for each non-empty line (after stripping whitespace), appends a dictionary with type 'text' and text set to the stripped line. If image_base64 is provided, appends a dictionary with type 'image_url' and image_url containing a data URL with the base64 image. If image_url is provided instead, appends a dictionary with type 'image_url' and image_url set to the provided URL. Returns the content list. This method structures data for multi-modal AI requests.
functions.usage_example: content = executor.create_message_content(['Check if button exists'], image_base64=img_data)

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: Streaming response from OpenAI

functions.docstring: Debug what's actually coming through the stream
functions.what_it_does: Debug utility to inspect streaming response chunks from OpenAI API. Initializes chunk_count to 0 and full_content to empty string. Iterates through response chunks, incrementing chunk_count. For each chunk, tries to access chunk.choices[0].delta.content. If content exists, appends it to full_content and logs the chunk number and content. If IndexError or AttributeError occurs, logs that the chunk has no content. After all chunks, logs a separator, the full concatenated result, and another separator. Returns the full_content string. This is a debugging tool to understand streaming response structure.

Parses and validates command-line arguments for the AURA framework. First checks if --version flag is present and if so, prints the framework version and exits. Then prints a banner with the framework version. If --addflow is present, creates a simplified parser that only accepts the --addflow argument and returns. Otherwise, creates a full argument parser with all framework options including --flow, --mode, --stack, --locale, --language, --printer_profile, --easy_enroll, --headless, --biz_model, --target, --simulator_platform, --debug, --payment_method, --collect_logs, --requirements, --base_yaml, and --batch_name. Validates that required arguments are present based on the mode: if mode is 'yaml', requires --base_yaml; otherwise requires --flow, --stack, --locale, and --target. For web platforms (web_chrome, web_safari, web_firefox), ensures --simulator_platform is not provided. For app platforms (windows_app, mac_app, android_app, ios_app), requires --simulator_platform and validates it against the allowed simulators for that platform using SIMULATOR_PLATFORM_MAPPING. Returns the parsed argparse.Namespace object containing all argument values.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Parsed command-line arguments containing flow name, stack, locale, target platform, and other configuration options

functions.what_it_does: Builds a comprehensive flow arguments dictionary from parsed command-line arguments by loading and resolving all necessary configuration data. First validates that the flow directory exists under Constants.TEST_FLOWS_PATH, raising FlowConfigError if not found. Loads locale data from locale_map.json using load_locale_data() and validates that the specified country exists in the map. Extracts region, languages list, country short code, and card_payment_gateway from the locale data. Determines the language to use (from --language arg or first language in locale's language list). Constructs the locale_code in format 'language-shortcode' (e.g., 'en-US'). Loads address data for the country using load_address_for_country(), raising FlowConfigError if no address found. If --printer_profile is specified, loads printer profiles using load_printer_profiles() and calls get_printer_details_for_country() to resolve the profile name, derivative_model, supplyvariant, and biz_model for the specified region and easy_enroll filter. Retrieves resolution configuration (width and height) from RESOLUTION_CONFIGS dictionary based on the target platform. If --simulator_platform is specified, retrieves the simulator_platform value from SIMULATOR_CONFIGS dictionary. Constructs and returns a dictionary containing all flow configuration including stack, locale, language, profile, derivative_model, biz_model, supplyvariant, easy_enroll, tenant_country, tenant_country_short, tenant_country_language_code, card_payment_gateway, payment_method, tenant_address, headless, target, simulator_platform, target_width, target_height, collect_logs, and requirements (parsed from --requirements string). Logs debug information about resolved locale, address, printer profile, and final flow args JSON.

parameters.name: req_str
parameters.description: Requirements string in format '[key1:value1, key2:value2]' or 'key1:value1,key2:value2' containing dynamic test requirements

functions.docstring:
Parse the --requirements into a dict.
    Accepted input examples:
      "[plan_pages:50, hpplus:activate, flip_shipping:skip]"
      "plan_pages:50,hpplus:activate"
      "plan_pages:50" (single)
    Rules:
      - Outer brackets [] optional.
      - Items separated by comma.
      - Each item key:value (first ':' splits key and value only).
      - Keys normalized to snake_case; values kept as string except numeric digits -> int.
      - Empty / malformed entries ignored.
functions.what_it_does: Parses a requirements string into a dictionary of key-value pairs for dynamic test configuration. Returns empty dict if req_str is None or empty. Strips whitespace from the input string. If the string starts with '[' and ends with ']', removes these outer brackets. Splits the string by comma to get individual requirement entries. For each entry, checks if it contains a colon separator. If no colon found, logs a debug message and ignores that entry. Splits each entry by the first colon only into key and value. Normalizes the key to snake_case using the to_snake_case() helper function. Trims whitespace from both key and value. If the value matches the regex pattern for an integer (including negative numbers), converts it to an int type. Stores the key-value pair in the result dictionary if the key is not empty. Logs the final parsed requirements dictionary at debug level. Returns the dictionary containing all parsed requirements with snake_case keys and appropriately typed values (int for numeric strings, str for others).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: String to convert to snake_case format

functions.what_it_does: Converts a string from camelCase or PascalCase to snake_case format. Uses two regex substitutions: first substitution uses pattern '(.)([A-Z][a-z]+)' to insert underscore between a character and an uppercase letter followed by lowercase letters (handles PascalCase like 'PlanPages' -> 'Plan_Pages'). Second substitution uses pattern '([a-z0-9])([A-Z])' to insert underscore between lowercase/digit and uppercase letter (handles camelCase like 'planPages' -> 'plan_Pages'). Finally converts the entire result to lowercase using .lower(). Returns the snake_case formatted string. For example, 'planPages' becomes 'plan_pages', 'PlanPages' becomes 'plan_pages', 'flipShipping' becomes 'flip_shipping'.

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the new test flow to create

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new test flow directory structure with all necessary files and folders. Constructs the base directory path as test_flows/flow_name. Creates a test_data subdirectory and a zSample subdirectory within it using os.makedirs with exist_ok=True. Converts the flow_name to snake_case format using to_snake_case() helper. Creates the main flow Python file at base_dir/snake_name.py with a header comment showing the relative path. Creates an empty __init__.py file in the base directory to make it a Python package. Creates a sample Excel spreadsheet file named snake_name_testdata.xlsx in the test_data/zSample directory using openpyxl library. The spreadsheet is created with a single sheet named 'Sheet1'. Prints a confirmation message showing the created structure including the base directory path, main Python file name, and sample spreadsheet name with location. This function is called when the --addflow command-line argument is used to scaffold a new test flow.

parameters.name: base_yaml
parameters.description: Path to the YAML file containing batch definitions

parameters.name: selected_batches
parameters.description: List of batch names to execute. If None, all batches in the YAML file will be processed

functions.docstring:
Process the YAML file and execute commands for the specified batches.  
     
    :param base_yaml: Path to the YAML file.  
    :param selected_batches: List of batch names to execute (None means execute all).
functions.what_it_does: Processes a YAML file containing batch definitions and executes test files for specified batches. Opens and reads the base_yaml file using yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary. Extracts the 'batches' list from the parsed data. Iterates through each batch in the batches list. For each batch, extracts the 'name' and 'files' list. If selected_batches parameter is provided and the current batch name is not in that list, prints a message indicating the batch was not found and skips to the next batch using continue. If the batch should be processed, prints 'Processing Batch: {name}'. Iterates through each file in the batch's files list. For each file, prints 'Executing file: {file}' with indentation. Contains a try-except block with a commented-out subprocess.run() call that would execute pytest with the file (currently just prints a separator line). If a subprocess error occurs, prints the error message. This function is part of the YAML-to-Aura merge effort and is called when --mode yaml is specified with a --base_yaml file.

Main entry point function for the AURA framework that orchestrates the entire test execution flow. Calls parse_args() to parse and validate command-line arguments. If --base_yaml argument is provided, prints a message indicating YAML-based execution, extracts the base_yaml path and optional batch_name, and calls process_yaml() with these parameters to execute YAML-defined test batches. If no base_yaml is provided, proceeds with native Aura execution: checks if --debug flag is set and if so, calls set_framework_log_level(logging.DEBUG) to enable debug logging and logs a debug message. Checks if --addflow argument is provided and if so, calls add_flow() with the flow name and returns immediately. Otherwise, wraps the main execution in a try-except block: calls build_flow_args() to construct the flow configuration dictionary from parsed arguments, logs the flow arguments as formatted JSON at info level. Based on the --mode argument, routes execution to the appropriate mode handler: calls run_scrape_mode() for 'scrape' mode, run_validate_mode() for 'validate' mode, or run_noop_mode() for 'noop' mode. If mode is invalid, logs an error and exits with code 1. Catches FlowConfigError exceptions, logs the error message, and exits with code 1. Catches all other exceptions, logs the full traceback using traceback.format_exc(), and exits with code 1. This function is called from the if __name__ == '__main__' block when the script is executed directly.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PlaywrightManager instance by setting all browser-related attributes to None or their default values. Sets self.playwright to None (will hold the Playwright instance), self.browser to Optional[Browser] None (will hold the browser instance), self.context to Optional[BrowserContext] None (will hold the browser context), self.page to Optional[Page] None (will hold the page instance), and self.logs_path to Optional[str] None (will hold the path where HAR and video logs are saved). These attributes are populated later when the setup() method is called.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.docstring: Setup playwright, browser, context and page
functions.what_it_does: Performs complete Playwright browser setup and returns a ready-to-use Page object. First starts Playwright by calling sync_playwright().start() and stores it in self.playwright. Reads the target platform from GlobalState.target and determines the browser type: 'webkit' for web_safari/mac_app/ios_app, 'firefox' for web_firefox, or 'chromium' for all others. Constructs launch_args dictionary with headless mode from GlobalState.headless. Constructs context_args dictionary with locale from GlobalState.language_code, viewport width and height from GlobalState.target_width and target_height, and device_scale_factor set to 2.0. Checks if HAR logging is enabled (GlobalState.collect_logs is 'har' or 'both') and if so, creates a logs directory in GlobalState.run_dir, constructs a HAR file path with flow name and timestamp, and adds record_har_path to context_args. Checks if video recording is enabled (GlobalState.collect_logs is 'video' or 'both') and if so, adds record_video_dir and record_video_size to context_args.
