functions.what_it_does: Retrieves printer details (profile name, SKU, supply variant, business model) for a specific printer profile and region with optional easy enroll filtering. First validates that profile_name exists in printer_profiles dictionary, raising ValueError if not found. Gets the SKUs dictionary for the profile and validates that the region exists, raising ValueError if not. Retrieves the list of SKUs for the region. If easy_enroll_flag is provided, converts it to boolean (checking if it equals 'yes' case-insensitively) and filters the SKUs list to only include SKUs where the 'easy_enroll' field matches the desired boolean value. Raises ValueError if no matching SKUs are found after filtering. Selects the first SKU from the (possibly filtered) list and extracts the 'sku' and 'supplyvariant' values. Returns a tuple of (profile_name, sku, supplyvariant, biz_model). This is used to get printer configuration details for test setup.

parameters.name: svg_xml
parameters.description: SVG XML content as string

parameters.name: fallback_duration_ms
parameters.description: Fallback duration in milliseconds if no duration found. Defaults to 1000.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animation duration from SVG XML content by searching for SMIL dur attributes or CSS animation-duration properties. First uses regex to search for SMIL dur attribute pattern 'dur="value(ms|s)"'. If found, extracts the numeric value and unit, converts to milliseconds (dividing by 1000 if unit is 'ms', keeping as-is if 's'), multiplies by 1000 to get milliseconds, converts to int and returns. If SMIL pattern not found, searches for CSS animation-duration pattern 'animation-duration: value(ms|s)' and performs the same conversion. If neither pattern is found, returns the fallback_duration_ms value. This helps determine appropriate frame capture timing for animated SVGs.

functions.method_signature: def extract_animated_svgs(page, min_size=5120, poll_interval=0.02, max_frames=100, upscale_factor=4, save_debug_frames=True, debug_frame_dir='./debug_svg_frames')

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum SVG size in bytes to consider. Defaults to 5120.

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Seconds between frame captures. Defaults to 0.02.

parameters.name: max_frames
parameters.description: Maximum frames to capture. Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: upscale_factor
parameters.description: Factor to upscale SVG for capture. Defaults to 4.

parameters.name: save_debug_frames
parameters.description: Whether to save individual debug frames. Defaults to True.

parameters.name: debug_frame_dir
parameters.description: Directory for debug frames. Defaults to './debug_svg_frames'.

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive function that detects, captures, and converts animated SVG elements on a page to GIF format with extensive animation detection logic. Queries all SVG elements on the page. For each SVG: checks visibility, extracts outer HTML, validates size against min_size threshold. Detects animation using multiple strategies: SMIL tags (animate, animateTransform), CSS animations (animationName, transitionProperty), JavaScript timers, requestAnimationFrame, script tags, and event handlers. Performs hard reset of animations by removing and re-adding the element to the DOM. Triggers animations via hover and mouseenter events. Waits a fixed initial delay. Captures multiple detection frames and compares them pixel-by-pixel using PIL Image and ImageChops to detect visual changes. If animation detected, upscales the SVG by modifying width/height attributes. Captures frames at poll_interval until max_frames reached or animation loops (detected by comparing frame hash to first frame hash). Calculates per-frame duration based on loop period. Restores original SVG size. Converts frames to GIF using PIL with palette optimization and disposal mode 2. Returns list of dictionaries containing element handle, outer HTML, GIF bytes, frame count, resolution, animation type, and timing information. This is a highly sophisticated animation extraction system with extensive logging and debugging capabilities.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum GIF size in bytes. Defaults to 1024.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animated GIF images from a web page by finding img elements with .gif sources and validating they are truly animated. Queries all img elements with src containing '.gif' using page.query_selector_all(). For each GIF element: checks visibility, gets the src attribute, validates it contains '.gif'. Makes an HTTP request to fetch the GIF bytes using page.context.request.get(). Validates response status is 200 and content size is at least min_size bytes. Opens the GIF bytes with PIL Image and checks if it has is_animated attribute set to True and n_frames > 1 to confirm it's actually animated (not a static GIF). If all validations pass, appends a dictionary to results containing the element handle, src URL, raw bytes, and size. Returns the list of animated GIF dictionaries. Wraps each element processing in try-except to continue on errors. This is used to capture existing animated GIFs for validation comparison.

functions.method_signature: def extract_animated_visuals(page, selector='.stepsImage', min_size=1024, screenshot_dir='.', context_hint=None, poll_interval=0.03, max_frames=60, detect_loop=True, save_debug_frames=False)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: CSS selector for elements to capture. Defaults to '.stepsImage'.

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum output size in bytes. Defaults to 1024.

parameters.name: screenshot_dir
parameters.description: Directory to save screenshots. Defaults to current directory.

parameters.name: context_hint
parameters.description: Optional context name for filename generation.

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Seconds between frame captures. Defaults to 0.03.

parameters.name: max_frames
parameters.description: Maximum frames to capture. Defaults to 60.

parameters.name: detect_loop
parameters.description: Whether to detect animation loops. Defaults to True.

parameters.name: save_debug_frames
parameters.description: Whether to save individual debug frames. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animated visual elements (SVG, Canvas, Lottie, CSS sprites) from a page by capturing frame sequences and converting to GIF or PNG. Queries all elements matching the selector. For each element: checks visibility, gets inner HTML, determines animation type by checking for SVG tags, canvas elements, 'lottie' text, or CSS background images. Generates a filename using context_hint and animation type. Captures frames in a loop up to max_frames: forces repaint by setting random outline style, scrolls element into view, takes screenshot, opens as PIL Image, computes SHA256 hash of image bytes, optionally saves debug frame PNG. If detect_loop is True and frame hash matches first frame hash after 3 frames, breaks loop indicating animation has looped. Determines if animated by checking for multiple unique frame hashes. If animated with 2+ frames, calculates duration per frame based on loop period if detected, converts frames to palette mode, saves as GIF with calculated duration and loop=0. If not animated, saves first frame as PNG. Reads the output file bytes. If size is less than min_size, deletes the file and skips. Appends result dictionary with element, animation_type, screenshot_path, is_animated, frames_captured, duration_per_frame_ms, size, and tag. Returns list of results. This provides flexible animation capture for various animation technologies with loop detection and automatic GIF/PNG selection.

parameters.name: exclude_str
parameters.description: String representation of exclusion regions, typically from Excel cell

functions.what_it_does: Parses a string representation of exclusion regions into a list of tuples. If exclude_str is empty or None, returns an empty list. Otherwise, uses ast.literal_eval() to safely parse the string as a Python literal (list or tuple). If the result is a single tuple (e.g., '(10, 20, 100, 50)'), wraps it in a list to ensure consistent list-of-tuples format. Returns the list of exclusion tuples where each tuple is (x, y, width, height). If parsing fails due to invalid syntax, catches the exception and returns an empty list. This is used to parse exclusion region data stored as strings in Excel cells.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of the pixel

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of the pixel

parameters.name: exclusions
parameters.description: List of exclusion region tuples (x, y, width, height)

functions.what_it_does: Checks if a pixel coordinate (x, y) falls within any of the defined exclusion regions. Iterates through each exclusion tuple in the exclusions list. For each exclusion, unpacks the tuple as (ex_x, ex_y, ex_w, ex_h) representing the top-left corner and dimensions of the exclusion rectangle. Checks if the pixel x coordinate is within the range [ex_x, ex_x + ex_w) and the y coordinate is within [ex_y, ex_y + ex_h). If both conditions are true, the pixel is inside this exclusion region and the function returns True immediately. If no exclusion region contains the pixel after checking all regions, returns False. This is used during image comparison to determine if a differing pixel should be ignored because it's in an excluded area.

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: List of (x, y) pixel coordinates representing failed pixels

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Tuple of (width, height) representing image dimensions

functions.what_it_does: Generates bounding box rectangles around clusters of failed pixels using flood-fill algorithm. If pixels list is empty, returns empty list. Creates a binary mask image using PIL Image.new() with mode 'L' (grayscale) initialized to 0 (black). Sets all failed pixel coordinates to 255 (white) in the mask. Initializes a visited set to track processed pixels and an empty boxes list. Defines an inner flood_fill function that performs iterative flood-fill starting from a seed pixel using a stack-based approach. The flood-fill explores connected white pixels (value 255) in 4 directions (left, right, up, down), tracking the minimum and maximum x and y coordinates to determine the bounding box. Returns a tuple (x, y, width, height) for the bounding box. Iterates through all pixels in the mask row by row. For each white pixel not yet visited, calls flood_fill to get its bounding box. Only adds boxes to the result list if both width and height are at least MIN_TOLERANCE_PIXELS (defined as constant 1). Returns the list of bounding box tuples. This is used to generate suggested exclusion regions for validation failures by grouping nearby failed pixels into rectangular regions.

parameters.name: img1_path
parameters.description: Path to the baseline image

parameters.name: img2_path
parameters.description: Path to the validation image

parameters.name: exclusions
parameters.description: List of exclusion region tuples

parameters.name: color_tolerance
parameters.description: Maximum color difference threshold. Defaults to COLOR_TOLERANCE constant (35).

parameters.name: original_exclude_str
parameters.description: Original exclusion string from Excel. If '[]', returns pass immediately.

functions.what_it_does: Performs pixel-by-pixel image comparison with exclusion region filtering and bounding box generation for failures. First checks if either image file does not exist, returning (False, None, [], []) if missing. If original_exclude_str is '[]' (empty exclusion list as string), returns (True, None, [], []) immediately treating it as a pass. Opens both images and converts to RGB mode. Determines the maximum width and height between the two images. Creates two new white background images of the maximum dimensions and pastes the original images at (0, 0), effectively padding smaller images with white. Initializes two lists: failed_pixels for all differing pixels and failed_pixels_outside_exclusions for pixels that differ and are not in exclusion regions. Iterates through every pixel coordinate in the padded images. For each pixel, gets RGB values from both images, calculates Euclidean distance in RGB space using sum of squared differences then square root. If the distance exceeds color_tolerance, adds the pixel to failed_pixels. Additionally checks if the pixel is not in any exclusion region using is_in_exclusion(); if not excluded, adds to failed_pixels_outside_exclusions. After processing all pixels, calls get_bounding_boxes() with failed_pixels_outside_exclusions to generate suggested exclusion regions. If no boxes were generated (all failures are within exclusions or no failures), returns (True, None, [], failed_pixels) indicating pass. Otherwise returns (False, None, boxes, failed_pixels_outside_exclusions) indicating failure with suggested exclusions. This is the core image comparison function that determines validation pass/fail status.

parameters.name: img2_path
parameters.description: Path to the validation image

parameters.name: failed_pixels_outside_exclusions
parameters.description: List of (x, y) coordinates of failed pixels not in exclusions

parameters.name: out_path
parameters.description: Path where the overlay image should be saved

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Alpha transparency value for the red overlay (0-255). Defaults to 120.

functions.what_it_does: Creates a visual diff image by overlaying red semi-transparent pixels on failed areas of the validation image. Opens the validation image at img2_path and converts to RGBA mode to support transparency. Creates a new transparent overlay image of the same size with all pixels set to transparent red (255, 0, 0, 0). Gets direct pixel access to the overlay using load(). Extracts the image dimensions. Iterates through each failed pixel coordinate in failed_pixels_outside_exclusions. For each coordinate, validates it's within image bounds (0 <= x < width and 0 <= y < height). If valid, sets the overlay pixel at that coordinate to semi-transparent red (255, 0, 0, alpha). After processing all failed pixels, composites the overlay onto the validation image using Image.alpha_composite() which blends the semi-transparent red overlay with the original image. Saves the resulting composite image to out_path. Returns True on success. This creates a visual representation of validation failures where failed pixels are highlighted in red, making it easy to see exactly where differences occurred.

functions.method_signature: def compare_gif_files(gif_path_1: str, gif_path_2: str) -> bool

parameters.name: gif_path_1
parameters.description: Path to the first GIF file

parameters.name: gif_path_2
parameters.description: Path to the second GIF file

functions.docstring: Compare two GIF files byte-wise. Returns True if identical.
functions.what_it_does: Performs byte-by-byte comparison of two GIF files to determine if they are identical. First checks if both files exist using os.path.exists(). If either file is missing, logs a warning with both file paths and returns False. Opens both files in binary read mode using context managers. Reads the complete content of both files using read() and compares the byte strings using equality operator. Returns True if the byte content is identical, False otherwise. This is used for validating animated GIF elements by comparing the baseline GIF captured during scraping with the GIF captured during validation.
functions.usage_example: is_same = compare_gif_files('baseline.gif', 'validation.gif')  # Returns True if identical

functions.method_signature: def compare_svg_xml(svg_xml_1: str, svg_xml_2: str) -> bool

parameters.name: svg_xml_1
parameters.description: First SVG XML content as string

parameters.name: svg_xml_2
parameters.description: Second SVG XML content as string

functions.docstring:
Robust SVG comparison: ignores whitespace, attribute order, float precision, and comment nodes.
Handles: attribute sorting, rounds floats, strips comments and normalizes text/tail.
functions.what_it_does: Performs robust semantic comparison of two SVG XML strings by normalizing and comparing their structure rather than raw text. Defines inner function normalize_float() that attempts to round float values to 3 decimal places, handling both single floats and space/comma-separated float lists (common in SVG path data). Defines clean_element() recursive function that normalizes an XML element by: sorting attributes alphabetically, normalizing float values in attributes, stripping whitespace from text and tail, removing comment nodes, recursively cleaning child elements, and sorting children by tag name, attributes, and text for deterministic order. Defines get_root() helper that parses XML string using ElementTree.fromstring(), catching parse errors and logging warnings. Parses both SVG strings to get root elements. If either parse fails, falls back to whitespace-insensitive string comparison using regex to remove all whitespace. If both parse successfully, calls clean_element() on both roots to normalize them. Serializes both normalized trees back to strings using ElementTree.tostring(). Compares the normalized strings and returns True if identical, False otherwise. This handles common SVG variations like attribute order differences, float precision differences, whitespace variations, and comment presence, focusing on semantic equivalence rather than exact text matching.
functions.usage_example: is_same = compare_svg_xml(baseline_svg, validation_svg)  # Returns True if semantically equivalent

functions.method_signature: def validate_context(flow_name, context_name, page, excel_path, output_folder, validation_workbook_path=None, screenshot_only=False, sub_context=None, lang_code=None, animations=False, target_dir=None)

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow

parameters.name: context_name
parameters.description: Name of the context/checkpoint being validated

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: excel_path
parameters.description: Path to the baseline Excel file with scraped data

parameters.name: output_folder
parameters.description: Directory for validation outputs

parameters.name: validation_workbook_path
parameters.description: Optional path for validation results workbook. Auto-generated if None.

parameters.name: screenshot_only
parameters.description: If True, only captures full-page screenshot without element validation.

parameters.name: sub_context
parameters.description: Optional sub-context name for granular checkpoints

parameters.name: lang_code
parameters.description: Language code for filename generation

parameters.name: animations
parameters.description: If True, validates animated SVG and GIF elements

parameters.name: target_dir
parameters.description: Target directory name for organizing outputs

functions.what_it_does: Main validation function that compares live page state against baseline scraped data with comprehensive element, text, screenshot, and animation validation. Creates output directories for validation screenshots, failure screenshots, and animations. Constructs subcontext suffix for filenames. If screenshot_only mode: captures full-page screenshot with retry logic (3 attempts with 2s delay), loads baseline Excel to find matching full-page baseline, performs image comparison with exclusion handling, generates validation report row with pass/fail status and suggested exclusions if failed, creates overlay diff image if failed, writes report to Excel, and returns early. For full validation mode: creates or loads validation workbook, loads baseline Excel and finds the correct sheet, extracts header row, filters baseline data rows matching context and sub_context. If animations flag is True: extracts all animated SVGs from page using extract_animated_svgs(), saves each SVG to validation_animations folder with hash-based filename. Queries all visible elements on the page with text content and valid bounding boxes, storing them in live_elements list. Iterates through each baseline row: handles animation type rows by extracting and comparing GIF or SVG animations, handles fullpage type rows by capturing and comparing full-page screenshots, handles regular element rows by finding matching live elements by text and tag, validating text and href matches, capturing element screenshots, performing image comparison with exclusions, generating suggested exclusion boxes on failure, creating overlay diff images. For each row, constructs a detailed validation result dictionary with context, timestamp, expected/actual values, pass/fail status, screenshot paths, diff images, exclusion regions, and comments. Writes all results to Excel using write_report_excel_context() with automatic highlighting. Logs completion message. This is the core validation engine that orchestrates all validation operations for a single context checkpoint.

Gets the selector for the specified feedback_option from the map, raising ValueError if option not found. Clicks the radio button using page.click() with force=True. If feedback_option is 'Other', fills the feedback text box with 'Other Reason'. If submit parameter is True, clicks the submit feedback button; otherwise clicks the return to account button. This automates the feedback selection step of the cancellation flow.

Gets the selector for the specified section_title, raising ValueError if not found. Locates the retention option container by navigating up two parent levels from the link using xpath. Locates the image, text paragraph (last p element), and button within the container. Verifies that the image, text, and button are all visible using expect().to_be_visible(). Gets today's date and formats as 'MMM DD, YYYY'. Extracts the last day date from step 1 text using regex to find date pattern. Parses the last day date and calculates final bill date as last_day + 1 day. Verifies step 1 (cancellation submitted) with today's date in title. Verifies step 2 (last day to print) with last_day date in title. For step 3 (cartridges deactivated), uses regex pattern to match a date range format allowing for the final bill date to vary by ±1 day to handle timezone and processing variations. Verifies step 4 and step 5 icons and content are visible. This validates the timeline shows correct dates with appropriate tolerance for date calculation variations.

Verifies the link is visible. Then locates the transfer subscription retention option text and link using XPath. Verifies the text is visible and contains 'Did you get a new printer?'. If email is provided, calls HPIDHelper.sign_in() to perform authentication. Creates DashboardSideMenuPage instance and gets the visible Instant Ink menu link element. Calls accept_banner_and_access_overview_page() to handle privacy banner. Defines a list of element lambda functions for modals that may appear on first access: special savings modal close, flip modal not now button, continue setting preferences, accept all preferences, no paper offer modal, skip tour, and another special savings modal close. If stack is 'pie' or 'stage', appends an additional special savings modal close to the list. Loops up to 6 times attempting to dismiss modals. In each iteration, creates a copy of the remaining elements list and iterates through each element function. For each element, tries to get the element, check if it's visible, and click it if visible. If successful, removes the element function from the list and logs which element was clicked. Catches and ignores exceptions for elements that are not present. Sleeps 2 seconds between iterations. This ensures a clean dashboard state by dismissing all first-time user experience modals.

functions.what_it_does: Adds credit card billing information using the PGS (Payment Gateway Service) payment provider with 3DS authentication support. Creates ConfirmationPage and ShippingBillingPage instances. Gets payment data for the specified card_type using common.get_payment_method_data(). Logs the card type being used. If country code is IT (Italy), fills the tax_id_input with a test tax ID. Checks if the billing continue button is enabled, then clicks it. Waits for the PGS iframe to be visible with 120s timeout. Locates the iframe and gets a frame locator. Within the iframe, fills the card number input with the credit card number from payment data. Selects the expiration month and year from dropdowns. Types the CVV into the CVV input. Checks if a billing next button exists in the iframe; if so, clicks it. Otherwise, clicks the SCA save button to proceed to 3DS authentication. Attempts to locate the 3DS1 redirect iframe with 5s timeout; if found, uses that frame and sets submit selector to the 3DS1 submit button. If not found, waits for the 2FA iframe and uses that with the ACS submit selector. Within the challenge frame, waits for the authentication result dropdown to be visible with 10s timeout. Selects the authentication result from payment data (defaults to 'AUTHENTICATED'). Waits for the submit button to be visible and clicks it to complete authentication. Waits for the update shipping billing message to appear with 60s timeout, confirming successful billing addition. This handles the complete PGS payment flow including Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) / 3D Secure.

Within the 2CO iframe, waits for and clicks the 'continue to billing' button with 60s timeout. Waits for and clicks the 'continue to payment' button with 60s timeout. Waits for the card name field to be attached with 3s timeout. Checks if the card_name_2co field is visible; if so, fills it with 'John Doe'. If not visible, waits for first_name_2co field, types 'John', presses Tab, waits for last_name_2co field, and types 'Doe'. Presses Tab to move to card number field. Types the credit card number from payment data into card_number_2co. Presses Tab and types the expiration date in MM/YY format into exp_date_2co. Presses Tab and types the CVV into cvv_2co. Waits for the place order button to be visible with 60s timeout and clicks it. Waits for the update shipping billing message to appear with 60s timeout, confirming successful billing addition. Logs success message. This handles the complete 2Checkout payment flow with its multi-step iframe navigation.

functions.method_signature: def sees_email_with_subject(tenant_email: str, subject: str, timeout: int = 240, poll_interval: int = 10)

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: The email address to check for received emails

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The subject line to search for in received emails

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for email to arrive. Defaults to 240 seconds (4 minutes)

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between polling attempts. Defaults to 10 seconds

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that an email with a specific subject has been received by the specified email address within the timeout period. This is a wrapper method that internally calls email_by_subject to perform the actual email search. It polls the Gmail API at regular intervals (poll_interval) until either the email is found or the timeout is reached. If the email is not found within the timeout, it raises a RuntimeError. This method is typically used in test assertions to verify that expected notification emails were sent.
functions.usage_example: EmailHelper.sees_email_with_subject('testuser@example.com', 'Welcome to HP Instant Ink', timeout=300, poll_interval=15)

functions.method_signature: def email_by_subject(tenant_email: str, subject: str, timeout: int = 240, poll_interval: int = 10)

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: The email address to search for received emails

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The subject line to search for in emails

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for email. Defaults to 240 seconds

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Seconds between polling attempts. Defaults to 10 seconds

functions.what_it_does: Searches for and retrieves an email message with a specific subject sent to the specified email address. First authenticates with Gmail API using common.authenticate_gmail(). Calculates the end time by adding timeout seconds to current time. Constructs a Gmail API query string with the recipient email and subject. Enters a polling loop that continues until end_time is reached. In each iteration, calls Gmail API's messages().list() with the query to search for matching emails. If messages are found, retrieves the first message ID and fetches the full message details using messages().get(). Returns the complete message object if found. If any exception occurs during an iteration, silently continues to next iteration. Sleeps for poll_interval seconds between attempts. If no email is found within timeout period, raises RuntimeError with descriptive message including the subject and email address.

functions.method_signature: def extract_email_details(tenant_email: str, subject: str, timeout: int = 240, poll_interval: int = 10)

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address to search for received emails

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject line to search for

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds. Defaults to 240

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Polling interval in seconds. Defaults to 10

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves and extracts detailed information from an email message including subject, pre-header text, and HTML body content. First calls email_by_subject to retrieve the message object. Extracts the headers array from the message payload. Searches headers for 'subject' header and extracts its value, defaulting to empty string if not found. Searches headers for 'x-preheader-text' header and extracts its value. Defines an inner recursive function find_html_part that traverses the message payload structure to locate the HTML body part. The function checks if current payload mimeType is 'text/html' and returns the body data if found. If not found, recursively searches through all parts in the payload. Calls find_html_part to get the HTML data. If HTML data exists, decodes it from base64 URL-safe encoding to UTF-8 string. Returns a dictionary containing three keys: 'subject' with the email subject, 'pre_header' with the pre-header text, and 'html_body' with the decoded HTML content. This method is useful for validating email content in tests.
functions.usage_example:
details = EmailHelper.extract_email_details('test@example.com', 'Welcome Email')
subject = details['subject']
html = details['html_body']

functions.what_it_does: Starts enrollment and signs in the user with retry logic to handle timing issues. Calculates end_time by adding timeout to current time. Enters a while loop that continues until end_time is reached. In each iteration, wraps operations in try-except to catch and ignore exceptions. Calls start_enrollment to navigate to enrollment URL and accept privacy banner. Logs enrollment start with tenant email. Checks if current URL contains 'printer-selection' - if yes, breaks out of loop as sign-in succeeded. If not on printer selection page, calls HPIDHelper.complete_sign_in with tenant email and default password to perform HPID authentication. Logs successful sign-in. If any exception occurs, silently continues to next iteration. After the try-except block, checks again if URL contains 'printer-selection' and breaks if true. If still not on printer selection after exception, sleeps for 10 seconds before next retry. This retry pattern handles intermittent failures in enrollment start or sign-in process.

functions.method_signature: def add_shipping(page, index=0, company_name=None, additional_check=False, callback=None)

parameters.name: page

functions.what_it_does: Adds shipping address information during enrollment with optional validation. Creates ConfirmationPage instance. Retrieves address data from GlobalState.address_payload at specified index, handling both list and single object. Extracts state name from address, checking both 'fullState' and language-specific keys. Attempts to click add_shipping_button if visible with 60 second timeout, wrapped in try-except to handle FLIP flow where button may not exist. Fills company_name_input if company_name parameter provided. Fills street1_input and street2_input with address data. Checks if country is in countries_without_city_mandate list - if not, fills city_input. Checks if country is in countries_without_states_mandate list - if not and state_name exists, clicks state_dropdown, gets dropdown element ID, constructs selector for dropdown options, waits for state option matching state_name to be visible, and clicks it. Checks if country is in countries_without_zip_mandate list - if not, fills zip_code_input. Fills phone_number_input with phone number. If additional_check is True, validates receive_update_msg is visible, checks if receive_update_checkbox is checked (should be unchecked by default, raises exception if checked), validates close_shipping_modal_button is visible, validates cancel_button is visible, validates save_button is visible. If any validation fails, logs error and calls callback with error screenshot. Clicks save_button to save shipping address. Attempts to handle suggested address modal by waiting for it with 5 second timeout and clicking ship_to_address_button if modal appears and button is enabled. If modal doesn't appear or button not available, logs error and calls callback. If additional_check is True, validates saved address by waiting for street1, street2, and city+state+zip text to be visible on page with 10 second timeout. If validation fails, logs error and calls callback.

functions.method_signature: def validate_order_received(page, status="statusNew", shipping="standard", trigger_id=None, order_link=None)

parameters.name: page

parameters.name: order_link
parameters.description: Direct URL to order. If provided, navigates directly to this URL

functions.what_it_does: Validates that an order has been received with correct status and shipping information. If trigger_id is provided, calls access() to authenticate and access_order_by_trigger_id() to navigate to the order. If order_link is provided instead, calls access() and then navigates directly to the order_link URL. Calls validate_shipping_fields() to verify the status and shipping values match expected values. Returns the current page URL as the order_link for future reference.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that order shipping fields match expected values. Calls common.find_field_text_by_header() to get the 'Status from fulfiller' field value and asserts it equals the expected status parameter, providing detailed error message if mismatch. Calls common.find_field_text_by_header() to get the 'Overall part status' field value and asserts it equals the expected status parameter, providing detailed error message if mismatch. Calls common.find_field_text_by_header() to get the 'Shipping option' field value and asserts it equals the expected shipping parameter, providing detailed error message if mismatch.

functions.what_it_does: Updates the error code field on an order in Rails Admin for testing error scenarios. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to click the Edit menu option. Locates the order_error_code input field. Fills the field with the provided error_code value. Logs info message with the error code being set. Locates the save submit button. Scrolls the save button into view if needed. Clicks the save button to submit the form. Logs info message confirming the order was updated with the error code.

Waits for network idle state and then load state to ensure page is fully loaded. Clicks the home nav link to navigate to the main Rails Admin page.

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific order in the Fulfillment Simulator Rails Admin by searching for its trigger ID. Calls RABaseHelper.access_page() to navigate to the Orders section. Types the trigger_id into the search query input field. Clicks the submit button to execute the search. Constructs CSS selector for the Show link in the order row options. Clicks the Show link to open the order details. Calls RABaseHelper.wait_page_to_load() to wait for the Order details page to fully load.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: status

parameters.name: tracking_number
parameters.description: Tracking number to assign. Defaults to '123456789'

functions.what_it_does: Updates an order's status and tracking information in the Fulfillment Simulator to simulate shipping. Waits for and clicks the 'Update order' link in the nav tabs. Selects the provided status value in the order_status dropdown. Selects the same status value in the part status dropdown (using selector that starts with 'part_'). Sets vendor to 'DHL' by filling the shipping_vendor field. Fills the tracking_number field with the provided tracking number. Clicks the Update submit button to save changes. Waits for a success message locator containing 'Call to Fulfillment Service returned status 201' to become visible. Asserts that the success message is visible, raising error if status 201 confirmation not found.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: trigger_id
parameters.description: Trigger ID of the order to process

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status to set. Defaults to 'statusShipped'

parameters.name: tracking_number
parameters.description: Tracking number. Defaults to '123456789'

functions.what_it_does: Orchestrates the complete order processing workflow in the Fulfillment Simulator including updating order status and triggering background jobs. Calls access() to navigate to the Fulfillment Simulator Rails Admin. Calls access_order_by_trigger_id() to navigate to the specific order. Calls update_order() with the provided status and tracking_number to simulate shipping. Calls RABaseHelper.complete_jobs() targeting the fulfillment service URL to process the CheckSqsOrderStatusUpdateJob background job. Calls RABaseHelper.complete_jobs() again targeting the fulfillment service URL to process the GeminiCallbackDispatcherJob background job. This ensures the order status update is propagated through the system.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Accesses and authenticates to the Gemini Rails Admin interface. Navigates to the Instant Ink URL concatenated with the Gemini Rails Admin login URL from common config with 120 second timeout. Waits for network idle and load states to ensure page is fully loaded. Checks if an alert warning message is visible indicating already signed in. If alert is visible, logs info message that already signed in to Gemini Rails Admin and returns early without attempting login. If not already signed in, types the rails_admin_user from common config into the admin email field. Types the rails_admin_password from common config into the admin password field. Clicks the admin submit button to log in.

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address of the tenant to search for

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific tenant's page in Gemini Rails Admin by searching for their email address. Navigates to the Instant Ink URL concatenated with the Gemini fetch tenant URL from common config with 120 second timeout. Calls common.retry_operation() to retry the email filling operation with a lambda that calls fill_and_assert_email helper function passing page and tenant_email. This retry pattern handles timing issues with the email input field. Clicks the submit button to execute the tenant search. Waits for a table link to appear with 180 second timeout and clicks the first result. Calls RABaseHelper.wait_page_to_load() to wait for the Tenant details page to fully load. Calls common.last_number_from_url() to extract and return the tenant ID from the URL.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Transitions a subscription to 'subscribed' state if not already in that state. Checks if the current State field value is not 'subscribed' using RABaseHelper.get_field_text_by_title(). If state is not subscribed, calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to click the Edit menu option. Locates the subscription_state_event dropdown. Wraps the dropdown selection in try-except to handle cases where the event option may not be available. Attempts to select the 'event_hise_pens_inserted' option which triggers the transition to subscribed state. If an exception occurs during selection, logs the error. Locates the save button with class 'btn btn-primary'. Scrolls the save button into view if needed. Clicks the save button to submit the state change. Calls RABaseHelper.wait_page_to_load() to wait for the Subscription page to reload. Asserts that the State field now equals 'subscribed' using RABaseHelper.get_field_text_by_title().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: kit_type
parameters.description: Type of kit to ship - 'welcome' or 'replenishment'. Defaults to 'welcome'

parameters.name: colors
parameters.description: List of color codes to ship (e.g., ['K', 'CMY']). If None, no colors are selected

functions.what_it_does: Ships ink cartridges to a subscriber via Gemini Rails Admin and returns the trigger ID. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to click the 'Ship it' menu option. Asserts that kit_type is either 'welcome' or 'replenishment', raising error if invalid. Sets the checkbox for the specified kit_type to checked using set_checked(True). If colors parameter is provided, iterates through each color in the list. For each color, asserts it is one of the valid values: 'K', 'CMY', 'C', 'M', 'Y'. Sets the checkbox for that color supply to checked using lowercase color code (e.g., 'k_supply'). Clicks the 'Ship Ink Now' submit button. Reads the alert message text from the page. Uses regex to search for 'trigger id (\w+)' pattern in the alert text. Extracts the trigger ID from the regex match if found, otherwise sets to empty string. Returns the trigger ID string.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Field title/label to verify

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Expected value for the field

parameters.name: retry
parameters.description: Whether to retry verification with page reloads. Defaults to True

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a Rails Admin field has the expected value with optional retry logic. Defines an inner operation function that gets the field text using RABaseHelper.get_field_text_by_title() for the specified title. Asserts the field text equals the expected value, providing detailed error message if mismatch. Logs info message confirming the field has the expected value. Returns True on success. Defines an inner on_retry function that logs a retry message and reloads the page, waiting for load state. Sets max_attempts to 12 if retry is True, otherwise 1 (no retries). Calls common.retry_operation() with the operation function, operation name, max_attempts, 10 second delay between attempts, and the on_retry callback if retry is enabled. This pattern allows verification to wait up to 2 minutes (12 attempts * 10 seconds) for async updates to propagate in Rails Admin.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: event_shift
parameters.description: Number of days to shift events (as string)

parameters.name: force_billing
parameters.description: Whether to create billing cycle after rollback. Defaults to True

functions.what_it_does: Shifts subscription events forward in time by a specified number of days, optionally forcing billing cycle creation. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to click the Edit menu option. Fills the subscription_rollback field with the event_shift value (number of days). Waits for the subscription_create_billing_cycle_after_rollback checkbox to be present. If force_billing is True, clicks the checkbox to enable billing cycle creation. Waits to verify the checkbox is checked. Waits for the save button with class 'btn btn-primary' to be present with 120 second timeout and clicks it. Waits for a success alert to appear with 10 second timeout. Asserts the success alert is visible, confirming the event shift was applied. This method is commonly used to simulate time passing in tests without waiting for real time.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Submits a charge for a billing cycle in Gemini Rails Admin. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to click the 'Submit Charge' menu option. Waits for the warning button with class 'btn btn-warning' to be present with 120 second timeout and clicks it to confirm the charge submission. Waits for a success alert to appear with 150 second timeout. Asserts that the success alert text contains 'Billing cycle has been charged', confirming the charge was processed successfully.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: refund_option
parameters.description: Type of refund to process (e.g., 'Instant Ink Overage Blocks')

parameters.name: blocks
parameters.description: Number of overage blocks to refund if refund_option is 'Instant Ink Overage Blocks'

functions.what_it_does: Processes a refund in Gemini Rails Admin with retry logic to handle timing issues. Enters a for loop that attempts the refund up to 60 times. Wraps each attempt in try-except. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to click 'Refund Payment' menu option. Clicks the refund-option dropdown. Selects the specified refund_option from the dropdown. If refund_option is 'Instant Ink Overage Blocks', fills the overage_amount field with the blocks value, presses NumpadEnter, and clicks the submit button for overage options. Otherwise, clicks the generic Submit button. Clicks the warning confirmation button with class 'btn btn-warning one-click-only'. Waits for success alert to appear with 10 second timeout. Gets the success message text. Asserts the success alert is visible. Logs info message with the success message and returns. If any exception occurs during an attempt and it's not the last attempt (59), logs warning with attempt number and retries after 2 second sleep and page reload. If all 60 attempts fail, logs error and re-raises the exception.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the country selector popup may appear

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to dismiss the country selector popup that may appear on the HPID page. Creates an HPIDPage instance with the provided page. Then tries to click the first country selector button using hpid_page.country_selector_button.first.click(). If the button is not found or any exception occurs during the click, the exception is caught and silently passed, allowing the flow to continue. This is a defensive method used to handle optional popups that may or may not appear.

functions.what_it_does: Performs the core HPID sign-in flow by entering credentials and submitting the form. Creates an HPIDPage instance. First fills the username field with the provided email using hpid_page.username.fill(email). Then clicks the username form submit button using hpid_page.user_name_form_submit.click() to proceed to the password screen. Next fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Finally clicks the sign-in button using hpid_page.sign_in.click() to complete the authentication. This is the core sign-in logic without any pre-steps like country dismissal.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HPID authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for HPID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HPID sign-in flow including all pre-steps and the actual sign-in. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup that may appear. Then creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the sign-in option button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.sign_in_option(timeout=20000) and clicks it to navigate to the sign-in form. Finally calls HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) to perform the actual credential entry and form submission. This is the complete sign-in flow used in test scenarios.

parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new HPID account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new HPID account

functions.what_it_does: Performs the HPID sign-up flow by filling the registration form with user details. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Fills the first name field with the hardcoded value 'InstantInk' using hpid_page.firstName.fill('InstantInk'). Fills the last name field with the hardcoded value 'User' using hpid_page.lastName.fill('User'). Fills the email field with the provided email using hpid_page.email.fill(email). Fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Clicks the market checkbox using hpid_page.market.click() to accept terms. Finally clicks the create account button using hpid_page.create_account_button.click() to submit the registration form. This creates a new HPID account but does not handle verification code confirmation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address where the verification code was sent

functions.what_it_does: Handles the HPID account verification code confirmation process with retry logic. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the submit code button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.submit_code(timeout=20000). Sets up retry logic with 3 attempts and 10-second wait time between attempts. For each attempt, waits for the specified wait time using page.wait_for_timeout(wait_time * 1000). Fetches the verification code from the email using common.fetch_verification_code(email). If a verification code is retrieved, fills the code field using hpid_page.code.fill(verification_code) and clicks the submit code button using hpid_page.submit_code.click(). Logs a success message with the email, stack, country code, and language code from GlobalState. Breaks out of the retry loop on success. If an exception occurs, logs a warning with the attempt number and continues to the next retry. If all retries fail and no verification code was obtained, logs an error message and returns. Finally waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load').

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for the new tenant account to be created

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HPID sign-up flow including all steps from country dismissal to account verification. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the create account button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.create_account_button_data(timeout=20000) and clicks it to navigate to the sign-up form. Calls HPIDHelper.sign_up(page, tenant_email, common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD) to fill and submit the registration form using the provided tenant email and the default password from the common module. Finally calls HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code(page, tenant_email) to handle the verification code confirmation process. This is the complete sign-up flow used in test scenarios to create and verify new HPID accounts.

After navigation, waits for the page load state to be 'load' using page.wait_for_load_state('load'). This is the basic navigation method without any additional handling of popups or banners.

Creates a PrivacyBannerPage instance and calls privacy_banner_page.accept_privacy_banner() to accept the privacy banner if it appears. Creates a LandingPage instance. Then attempts to close any promotional banner by clicking landing_page.close_banner.click(). If the close banner button is not found or cannot be clicked, catches the exception and prints an error message but continues execution. This is the recommended method for accessing the landing page in tests as it handles common UI elements.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RAF) banner is visible on the landing page and contains the expected text about free months. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the RAF banner element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_banner). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible with a 30-second timeout. Extracts the banner text content using raf_banner.text_content(). Constructs the expected text string as 'You have {expected_months} free month of HP Instant Ink'. Asserts that the expected text is present in the banner text, raising an assertion error with details if not found. Logs a success message with the expected text using framework_logger.info(). Returns True on successful verification.

Locates the RAF footnote element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_footnote). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the footnote is visible with a 30-second timeout. This is a simple visibility check without text content validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: section_selector
parameters.description: CSS selector string for the section element to check

functions.what_it_does: Verifies whether a page section is currently visible within the browser viewport. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript in the browser context. The JavaScript code takes the section_selector as a parameter, uses document.querySelector() to find the element, returns false if the element is not found, gets the element's bounding rectangle using getBoundingClientRect(), and checks if the element is within the viewport by verifying that rect.top is greater than or equal to 0 and rect.bottom is less than or equal to the window inner height or document client height. Returns the boolean result indicating whether the section is in the viewport. This is used to verify that clicking navigation links properly scrolls sections into view.

functions.what_it_does: Tests the complete consumer landing page OOBE (Out-of-Box Experience) flow for desktop viewport. Navigates to the landing page URL, accepts privacy banner if present, tests the Sign Up Now button functionality by clicking it and verifying HPID page appears, returns to landing page, then systematically tests each navigation tab (How It Works, Instant Paper Plan, Plans, FAQs) by clicking each tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the corresponding section is visible, checking if the section is in viewport, logging results, and optionally calling the callback function for screenshots. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors. This is a comprehensive OOBE flow test covering all major landing page sections and navigation.

Navigates to the landing page URL, accepts privacy banner if present, clicks the mobile menu button, tests the mobile Sign Up button functionality by clicking it and verifying HPID page appears, returns to landing page, then systematically tests each mobile navigation tab by opening the mobile menu, clicking the tab (How It Works, Instant Paper Plan, Plans, FAQs), waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the corresponding section is visible, checking if the section is in viewport, logging results, and optionally calling the callback function for screenshots. Uses mobile-specific locators like mobe_menu_button, mobe_sign_up_button, mobe_how_it_works_tab, etc. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors. This is a comprehensive MOBE flow test covering all major landing page sections and mobile navigation.

Creates a LandingPage instance. Tests the following links in sequence: HP.com link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/home.html'), Privacy Statement link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/privacy.html'), HP Connected Terms of Use link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hpsmart.com/us/en/tou'), Cookie Settings link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/use-of-cookies.html'), Your Privacy Choices link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/your-privacy-choices.html'), and Instant Ink Terms of Service link (verifies URL contains 'https://instantink-stage1.hpconnectedstage.com/us/en/terms'). For each link, waits for the element to be visible, scrolls it into view if needed, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab, clicks the link, waits for the new page to load, uses expect().to_have_url() with regex to verify the URL, logs success, optionally calls callback for screenshot, and closes the new tab. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors.

functions.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ links on the landing page by systematically expanding FAQ sections, clicking questions, and verifying that links within answers open correctly. Creates a LandingPage instance. Stores the original URL. Scrolls the FAQs section into view and verifies it is visible. Defines a list of FAQ sections to test: General, Monthly plans, Yearly plans, and Pay As You Print plans, each with their section name and section ID. For each FAQ section, logs the section name, navigates back to the original URL if needed, locates and clicks the section tab using the section ID, waits 500ms, checks if a 'Show more' button exists and clicks it if present, locates all question buttons within the section, iterates through each question by index, clicks the question, waits 300ms, gets the aria-controls attribute to find the answer element, verifies the answer is visible, locates all links within the answer, iterates through each link by index, gets the link href, skips mailto and tel links, clicks the link, waits 2 seconds, gets all pages in the context, brings the last page to front, waits for network idle, attempts to verify either the HP branding image is visible or the URL contains '/terms', logs success, closes the new page, navigates back to the original URL, re-expands the section and question to continue testing remaining links. Handles exceptions gracefully by logging warnings and continuing. Finally ensures the page returns to the original URL. This is a comprehensive FAQ validation that tests all sections, questions, and links.

Gets the languages array and country code for the specified country from the locale map. Takes the first language from the languages array. Constructs the locale code as '{language}-{short_code}' where short_code is the lowercase country code. Locates and clicks the country selector element using the data-testid attribute with the locale code using page.locator(f'[data-testid="{locale_code}-selector"]').click(). This allows selecting different countries for testing localized landing pages.

functions.what_it_does: Validates the emergency banner on the landing page for different countries by checking the background color and icon. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the emergency banner element and gets its parent using landing_page.emergency_banner.locator('..'). Gets the font and background color details using common.get_font_details(banner_element). Extracts the detected background color. Logs the detected color. Defines a country_configs dictionary mapping country names to their expected banner configurations including name, expected_color (RGB string), and expected_icon_path (SVG path string or None). Gets the configuration for the target country from the dictionary. Logs the country and banner name being tested. Asserts that the detected color matches the expected color, raising an assertion error with details if not. Logs color validation success. If the config has an expected_icon_path (for countries like Finland, Portugal, Australia, Norway, Poland, Estonia), locates the SVG path element within the banner, gets its 'd' attribute, and asserts it matches the expected path. Logs SVG path validation success. If the config has no expected_icon_path (for Bulgaria), locates the orange banner icon element using the class 'orangeBannerIcon', asserts it exists, and logs success. Finally logs overall validation success. This method supports validating different banner types: Red Minus Banner (Finland), Orange Alert Banner (Bulgaria), Green Done Banner (Portugal), Blue Done Banner (Australia), White Done Banner (Norway), Grey Done Banner (Poland), and Black Done Banner (Estonia).

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Either a locator object or a string selector

functions.docstring: Helper method to get text from a page element
functions.what_it_does: Helper method to extract text from a page element given either a locator object or a string selector. Checks if the selector has an 'inner_text' attribute using hasattr(). If it does, calls selector.inner_text() directly and returns the result, treating it as a locator object. If it does not, treats it as a string selector, creates a locator using page.locator(selector), gets the first element using .first, calls inner_text() on it, and returns the result. This provides a flexible way to get text from elements regardless of how they are specified.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Currency value to normalize - can be string, number, or None

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values for consistent comparison by removing currency symbols and formatting. If value is falsy (None, empty string, etc.), returns '0.00'. Converts value to string, strips whitespace, replaces commas with periods for decimal normalization, removes dollar signs, euro signs, and pound signs, removes non-breaking spaces and regular spaces. If the normalized value is a dash, em dash, 'N/A', or 'n/a', returns '0.00'. Wraps the conversion in a try-except block. Attempts to convert the normalized string to a float. If successful, formats it as a string with 2 decimal places and returns it. If conversion fails, returns the normalized string as-is. This ensures currency values can be reliably compared regardless of formatting or currency symbol.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selector or locator for the currency field element

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected currency value - can be string, int (in cents), or float

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, checks if expected value is contained in actual text. If False, requires exact match. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a currency field contains the expected value with support for partial or exact matching. Gets the actual text from the selector using PrintHistoryHelper.get_text_from_selector(). If expected is an integer, divides it by 100 to convert from cents to dollars. Normalizes the expected value using PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value(). If partial_match is True, replaces commas with periods in the actual text and asserts that the expected normalized value is contained in the actual text, raising an assertion error with details if not. If partial_match is False, normalizes the actual text using normalize_currency_value() and asserts that the normalized actual value exactly equals the normalized expected value, raising an assertion error with details if not. This provides flexible currency validation for both exact and partial matching scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expected_values
parameters.description: Dictionary containing expected values for billing fields. Keys can include: total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, total.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies all billing section fields against expected values from a dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each possible field in the expected_values dictionary, performs validation. If 'total_pages_printed' is present, extracts numbers from the total_pages_printed element text, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'pages' is present, extracts numbers from the plan_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'rollover' is present and not a dash, extracts numbers from the rollover_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'additional' is present and not a dash, extracts numbers from the additional_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'plan_price' is present, validates the plan_price_text field using validate_currency_field with partial_match=True. If 'overage_price' is present, validates the overage_description_text field with partial_match=True. If 'previous' is present, validates the previous_billing_text field with exact match. If 'charges' is present, validates the current_billing_text field with exact match. If 'tax' is present and not a dash, attempts to validate the tax-amount field with exact match, catching exceptions and logging warnings if the element is not found. If 'total' is present, validates the current_total_text field with exact match. This comprehensive method validates all billing section details in a single call.

parameters.name: page
