parameters.name: expected_data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries, each containing 'billing_cycle' key (1-based index) and expected values for that cycle

functions.what_it_does: Verifies billing data for multiple billing cycles by iterating through expected data and selecting each cycle. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each row in the expected_data list, extracts the billing_cycle value and converts it to a 0-based index by subtracting 1. Logs which billing cycle is being validated. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs that the dropdown was opened. Locates all billing cycle options using page.locator(print_history.elements.billing_cycle_options). Clicks the option at the calculated index using .nth(billing_cycle_index).click(). Logs which option was selected. Waits 1 second using page.wait_for_timeout(1000) for the table to update. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section(page, row) to validate all fields for that cycle. This allows validating multiple billing cycles in a single test by providing a list of expected data dictionaries.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ questions and links on the print and payment history page by clicking questions, verifying answers, and testing navigation links. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. Clicks faq_question_1 and verifies faq_answer_1 is visible. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new tab when clicking faq_terms_of_service_link. Verifies the popup URL contains '/terms' using expect().to_have_url() with regex. Closes the popup. Clicks faq_question_2 and verifies faq_answer_2 is visible. Clicks faq_overview_link and verifies the overview page status_card_title is visible. Navigates back to print history using side_menu.print_history_menu_link.click(). Clicks faq_question_2 again, verifies the answer, clicks update_plan_link.first, and verifies the update plan page_title is visible. Navigates back to print history. Clicks faq_question_3 and verifies faq_answer_3 is visible. Clicks faq_question_4, verifies faq_answer_4 is visible, clicks update_plan_link.last, and verifies the update plan page_title is visible. Navigates back to print history. This comprehensive method tests all FAQ interactions and navigation flows.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Validates the print history page for printers without pens by checking which elements are visible and which are not. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify print_history_section is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify page_title is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify print_history_card_title is NOT visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify how_is_calculated_link is NOT visible. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify total_printed_pages is NOT visible. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify plan_pages_bar is NOT visible. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify plan_details_card is NOT visible. This validates that the no-pens print history page shows only basic elements and hides detailed billing information.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the download all invoices button with retry logic and verifies the invoices page opens. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Implements a retry loop with 60 attempts. For each attempt, wraps operations in a try-except block. Clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify download_all_invoices_button is visible with a 5-second timeout. If successful, breaks out of the loop. If an exception occurs and it is not the last attempt (59), logs a retry message with the attempt number, waits 60 seconds using time.sleep(60), reloads the page using page.reload(), and waits for print_history_card_title to be visible with a 90-second timeout. If all 60 attempts fail, raises an Exception. After successful visibility, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when clicking the button. Clicks the download_all_invoices_button. Gets the new tab. Asserts that 'invoices' is in the new tab URL, raising an assertion error with details if not. This method includes robust retry logic for handling delayed invoice availability.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: notifications_text
parameters.description: Expected notification text to find in the print history table

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text appears in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden using is_hidden(). If hidden, clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table is visible with a 30-second timeout. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for the table to fully load. Gets the inner text of the print_history_table. Asserts that the notifications_text parameter is contained in the table content, raising an assertion error with details including the actual table content if not found. This validates that expected notifications are displayed in the print history.

functions.method_signature:
@staticmethod
    def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price value as a string

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan page count as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the print history page shows the expected price and page count. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Gets the text content of the plan_price_text element. Extracts all numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Asserts that the first extracted number matches the expected plan_value parameter, raising an assertion error with details if not. Asserts that the second extracted number matches the expected plan_pages parameter, raising an assertion error with details if not. This validates both the plan price and page count in a single call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: total_value
parameters.description: Expected current total value as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total value on the print history page matches the expected value. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Gets the text content of the first current_total_text element using .first.text_content(). Extracts all numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Asserts that the first extracted number matches the expected total_value parameter, raising an assertion error with details if not. This validates the current billing total displayed on the page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: page_title
parameters.description: The title text that should appear in the h1 heading to confirm page load

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds before throwing timeout error. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for a specific page to fully load by checking for the presence of an h1 heading element containing the text 'Details for {page_title}'. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method with a dynamic selector constructed using an f-string that combines the page_title parameter into the selector pattern 'h1:has-text("Details for {page_title}")'. The method will wait up to the specified timeout duration (default 60 seconds) for the element to appear. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will throw a TimeoutError. This is commonly used after navigation to ensure the target page has fully loaded before proceeding with further interactions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: base_url
parameters.description: The base URL of the application to which the Resque path will be appended

functions.what_it_does: Navigates the browser to the Resque scheduler page by constructing the full URL from the provided base_url and appending the path '/resque/schedule'. Uses Playwright's page.goto() method to perform the navigation. Resque is a background job processing system, and this method provides access to its web interface where scheduled jobs can be viewed and managed. This is typically used as a setup step before running or monitoring background jobs in test scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: base_url
parameters.description: The base URL of the application

parameters.name: job_name
parameters.description: The exact name of the Resque job to be executed

functions.what_it_does: Executes a specific Resque background job by name through the Resque web interface. First constructs a row selector to find the table row containing the exact job name using the pattern 'tr:has(td:text-is("{job_name}"))'. Then constructs a button selector to find the 'Queue now' button within that row. Waits for the button to be visible and clicks it to queue the job for immediate execution. After clicking, waits for the Queues page heading 'h1.wi:has-text("Queues")' to appear confirming the action. Then navigates to the Resque working page at '/resque/working' to monitor job execution. Finally calls RABaseHelper.wait_for_job_to_disappear() to wait until the job completes and disappears from the working jobs list. This provides a complete flow for triggering and waiting for background job completion.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: base_url
parameters.description: The base URL of the application

parameters.name: jobs
parameters.description: List of job names to be executed sequentially. Defaults to empty list.

functions.what_it_does: Executes multiple Resque background jobs sequentially by iterating through the provided jobs list. For each job in the list, it first calls RABaseHelper.access_resque() to navigate to the Resque scheduler page, then calls RABaseHelper.run_job() to execute that specific job and wait for its completion. This method provides a convenient way to run a series of background jobs in order, ensuring each job completes before starting the next one. Commonly used in test setup or teardown to process multiple background tasks that need to complete before test execution can proceed.

functions.method_signature: def wait_for_job_to_disappear(page, job_name: str, timeout: int = 60)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: job_name
parameters.description: The name of the job to monitor for completion

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds before raising RuntimeError. Defaults to 60 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Monitors the Resque working jobs table and waits for a specific job to complete and disappear from the list. Uses a polling loop that runs until the timeout is reached. In each iteration, it waits for the 'table.workers' element to appear with a 5-second timeout. Then locates all table rows except the header using 'table.workers tbody tr:not(:first-child)'. Iterates through each row to check if the queue cell contains the job_name text. If the job is found, sets job_found flag to True and breaks the inner loop. If job_found is False (job not in table), the method returns successfully indicating the job has completed. If the job is still present, waits 2 seconds, reloads the page, and waits for network idle state before checking again. If any exception occurs during checking (like element not found), returns successfully assuming the job completed. If the job is still present after the timeout duration, raises a RuntimeError with a descriptive message. This provides robust job completion monitoring with automatic page refresh and error handling.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: page_name
parameters.description: The exact text of the navigation link to click

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking a navigation link with the provided page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the page_name parameter. The XPath pattern is '//a[@class="nav-link" and normalize-space(text())="{page_name}"]'. The normalize-space function removes leading and trailing whitespace from the link text for more reliable matching. Once the link is located, clicks it to navigate to the target page. This method is commonly used to navigate through the application's main navigation menu.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The text of the menu item to click

functions.what_it_does: Clicks a menu item by locating an element with a class attribute starting with 'nav-item' that contains the specified menu text. Uses the CSS selector pattern '[class^="nav-item"]:has-text("{menu}")' to find the menu element. Waits for the element to be visible with a very long timeout of 120000ms (2 minutes) before clicking it. The extended timeout suggests this is used for menus that may take time to load or become interactive. After the element is found and visible, performs a click action to activate the menu item.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text to search for in card headers

functions.what_it_does: Locates and returns a card body element by searching for a card with a specific title in its header. Constructs a combined CSS selector that matches a div.card element containing either an h5.card-header with exact text matching the title or an h5.card-header with an anchor child whose text matches the title. The selector pattern is 'div.card:has(h5.card-header:text-is("{title}"), h5.card-header a:text-is("{title}"))'. First attempts to locate the card using page.locator(). If the card count is 0, waits up to 10 seconds for the card to appear. If still not found after waiting, raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating the field with that title was not found. If the card is found, returns a locator for the card body element within that card using card.locator('div.card-body'). This method is commonly used to extract data from labeled sections on detail pages.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the links

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves all link elements within a card section identified by its title. First calls RABaseHelper.get_item_by_title() to get the card body element for the specified title. Then locates all anchor elements within that card body using items.locator('a'). Checks if any links were found by calling links.count(). If the count is 0, raises a ValueError indicating that no links were found in the section with that title. If links are found, returns the links locator object which can be used to interact with or extract information from the links. This method is useful for extracting navigation links or related resource links from titled sections on detail pages.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the links

functions.what_it_does: Extracts and returns a list of all href URL values from links within a card section identified by its title. First calls RABaseHelper.get_links_by_title() to get all link elements in the titled section. Then uses a list comprehension to iterate through each link by index, calling links.nth(i).get_attribute('href') to extract the href attribute value for each link. Returns a Python list containing all the extracted URL strings. This is useful for collecting all link destinations from a section for validation or further navigation in tests.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the field

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the text content from a field within a card section identified by its title. First calls RABaseHelper.get_item_by_title() to get the card body element for the specified title. Then calls inner_text() on the field element to extract all visible text content including nested elements. Finally strips leading and trailing whitespace using strip() and returns the cleaned text string. This method is commonly used to extract data values from labeled fields on detail pages for validation in test assertions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: link_text
parameters.description: The text content of the link to click (can be partial match)

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the link

functions.what_it_does: Clicks a specific link within a titled card section by searching for a link whose text contains the provided link_text. First calls RABaseHelper.get_links_by_title() to get all links in the section. Initializes a clicked flag to False. Then iterates through all links by index using range(links.count()). For each link, gets the link element using links.nth(i), extracts its text content using inner_text(), and strips whitespace. Checks if the link_text parameter is contained within the link's text content. If a match is found, clicks the link using link.click(), sets clicked flag to True, and breaks out of the loop. After the loop, asserts that clicked is True, raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if no matching link was found. Returns True if the link was successfully clicked. This method provides robust link clicking with partial text matching and clear error messages.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the boolean field

functions.what_it_does: Extracts a boolean value from a field by examining the icon class used to represent the boolean state. First calls RABaseHelper.get_item_by_title() to get the card body element for the specified title. Then locates the second span element within that field using field.locator('span').nth(1) and gets its class attribute value. Checks if the class string contains 'fa-check' (Font Awesome check icon) and returns True if found, indicating a true/enabled state. Checks if the class contains 'fa-times' (Font Awesome times/cross icon) and returns False if found, indicating a false/disabled state. If neither icon class is found, returns None indicating the boolean state could not be determined. This method is useful for extracting boolean field values that are displayed as icons rather than text.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the links

parameters.name: text_filter
parameters.description: The text string to filter links by (partial match)

functions.what_it_does: Filters and returns a list of link elements whose text content contains a specific text_filter string. First calls RABaseHelper.get_links_by_title() to get all links in the titled section. Initializes an empty filtered_links list. Then iterates through all links by index using range(all_links.count()). For each link, gets the link element using all_links.nth(i) and extracts its text content using inner_text(). Checks if the text_filter string is contained within the link text. If a match is found, appends the link element to the filtered_links list. After iterating through all links, returns the filtered_links list containing only the matching link elements. This method is useful for extracting a subset of links based on text content for further interaction or validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: current_date
parameters.description: Expected current date as string in 'MMM DD, YYYY' format or datetime object

parameters.name: final_bill_date
parameters.description: Expected final billing date as string in 'MMM DD, YYYY' format or datetime object

functions.what_it_does: Validates template data by extracting JSON from a pre element on the page and comparing date values against expected dates. First locates a pre element within the page-content section using '[class="page-content"] pre' and extracts its text content. Parses the text as JSON using json.loads() to get a data dictionary. Creates a timezone offset of 3 hours using timedelta(hours=3). Extracts the 'cancel_initiate_date' epoch timestamp from the data, converts it to a datetime object using datetime.fromtimestamp(), and adds the timezone offset. Extracts the 'final_charge_date_start' epoch timestamp, converts it to datetime, and adds the timezone offset. If current_date is a string, converts it to a datetime object using strptime with format '%b %d, %Y'. If final_bill_date is a string, converts it similarly. Asserts that the epoch_cancel_initiate_date formatted as 'MMM DD, YYYY' matches the current_date formatted the same way, raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if they don't match. Asserts that the epoch_date_time_start formatted as 'MMM DD, YYYY' matches the final_bill_date formatted the same way, raising an AssertionError if they don't match. This method provides comprehensive date validation with timezone adjustment and flexible input format handling.

functions.method_signature: def access_link_by_title_with_retry(page, link_text, title, max_attempts=12, delay=10)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: link_text
parameters.description: The text content of the link to click (can be partial match)

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the link

parameters.name: max_attempts
parameters.description: Maximum number of retry attempts. Defaults to 12.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Delay in seconds between retry attempts. Defaults to 10 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click a link within a titled section with automatic retry logic for handling timing issues or dynamic content. Calls the common.retry_operation() utility function with a lambda that wraps RABaseHelper.access_link_by_title(page, link_text, title). Passes the link_text as the operation identifier, max_attempts for retry count, delay for wait time between retries, and page.reload as the on_retry callback function. This means if the link click fails (link not found or not clickable), the page will be reloaded and the operation will be retried up to max_attempts times with delay seconds between each attempt. This provides robust link clicking for scenarios where links may appear dynamically or take time to become available after page load.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k,c,m,y' or 'cmy'). Valid values: k, c, m, y, cmy

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that specific cartridge types are displayed as installed on the shipment tracking page. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter by comma to get a list of individual cartridge types. Creates a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type strings ('k', 'c', 'm', 'y', 'cmy') to their corresponding selector strings retrieved from the page object using get_selector() method. Iterates through each cartridge type in the cartridges list. For each type, strips whitespace and checks if it exists in the cartridge_locators dictionary. If not found, raises a RuntimeError with a message indicating invalid cartridge type. If found, waits for the cartridge element to appear using page.wait_for_selector() with a 20-second timeout. Then asserts that the element is visible using is_visible(), raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if the cartridge is not visible. This method validates that all specified cartridge types are properly displayed on the page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: First cartridge type or types to verify (comma-separated string)

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Fallback cartridge type or types to verify if first verification fails

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to verify cartridge installation messages with fallback logic for handling different cartridge type scenarios. First tries to verify the cartridge parameter by calling ShipmentTrackingHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge) within a try block. If a RuntimeError is raised (indicating the first cartridge type was not found or invalid), catches the exception and attempts to verify the cartridge2 parameter instead by calling verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2). This provides flexible validation for scenarios where different printer models may use different cartridge configurations (e.g., separate color cartridges vs. combined CMY cartridge). If the second verification also fails, the RuntimeError will propagate up to the caller.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that all printer color cartridges are displayed as installed by checking each color returned from the common.printer_colors() utility function. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Creates a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping uppercase cartridge type strings ('K', 'C', 'M', 'Y', 'CMY') to their corresponding selector strings retrieved using get_selector(). Iterates through each cartridge type returned by common.printer_colors() which provides the list of colors for the current printer configuration. For each type, strips whitespace and checks if it exists in the cartridge_locators dictionary. If not found, raises a RuntimeError indicating invalid cartridge type. If found, waits for the cartridge element to appear with a 20-second timeout. Then asserts the element is visible, raising an AssertionError if not. This method validates that all cartridges for the current printer model are properly displayed, using dynamic color configuration rather than hardcoded values.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the view entire history link or button on the shipment tracking page to expand the full shipment history table. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Waits for the shipment_history_table element to be visible with a 30-second timeout using wait_for() with state='visible'. Once visible, clicks the element using click(). This action typically expands a collapsed history section or navigates to a full history view showing all past shipments and cartridge installations.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the shipment history table contains expected cartridge counts by parsing description text and validating numbers. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Waits for shipment_history_table to be visible with 30-second timeout and clicks it to expand the history. Gets the printer colors list from common.printer_colors(). Waits for the shipment_history_description element to be visible with 10-second timeout. Extracts the text content of the first description element using first.text_content() with 1-second timeout. Uses common.extract_numbers_from_text() to parse all numbers from the text into a cartridge_numbers list. Asserts that the length of colors list equals the length of cartridge_numbers list, raising an AssertionError if counts don't match. Asserts that the first cartridge number is '1', indicating 1 black cartridge. Asserts that the second cartridge number is '1', indicating 1 CMY or first color cartridge. If the colors list has 4 items (separate color cartridges), asserts that the third and fourth numbers are also '1'. Then extracts text from the last description element using last.text_content(). Parses numbers from this text into wk_numbers list. Asserts that wk_numbers has exactly 1 element. Asserts that the welcome kit number is '1'. This comprehensive validation ensures the shipment history shows the correct cartridge and welcome kit counts.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that free trial information elements are not visible on the update plan page. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Then uses Playwright's expect() assertion to verify that the free_trial_info_card element is not visible by calling expect(update_plan_page.free_trial_info_card).not_to_be_visible(). Similarly verifies that the free_trial_plan_details element is not visible using expect(update_plan_page.free_trial_plan_details).not_to_be_visible(). This method is used to confirm that free trial promotional content is not displayed for users who are not eligible for or not in a free trial period.

functions.method_signature: def verify_available_plans(page, expectedPlans = [10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, 1500], plan_type: str = None)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expectedPlans
parameters.description: List of expected plan page counts in order. Defaults to [10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, 1500].

parameters.name: plan_type
parameters.description: Optional plan type to verify. If 'ink_and_paper', verifies that plan cards include ink and paper. If None, verifies they do not include ink and paper.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the correct plan cards are displayed with expected page counts and plan type information. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses expect() to verify that plan_card_container is visible with a 120-second timeout. Locates all plan card elements using the selector '[data-testid^="plans-selector-plan-card-container"]' and gets them as a list with all(). Counts the plan elements and asserts that the count equals the length of expectedPlans list, raising an AssertionError if counts don't match. Iterates through each plan card by index. For each card, gets the text content using get_plan_by_position(i).text_content(). Uses regex re.findall() with pattern r'\d+\.\d+|\d+' to extract all numbers (including decimals) from the card text. Converts the first extracted number to an integer as actual_plan. Gets the corresponding expected_plan from the expectedPlans list. Asserts that actual_plan equals expected_plan, raising an AssertionError with details if they don't match. If plan_type is 'ink_and_paper', asserts that the text 'Includes ink and paper' is present in the card text. If plan_type is None, asserts that 'Includes ink and paper' is NOT present in the card text. This comprehensive validation ensures all plan cards display correct page counts and appropriate plan type messaging.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expectedPlan
parameters.description: The expected current plan page count (e.g., 10, 25, 50)

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific plan is marked as the current plan by checking for the presence of a plan tag indicator. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Gets the plan card element for the specified expectedPlan page count by calling update_plan_page.get_plan_by_pages(expectedPlan). Within that plan card, locates the plan tag container element using the selector '[data-testid$="plan-tag-container"]'. Uses expect() to verify that this tag container element is visible, which indicates the plan is marked as the current active plan. If the tag is not visible, the expect assertion will fail with a timeout error.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: The page count of the plan to select (e.g., 10, 25, 50, 100)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a subscription plan by clicking the button within the plan card for the specified page count. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Gets the plan card element for the specified plan_pages count by calling update_plan_page.get_plan_by_pages(plan_pages). Within that plan card, locates the button element using plan_card.locator('button'). Clicks the button to select that plan. This action typically initiates the plan change or upgrade/downgrade flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_button
parameters.description: The plan button identifier or index to select

functions.what_it_does: Selects a plan by clicking a specific plan button identified by an index or identifier, with explicit waits for button visibility and detachment. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Gets the plan button element by calling update_plan_page.get_select_plan_button(plan_button). Waits for the button to be visible with a 90-second timeout using wait_for() with state='visible'. Clicks the button using click(). Then waits for the button to be detached from the DOM with a 90-second timeout using wait_for() with state='detached', which indicates the plan selection action has completed and the page has transitioned. This method provides robust plan selection with explicit synchronization points.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: The 1-based position of the plan card to select (e.g., 1 for first plan, 2 for second plan)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a plan by clicking the button in the plan card at a specific position in the list. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Gets the plan card element at the specified position by calling update_plan_page.get_plan_by_position(position-1), subtracting 1 to convert from 1-based position to 0-based index. Within that plan card, locates the button element using locator('button'). Clicks the button to select that plan. This method is useful when you want to select a plan by its display order rather than by page count.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute if the cancellation feedback page appears. Called with parameters (step_name, page, screenshot_only=True).

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete subscription cancellation flow including clicking cancel link, confirming cancellation, and handling optional feedback page. First creates UpdatePlanPage and CancellationPage instances. Waits for the cancel_instant_ink link to be visible with a 90-second timeout using wait_for(). Clicks the cancel link to initiate cancellation. Waits for the confirm_cancellation_button to be visible with a 120-second timeout. Clicks the confirmation button to proceed with cancellation. Then enters a try-except block to handle the optional cancellation feedback page. Tries to wait for the continue_button to be visible with a 90-second timeout. If the button appears, prints a message indicating the feedback page is visible, sleeps for 5 seconds, and if a callback function was provided, calls it with parameters ('Returned_Confirmation_Page', page, screenshot_only=True) to allow custom handling like taking a screenshot. If the continue button does not appear (TimeoutError), the except block clicks the confirm_cancellation_button again to complete the cancellation. This method handles both cancellation flows - with and without the feedback page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Validates that clicking the terms of service link opens a new popup window with the correct terms page URL. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses page.expect_popup() context manager with a 30-second timeout to capture the popup window that will open. Within the context, clicks the terms_and_service_link element. After the click, gets the popup page object from popup_info.value. Uses expect() to verify that the popup page URL matches the regex pattern r'/terms' with a 90-second timeout, ensuring the terms page loaded correctly. Finally closes the popup page using terms_page.close(). This method validates both the popup behavior and the correct navigation to the terms page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: The expected plan page count as a string (e.g., '50', '100')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a plan change message banner is displayed with the correct plan page count. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses expect() to verify that the pending_change_plan element is visible with a 90-second timeout. Extracts the actual text content from the banner using inner_text(). Uses regex re.findall() with pattern r'\d+' to extract all numbers from the text into a numbers list. Asserts that the plan parameter string is present in the numbers list, raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if the expected plan number is not found in the banner text. This validates that the plan change confirmation message displays the correct target plan.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: The button to click - either 'Cancel' or 'Undo Upgrade'

functions.what_it_does: Handles the undo plan change modal by clicking either the cancel button or the undo confirmation button. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Clicks the cancel_change_plan_link to open the undo plan change modal. Then checks the button parameter value. If button is 'Cancel', clicks the undo_plan_change_cancel_button with a 90-second timeout, then uses expect() to verify the cancel button is no longer visible, confirming the modal closed without undoing the change. If button is 'Undo Upgrade', clicks the undo_plan_change_confirmation button with a 90-second timeout, then uses expect() to verify the confirmation button is no longer visible, confirming the plan change was successfully undone and the modal closed. This method provides conditional logic for handling both modal outcomes.

functions.method_signature: def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price or value as a string (e.g., '9.99', '19.99')

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan page count as a string (e.g., '50', '100')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan details card displays the correct plan value and page count. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses expect() to verify that the plan_details_card element is visible with a 90-second timeout. Extracts the text content from the plan_information element using text_content(). Uses common.extract_numbers_from_text() to parse all numbers from the text into a numbers list. Asserts that the first number in the list equals the plan_value parameter, raising an AssertionError with details if they don't match. Asserts that the second number in the list equals the plan_pages parameter, raising an AssertionError if they don't match. This validates that the plan information section displays both the correct pricing and page count for the selected plan.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: elements
parameters.description: ElementLocators instance or subclass containing selector definitions

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the WaitProxy by storing the Playwright page instance and the elements object containing selector definitions. Sets self.page to the provided page parameter. Sets self.elements to the provided elements parameter. These stored references are used by the __getattr__ method to create wait functions that call page.wait_for_selector with the appropriate selector string.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The attribute name being accessed (should match a selector name in elements)

functions.what_it_does: Intercepts attribute access on the WaitProxy instance to provide dynamic wait functions. When an attribute is accessed (e.g., wait.login_button), checks if the elements object has an attribute with that name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If found, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name). Returns a lambda function that accepts keyword arguments and calls self.page.wait_for_selector(selector, **kwargs), allowing the caller to pass timeout and other wait options. If the attribute name is not found in elements, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message indicating no selector with that name exists for wait operations. This enables concise wait syntax like 'self.wait.login_button(timeout=5000)' instead of verbose wait_for_selector calls.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the BasePageObject by storing the Playwright page instance, creating an ElementLocators instance, and creating a WaitProxy instance. Sets self.page to the provided page parameter for use in all page interactions. Creates self.elements as a new ElementLocators() instance which will be overridden by child classes with their own Elements inner class containing actual selector definitions. Creates self.wait as a new WaitProxy(page, self.elements) instance to provide convenient wait operations. These three attributes form the foundation of the page object pattern used throughout the framework.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The attribute name being accessed (should match a selector name in elements)

functions.what_it_does: Implements dynamic attribute access to automatically convert selector strings into Playwright locator objects. When an attribute is accessed on the page object (e.g., page_obj.login_button), checks if the elements object has an attribute with that name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If found, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name) and returns self.page.locator(locator_str) which creates and returns a Playwright Locator object for that selector. If the attribute name is not found in elements, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message including the class name. This magic method enables page objects to access locators as simple attributes without manually creating locator objects, making page object code much cleaner and more readable.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The selector name to retrieve

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the raw selector string for a given selector name without creating a Playwright locator object. Checks if the elements object has an attribute with the provided name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If found, returns the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name). If not found, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message including the class name and selector name. This method is useful when you need the actual selector string rather than a locator object, such as when passing selectors to helper methods or when constructing dynamic selectors.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

Then defines all cancellation banner UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Creates locators for see_cancellation_timeline link using data-testid selector, keep_subscription_button using a composite selector targeting a button within the cancellation banner, cancellation_banner container, resume_button and back_button for the subscription resume modal, and resume_banner for the success message. All locators are stored as string selectors on the elements object for later use by test methods.

parameters.name: self

Then defines an extensive collection of 82 locators covering all elements across the entire cancellation flow. Locators are organized into logical groups: printer information elements (printer_img, printer_info_name, printer_info_serial), cancellation summary elements (cancellation_summary_image, subscribed_info, confirm_cancellation_button, keep_enrollment_button), retention section elements (change_plan_link, pause_plan_link, transfer_subscription_link, contact_hp_support_link), change plan modal elements with all plan buttons from 10 to 1500 pages, cancellation feedback survey elements with multiple radio button options for different cancellation reasons, paper subscription specific elements, timeline elements, and various modal and navigation elements. All locators are stored as string selectors on the self.elements object.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: The number of pages for the plan button to retrieve (e.g., '10', '25', '50', '100', '300', '500', '700', '1500')

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves a Playwright locator object for a specific plan button based on the provided plan_pages parameter. Constructs the attribute name by formatting plan_attr as f'plan_{plan_pages}_button' (e.g., 'plan_50_button' for plan_pages='50'). Uses hasattr() to check if the self.elements object has an attribute with that name. If the attribute exists, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, plan_attr), creates a Playwright locator using self.page.locator(selector), and returns the locator object. If the attribute does not exist, raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating that the plan button for the specified page count was not found. This method provides a flexible way to select plan buttons without hardcoding each plan option in test code.

Creates locators for the signup button, promotional page elements (background and disclaimer), keypoint intelligence link, shipping/billing page, six footer navigation links (HP.com, Wireless Print Center, Help Center, Terms of Use, Privacy, Privacy Choices), subscription state indicator, enrollment link, printer options control, update plan link, remove printer link, printers page title, and print plans page container. All locators use data-testid attributes for reliability where available, with some using CSS class or ID selectors with partial matching for dynamic elements.

parameters.name: self

Sets the default timeout for all page operations to 30000 milliseconds (30 seconds) using page.set_default_timeout(30000). Then defines all side menu UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Creates locators for the mobile menu toggle button, the HP Instant Ink parent menu item, five submenu items under Instant Ink (Overview, Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking), the My Account parent menu item, two submenu items under My Account (Shipping & Billing, Notifications), and the Printers menu item. All locators use data-testid attributes for reliable element identification.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The submenu element to click

parameters.name: parent_element
parameters.description: The parent menu element that needs to be expanded first

functions.what_it_does: Private helper method that attempts to click a target submenu element with automatic parent menu expansion if needed. First tries to click the target_element directly. If that click fails (raises an exception, likely because the element is not visible or not clickable), it catches the exception, then clicks the parent_element to expand the parent menu, and then clicks the target_element again. This handles the common scenario where submenu items are hidden until their parent menu is expanded. The try-except block with bare except ensures the method continues even if the first click attempt fails for any reason.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The submenu element under HP Instant Ink to click

functions.what_it_does: Private helper method that attempts to click a submenu element under the HP Instant Ink menu with intelligent handling of mobile layout and parent menu expansion. First tries to click the target_element with a short 2000ms timeout and returns immediately if successful. If that fails, it catches the exception and continues. Then checks if the mobile menu toggle button (expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout) is visible, and if so, clicks it to expand the mobile menu and waits for the target_element to become visible with a 30000ms timeout. This handles mobile responsive layouts where the menu is initially collapsed. Both of these steps are wrapped in try-except blocks to handle cases where they are not needed. Finally, calls _click_with_parent_expansion to click the target element with automatic expansion of the instant_ink_menu_link parent if needed. This method handles three levels of menu complexity: direct click, mobile menu expansion, and parent menu expansion.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The submenu element under My Account to click

functions.what_it_does: Private helper method that clicks a submenu element under the My Account menu with automatic parent menu expansion. Simply calls _click_with_parent_expansion with the target_element and my_account_menu_link as the parent. This is a convenience wrapper that simplifies calling code by not requiring the caller to specify the parent menu link.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

Calls _click_with_instant_ink_expansion with overview_menu_link to handle mobile menu expansion and parent HP Instant Ink menu expansion if needed. This method abstracts away the complexity of menu expansion logic and provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the overview page.

If not visible, it attempts to expand the mobile menu toggle if it is visible by clicking expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout with a 30000ms timeout, wrapped in a try-except block to handle cases where mobile menu is not present. Then clicks the instant_ink_menu_link to expand the parent menu. This method is useful when you need to verify submenu items are present without clicking them.

parameters.name: self

If not visible, clicks the my_account_menu_link to expand the parent menu. This method is useful when you need to verify submenu items are present without clicking them.

parameters.name: self

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: The card index or identifier to locate a specific plan card

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for a specific ink plan card's pages container by dynamically constructing a selector based on the provided card parameter. Uses page.locator() with an f-string to build the selector '[data-testid="landing-page-plans-container"] [data-testid^="lp-plans-plan-card-{card}"] [class^="styles__PagesContainer-"]' where {card} is replaced with the card parameter value. This allows tests to target specific plan cards by their index or identifier. The locator targets the pages container within a specific plan card, which likely contains pagination or page indicators for multi-page plan details.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: The card index or identifier to locate a specific plan card

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for a specific ink plan card by dynamically constructing a selector based on the provided card parameter. Uses page.locator() with an f-string to build the selector '[data-testid="landing-page-plans-container"] [data-testid="lp-plans-plan-card-{card}"] [class^="styles__PagesContainer-"]' where {card} is replaced with the card parameter value. This allows tests to target specific plan cards by their index or identifier for interaction or verification. The locator targets the entire plan card container including all its content.

parameters.name: self

Uses page.locator() with the flag_icon selector from self.elements and calls .first to get only the first matching element. This is useful when there are multiple flag icons on the page but you only want to interact with the first one, typically the one in the header. The property decorator allows this to be accessed as an attribute rather than a method call.

This ensures that even if there are multiple elements matching the selector, only the first one is returned. The property decorator allows this to be accessed as an attribute rather than a method call.

Then defines five notification-related locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Sets self.elements.table to the jarvis-notifications-table data-testid selector. Constructs self.elements.table_row by concatenating the table selector with ' tr' to target table rows. Sets self.elements.notifications_box to the portal-notifications-center data-testid selector. Finally sets self.elements.notifications_table to a composite selector that matches either jarvis-notifications-table or portal-notifications-center. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.table selector string to create a locator for the jarvis-notifications-table element. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element if multiple tables exist on the page. This property allows test code to access the notifications table using notifications_page.table syntax without calling it as a method. Returns a Playwright Locator object that can be used for further interactions like clicking, getting text, or waiting for visibility.

parameters.name: self

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the OssEmulatorPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines 52 locators organized into logical sections. First defines Dev Tab locators including dev_tab, oss_hpid_login, sign_in_button, username_input, user_name_form_submit, password_input, novelli_button, and app_post_oobe_agreements_features for authentication and navigation. Then defines Quick Options locators like yeti_3m for preset configurations. Next defines extensive Device Tab locators including serial_number, sku, uuid, cloud_id, fingerprint, registration_state, usage_tracking_consent, language_config, country_config, load_main_tray, insert_ink, and calibration for device configuration. Defines App Tab locators including app_tab, app_type, and app_type_label for application settings. Defines Page Elements like start_flow_button for flow control. Defines extensive Offers Elements including accept_all_button, Hp_Plus_Continue_button, Activate_HP_Plus, do_not_activate_hp_plus, decline_hp_plus, ds_continue_button, next_button, learn_more_button, value_prop_title, instant_ink_reminder, and value_prop_learn_more for handling subscription offers. Defines Post-OOBE Elements including subscription_checkbox and oobe_checkbox for post-setup configuration. Finally defines printer replacement elements including replace_printer_radio_button, continue_button, and continue_selection. All locators are stored on the self.elements object for access throughout the page object.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the overview/dashboard is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the OverviewPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines 157 locators organized into logical functional sections. Defines enrollment and printer replacement locators including enroll_or_replace_button, enroll_printer_button, replace_printer_button, finished_replacing_printer_modal, complete_enrollment_button, connect_later_button, printer_replacement_page, and enroll_modal_close. functions.what_it_does: Waits for the page title element to appear on the overview page and returns the element. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will raise a TimeoutError.

parameters.name: self

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the change plan link element from the overview page. Calls self.page.get_by_test_id() with the self.elements.change_plan_link selector value (which is '[data-testid='change-plan-link'] > span') to locate the element using Playwright's get_by_test_id method. Then calls .first on the result to return only the first matching element if multiple exist. Returns a Playwright Locator object for the change plan link that can be used for further interactions like clicking or getting text. This method provides convenient access to the change plan link without requiring test code to know the exact selector.

Then calls .first on the result to return only the first matching element. Returns a Playwright Locator object for the printer name element that can be used to get the text content or verify visibility. This method is useful for verifying which printer is currently selected or displayed in the status card.

Then calls .last on the locator to return the last matching element if multiple exist (since RAF codes may appear in multiple places). This property allows test code to access the RAF code using overview_page.raf_code syntax without calling it as a method. Returns a Playwright Locator object that can be used to get the text content of the referral code or URL for sharing.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PlanSelectorV3Page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then defines 13 locators for the plan selector interface. Sets self.elements.plans_selector_v3 to the plan selector combobox data-testid. Sets self.elements.plans_listbox_v3 to the role='listbox' selector for the dropdown list. Sets self.elements.plan_option to role='option' for individual plan options. Sets self.elements.ink_paper_button and self.elements.ink_only_button for the two plan type selection buttons. Sets self.elements.content_area_v3 for the main content area. Sets self.elements.continue_button for the proceed button. Defines four header element locators: virtual_agent_link, support_phone_number_link, country_selector_button, and account_button. Finally defines two plan card locators: pay_as_you_print_plan_card and monthly_plan_card. All locators are stored on the self.elements object for access throughout the page object.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PlanSelectorV3Page object

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: The plan page count value to select, such as '10', '50', '100', '300', '700'. This is the number of pages included in the plan.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific subscription plan from the plan selector dropdown by clicking the option that contains the provided plan_value. Constructs an XPath selector using f-string formatting: '//*[@role="option" and contains(normalize-space(.),"{plan_value} ")]' which finds an element with role='option' whose normalized text content contains the plan value followed by a space. Logs an info message using framework_logger.info() stating which plan is being selected with the constructed selector. Then calls self.page.locator(option_selector).click() to locate the plan option and click it. Logs another info message confirming the plan selection was clicked successfully. This method handles the plan selection interaction so test code can simply call select_plan('100') to choose the 100-page plan without needing to know the exact selector logic.

This extended timeout accommodates potentially slow operations related to printer updates. After setting the timeout, defines the continue button locator as self.elements.continue_button using data-testid selector '[data-testid='btn-continue']'. parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.what_it_does: Accepts automatic printer updates by clicking the continue button on the printer updates page. The method is wrapped in a try-except block for error handling. Inside the try block, it accesses the continue_button locator through self.continue_button (which is an alias for self.elements.continue_button provided by BasePageObject) and calls the click() method on it to perform the click action. If any exception occurs during the click operation (such as element not found, element not clickable, or timeout), the except block catches the exception and prints an error message to the console using print() with the format 'Automatic updates continue button not found or could not be clicked: {e}' where {e} is the exception details. The method does not raise the exception, allowing the test to continue even if the button click fails.

Inside the try block, it accesses the accept_button locator through self.accept_button (which is an alias for self.elements.accept_button provided by BasePageObject) and calls the click() method on it to perform the click action. If any exception occurs during the click operation (such as element not found, element not clickable, or timeout), the except block catches the exception and prints an error message to the console using print() with the format 'Privacy banner not found or could not be accepted: {e}' where {e} is the exception details. The method does not raise the exception, allowing the test to continue even if the banner is not present or cannot be clicked, which is appropriate since the privacy banner may not appear on every page load.

parameters.description: Zero-based index of the FAQ question to retrieve. For example, 0 for the first question, 1 for the second question, etc.

functions.docstring: Returns the locator for a specific FAQ question by index (0-based).
functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for a specific FAQ question button element based on the provided zero-based index. First creates a locator for all FAQ question buttons using self.page.locator(self.elements.faq_question) which matches all buttons within the accordion with selector '[data-testid="accordion"] button'. Then uses the .nth(index) method to select the specific question at the given index position. This allows tests to interact with individual FAQ questions by their position in the list, such as clicking to expand a specific question or verifying its text content. The returned locator can be used for click actions, text retrieval, or visibility checks.

parameters.name: self

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: String name of the selector to retrieve from the elements dictionary, such as 'page_title', 'login_button', 'faq_card', etc.

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a selector string from the elements dictionary by name. First checks if the provided name exists as a key in the self.elements dictionary using the 'in' operator. If the name exists, returns the corresponding selector string value from self.elements[name]. If the name does not exist in the elements dictionary, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message indicating that the selector name was not found in this page object class. This method provides a safe way to access selectors dynamically with proper error handling, which is useful when selector names are determined at runtime or passed as parameters from test data.

The page elements section includes 6 general locators: page title, cancel button, notification messages, modal close button, and continue button. All locators are stored as attributes of the inherited self.elements dictionary for organized access.

parameters.name: page

functions.docstring: Selects the business account type radio button
functions.what_it_does: Selects the business account type by clicking the business radio button in the billing form. This is a static method that takes a Playwright page instance as a parameter rather than using self. It creates a locator for the business radio button using the selector '[data-testid="business-radio-button"]', then uses .first to get the first matching element (in case multiple exist), and performs a click action with force=True to ensure the click succeeds even if the element is not in the viewport or is obscured. The force=True parameter bypasses Playwright's actionability checks. This method is typically called during billing setup when the user needs to specify they are setting up a business account rather than a personal account, which then reveals additional fields like company name.

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves the change plan button selector for a specific page count plan. Constructs an attribute name by concatenating 'change_plan_button_' with the pages parameter (e.g., 'change_plan_button_100' for pages=100). Uses hasattr() to check if the UpdatePlanPage instance has an attribute with that name. If the attribute exists, uses getattr() to retrieve and return the selector value from self.elements. If the attribute does not exist (meaning the page count is not one of the supported plans), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating that the plan with the specified page count was not found. This method provides a programmatic way to access plan buttons without hardcoding the specific page count in test code.

Constructs a data-testid selector string by formatting 'plans-selector-plan-card-container-{plan_number}' with the provided plan_number parameter. Calls self.page.locator() with the constructed selector string to create and return a Playwright Locator object that can be used for interactions like clicking, checking visibility, or extracting text. This method allows tests to interact with plan cards by their position rather than by page count, which is useful when the exact page count mapping is not known or when iterating through all available plans.

Uses hasattr() to check if the UpdatePlanPage instance has an attribute with that name on self.elements. If the attribute exists, uses getattr() to retrieve and return the selector value from self.elements. If the attribute does not exist (meaning the page count is not one of the supported plans), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating that the plan with the specified page count was not found. This method provides a programmatic way to access plan card selectors without hardcoding the specific page count in test code, enabling data-driven test approaches.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the cancel instant ink link with special handling. Accesses the cancel_instant_ink selector from self.elements.cancel_instant_ink, passes it to self.page.locator() to create a Locator object, and then calls .last on the result to get the last matching element. This is necessary because there may be multiple cancel links on the page (one in the plan details card and one in the header), and this property ensures the one in the plan details card is selected by taking the last occurrence. Returns the Locator object that can be used for clicking or checking visibility. The @property decorator allows this method to be accessed like an attribute without parentheses.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the free trial info card with special handling. Accesses the free_trial_info_card selector from self.elements.free_trial_info_card, passes it to self.page.locator() to create a Locator object, and then calls .first on the result to get the first matching element. This is necessary because the class selector may match multiple elements on the page, and this property ensures the correct free trial info card is selected by taking the first occurrence. Returns the Locator object that can be used for extracting text, checking visibility, or other interactions. The @property decorator allows this method to be accessed like an attribute without parentheses.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PrinterData instance by setting named attributes for all six printer data fields. Assigns self.entity_id, self.model_number, self.device_uuid, self.cloud_id, self.postcard, and self.fingerprint from the provided parameters. parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Dictionary containing configuration parameters for stack, profile, country, payment settings, and other test execution parameters

functions.what_it_does: Initializes all module-level global variables from the provided args dictionary and STACK_CONFIG. Extracts stack, profile, derivative_model, biz_model, tenant_country, tenant_country_short, tenant_country_language_code, tenant_address, headless, target, simulator_platform, target_width, target_height, supplyvariant, card_payment_gateway, and payment_method from args. Looks up the stack configuration from STACK_CONFIG dictionary using the stack value. Sets all URL endpoints (instantink_url, agena_url, cartridgepacker_url, fulfillmentservice_url, oss_url, hpid_url, stratus_authz, stratus_tropos, usermgt, ucde_authz, ucde_tropos, ucde_server, portalshell_url, onboarding_center_url) from the stack config. Sets Rails admin configuration (gemini_rails_admin_login_url, gemini_fetch_tenant_url, agena_cps_and_fulfillment_rails_admin_login_url, rails_admin_user, rails_admin_password). Sets client IDs and secrets (stratus_client_id, ucde_client_id, gemini_client_id, client_secrets). Sets MongoDB and S3 storage configuration. Logs all configuration values at debug level. This function must be called before any other test flow functions to ensure all global configuration is properly initialized.

Convenience setup function that calls set_globals with configuration values extracted from GlobalState. Builds the args dictionary by reading stack, printer_profile, derivative_model, biz_model, supplyvariant, country, country_code, language_code, address_payload, headless, target, simulator_platform, card_payment_gateway, and payment_method from GlobalState attributes. Then calls set_globals(args) to initialize all module-level globals. Also creates a 'temp' directory if it doesn't exist for temporary file storage. This function is typically called at the beginning of test execution to initialize the test environment from GlobalState configuration.
functions.usage_example: setup()  # Called at test initialization to set up environment from GlobalState

functions.what_it_does: Generates a unique test email address for tenant account creation using the Gmail plus addressing feature. Creates a random 8-character suffix using lowercase letters and digits. Constructs email in format 'hello.instantink+{suffix}@gmail.com'. Stores the generated email in the global _tenant_email variable. Logs the generated email at info level. Returns the generated email string. This email format allows all test emails to be received by a single Gmail account (hello.instantink@gmail.com) while maintaining unique addresses for each test run, which is useful for verification code retrieval and account isolation.

Authenticates with Gmail API using OAuth2 refresh token credentials. Creates a Credentials object with the GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN, GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET, and GMAIL_SCOPE from environment variables. Calls creds.refresh(Request()) to obtain a fresh access token from the refresh token. If refresh fails with invalid_grant or invalid_request error, raises RuntimeError with message to update the refresh token. Builds and returns a Gmail API service object using googleapiclient.discovery.build with the refreshed credentials. This service object is used by fetch_verification_code to retrieve verification emails.

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: HTML content of verification email

functions.what_it_does:
Extracts the 6-digit verification code from HPID verification email HTML content. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse the HTML. Searches for a

element with class 'code' which contains the verification code. If the element is not found, raises RuntimeError. Extracts the text content from the element using get_text(strip=True). Logs the extracted code at debug level. Returns the verification code string. This function is deprecated in favor of new_extract_verification_code which has more robust pattern matching.

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address to search for verification code

functions.what_it_does: Fetches the verification code from Gmail for the specified tenant email address with automatic retry logic. Decorated with @retry to attempt up to 3 times with 5-second wait between attempts. Calls authenticate_gmail() to get Gmail API service. Constructs query string 'to:{tenant_email} newer_than:5m' to find recent messages. Calls Gmail API users().messages().list() to search for messages. If no messages found, raises RuntimeError. Gets the full message content using users().messages().get() with format='full' for the first message. Extracts the message parts and searches for HTML body data in either the main payload body or in parts with mimeType='text/html'. If no HTML data found, raises RuntimeError. Base64 decodes the HTML data. Calls new_extract_verification_code() to extract the code from HTML. Returns the verification code string. The retry decorator ensures transient failures (email not yet received) are automatically retried.
functions.usage_example: code = fetch_verification_code('hello.instantink+test123@gmail.com')  # Automatically retries up to 3 times

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new HPID account through the HPID sign-up UI flow. Navigates to the HPID URL (_hpid_url). Sets viewport size to 1366x768. Waits for and clicks the sign-up button. Fills the firstName field with DEFAULT_FIRSTNAME. Fills the lastName field with DEFAULT_LASTNAME. Fills the email field with the provided email or global _tenant_email. Fills the password field with DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks the sign-up submit button. Waits for page load. Executes the optional callback function if provided. Enters a 60-second polling loop checking for either the submit-code element (onboarding center flow) or 'myaccount' in URL (my account flow). If submit-code element appears, calls confirm_code_by_onboarding_center(). If myaccount URL detected, calls confirm_code_by_my_account(). Sleeps 2 seconds between checks. Logs success message. Returns the page object. This function handles the HPID account creation flow which can branch into different verification flows depending on the environment.

parameters.name: check_func

functions.what_it_does: Safely executes a check function and returns its result, catching any exceptions and returning False if an exception occurs. Wraps the check_func call in a try-except block. If the function executes successfully, returns its result. If any exception occurs, catches it and returns False. This is used in polling loops to check for element presence or URL conditions without raising exceptions when elements are not yet present.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Confirms HPID account creation by entering verification code in the My Account flow. Clicks the security tab using locator 'li[id="security"]'. Clicks the Verify button using get_by_role. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the code from email. Fills the verification code textbox using get_by_role with the retrieved code. Clicks the Verify button again to submit the code. This handles the verification flow when HPID creation redirects to the My Account page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Confirms HPID account creation by entering verification code in the Onboarding Center flow. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the code from email. Fills the code field (#code) with the retrieved code. Clicks the submit-code button (#submit-code). Attempts to wait for country residence selector (#countryResidence) with 20-second timeout. If country selector appears, clicks it and selects the country using data-value attribute matching _tenant_country_short. Clicks the continue button (#continue-button). If country selector does not appear (timeout exception), logs debug message and continues. Logs success message. This handles the verification flow when HPID creation goes through the Onboarding Center which may require country selection.

Retrieves the verification code from Gmail with retry logic. Initializes code variable to None. Loops 3 attempts (1 to 3). In each attempt, tries to call fetch_verification_code(_tenant_email). If successful, breaks out of loop. If exception occurs, logs warning with attempt number and error message, then sleeps 15 seconds before next attempt. After loop, if code is still None, raises RuntimeError indicating all 3 attempts failed. Logs the retrieved code at info level. Returns the code string. This provides a higher-level retry wrapper around fetch_verification_code with longer delays between attempts.
functions.usage_example: code = get_verification_code()  # Retries up to 3 times with 15-second delays

parameters.name: test_requirements
