functions.docstring: Determine plan pages from requirements or use default.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan page count from test requirements or returns default value. Extracts plan_pages value from test_requirements dictionary using get(). If plan_pages is not present or is None/empty, sets plan_pages to default value '100' and logs info message. If plan_pages is present, logs info message with the value from requirements. Returns the plan_pages value. This allows tests to specify custom plan page counts or fall back to a sensible default.

functions.docstring: Determine plan type from parameter or requirements.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan type (ink_only or ink_and_paper) from parameter or test requirements. If plan_type_param is not 'ink_only', logs info message and returns plan_type_param as-is. If plan_type_param is 'ink_only', extracts paper_option from test_requirements. Sets plan_type to 'ink_and_paper' if paper_option is 'add', otherwise sets to 'ink_only'. Logs info message with determined plan type and paper_option value. Returns the plan_type string. This allows plan type to be determined either explicitly via parameter or implicitly via paper_option requirement.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account through the complete sign-up flow. First tests browser connectivity by navigating to about:blank. Then navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait and 120-second timeout. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Attempts to accept privacy banner by clicking #onetrust-accept-btn-handler with 10-second timeout, logs debug if not found. Locates sign-up button using multiple test-id selectors and waits for visibility with 3000-second timeout (likely a typo, should be 30 seconds). Clicks sign-up button. Waits for create-account button to be visible. Clicks create-account button. Waits for sign-in element to be visible. Waits explicitly for firstName field to be visible with 60-second timeout. Waits additional 1 second for form to be interactive. Fills firstName, lastName, email (_tenant_email), and password (DEFAULT_PASSWORD) fields. Clicks market checkbox. Clicks sign-up submit button. Waits for submit-code element with 30-second timeout. Attempts to fetch verification code 3 times with 5-second delays between attempts. If all attempts fail, raises RuntimeError. Fills code field with retrieved code. Clicks submit-code button. Waits for stepper element to be visible with 120-second timeout. Logs success message. Navigates to _portalshell_url with 60-second timeout. Waits 60 seconds. Attempts to click side menu toggle, ignores exception if not found. Clicks HP Instant Ink side menu item with 120-second timeout. Returns the page object. This is a comprehensive account creation flow that handles the full v2 enrollment UI including privacy banner, account creation form, email verification, and navigation to portal shell.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Logs into an existing Instant Ink v2 account. Logs info message with username. Locates sign-up button using multiple test-id selectors. Checks if sign-up button is visible with 5-second timeout. If not visible, navigates to _instantink_url with 120-second timeout. Attempts to accept privacy banner by clicking #onetrust-accept-btn-handler with 10-second timeout, logs debug if not found. Clicks the first sign-up button. Sleeps 5 seconds. Waits for and clicks sign-in button. Fills username field with provided username. Clicks 'Use password' link. Fills password field with DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks sign-in button. If oobe parameter is True, waits for 'Add a different printer' radio button to be visible with 180-second timeout, clicks it, waits for continue button to be visible with 8-second timeout, and clicks continue button. Returns the page object. This handles the login flow for existing accounts with optional OOBE device selection flow.

functions.what_it_does: Logs out of Instant Ink v2 account. Locates account button using test-id 'account-button'. Locates sign-out button using test-id 'sign-out-button'. Uses expect() to verify account button is visible with 3-second timeout. Clicks account button. Uses expect() to verify sign-out button is visible with 3-second timeout. Clicks sign-out button. Locates sign-up button using multiple test-id selectors. Uses expect() to verify sign-up button is visible with 120-second timeout to confirm logout completed. This ensures the user is fully logged out and returned to the landing page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Onboards an HPID account to the UCDE (Unified Cloud Device Ecosystem) system via OAuth flow and API calls. Generates a random GUID for OAuth state parameter. Constructs UCDE authorization URL with openid/email/profile scope, authorization code response type, state GUID, UCDE client ID, and redirect URI. Logs the authorization URL at debug level. Navigates page to the authorization URL with 60-second timeout. Waits for page load. Sleeps 5 seconds. Captures the final URL after OAuth redirect. Logs the final URL. Verifies 'code=' is present in URL, raises RuntimeError if not found. Extracts the authorization code from URL query parameters. Logs the retrieved OAuth code. Constructs token exchange URL. Creates Basic authentication header by base64 encoding client_id:client_secret. Sends POST request to token endpoint with authorization_code grant type, code, and redirect_uri. Logs token exchange response. Raises exception if response status is not successful. Extracts access_token and id_token from response JSON. Raises RuntimeError if either token is missing. Logs success message. Constructs UCDE account creation URL. Sends POST request with Bearer access_token, JSON body containing countrySet array with _tenant_country_short, idToken, and language code. Logs account creation response. Raises exception if response status is not 201. Logs success message with stack and country/language. Returns the page object. This function performs the complete HPID to UCDE onboarding flow including OAuth authorization, token exchange, and account creation API calls.

Obtains an OAuth access token for the Gemini client using client credentials flow. Constructs token URL using _stratus_authz endpoint with /openid/v1/token path. Creates Basic authentication header by base64 encoding gemini_client_id:gemini_client_secret. Sends POST request to token endpoint with Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded, Authorization Basic header, and grant_type=client_credentials in body. Raises exception if response status is not successful. Extracts access_token from response JSON. Returns the access token string. This token is used for Gemini API calls that require service-level authentication rather than user-level authentication.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to get token for, defaults to global _tenant_email

functions.what_it_does: Obtains an organization-aware user access token through a multi-step token exchange process. Step 1: Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get Gemini service token, logs first 8 characters. Step 2: Sends GET request to usermgt service to find user by email using Gemini token, extracts userResourceId and idpId from first result, logs values. Step 3: Sends GET request to usermgt service to get user tenant details using userResourceId, extracts tenantResourceId from first result, logs value. Step 4: Gets Stratus client token by sending POST to token endpoint with client_credentials grant using Stratus client ID and secret, logs first 8 characters. Step 5: Performs token exchange by sending POST to token endpoint with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange, subject_token=Stratus client token, subject_token_type=access_token, requested_user_type=stratusid, requested_user=userResourceId, and tenant_id=tenantResourceId. Extracts org-aware access token from response. Logs first 8 characters of final token. Returns tuple of (org_token, tenant_id). This complex token exchange flow is required to obtain a user-scoped token that is aware of the user's organization/tenant context, which is needed for device claiming and other tenant-scoped operations.

functions.method_signature: def retry_operation(operation, operation_name='Operation', max_attempts=5, delay=2, on_retry=None)

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Callable function to execute with retry logic

parameters.name: operation_name
parameters.description: Descriptive name for logging

parameters.name: max_attempts
parameters.description: Maximum number of retry attempts

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Delay in seconds between retry attempts

parameters.name: on_retry
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute before each retry

functions.what_it_does: Generic retry wrapper that executes an operation with configurable retry logic. Loops from attempt 1 to max_attempts. Logs info message with operation name and attempt number. Executes the operation callable. If successful, logs success message and returns the result. If exception occurs, logs warning with error message. If this was the last attempt, logs error message and re-raises the exception. If on_retry callback is provided, executes it in a try-except block, logging warning if callback fails. Logs info message about retrying with delay. Sleeps for the specified delay. Continues to next attempt. This provides a reusable retry pattern for operations that may fail transiently, with optional cleanup/recovery logic via the on_retry callback.
functions.usage_example: result = retry_operation(lambda: api_call(), operation_name='API Call', max_attempts=3, delay=5, on_retry=lambda: reset_connection())

functions.what_it_does: Creates a virtual printer through the G2 simulator API with retry logic for each step. Defines helper function make_request(method, url, **kwargs) that sends HTTP request using session, raises exception on error, and returns response. Step 1 (step_create_printer): Constructs URL to G2 simulator printers endpoint. Builds JSON body with stack, profile, biz_model, offer=1, fipsflag='true', and derivative_model. Logs body. Calls make_request with POST method. Returns response JSON. Wraps step 1 in retry_operation with 5 max attempts. Extracts entity_id, model_number, and device_uuid from response. Logs printer creation with serial number, model, and UUID. Step 2 (step_register_printer): Constructs URL to register endpoint for entity_id. Calls make_request with POST method. Returns cloud_id from response JSON. Wraps step 2 in retry_operation with 5 max attempts. Logs cloud_id. Step 3 (step_get_postcard): Constructs URL to claimpostcard endpoint for entity_id. Calls make_request with POST method. Gets response text and strips whitespace. If text starts with '{', base64 encodes it, otherwise removes quotes. Returns postcard string. Wraps step 3 in retry_operation with 3 max attempts. Step 4 (step_get_fingerprint): Constructs URL to devicefingerprint endpoint for entity_id. Calls make_request with GET method. Gets response text and strips whitespace. If text starts with '{', base64 encodes it, otherwise removes quotes. Returns fingerprint string. Wraps step 4 in retry_operation with 3 max attempts. Creates PrinterData tuple with all six values. Appends to global _printer_created list. Returns PrinterData object. This function orchestrates the complete virtual printer creation flow with robust retry logic for each API call.
functions.usage_example: printer = create_virtual_printer(); entity_id = printer.entity_id; cloud_id = printer.cloud_id

functions.what_it_does: Claims a virtual printer to a tenant account through the UCDE API with retry logic. Logs info message with model number and tenant ID. Constructs autoClaimRequest payload with productNumber, uuid, claimPostcard, fingerprint, and selectedBizModel (_biz_model). Logs truncated payload at debug level. Loops up to 10 attempts. Logs attempt number. Sends POST request to UCDE tropos devices endpoint for the tenant with JSON payload and Bearer token authorization. Logs response status and text at debug level. Raises exception if status is not successful. Logs success message and returns (exits function). If exception occurs, logs warning with attempt number and error. If this was the last attempt, logs error and re-raises exception. Logs retry message and sleeps 2 seconds before next attempt. This function handles the device claiming API call which associates a virtual printer with a user's tenant account, with robust retry logic to handle transient failures.

parameters.description: Tenant resource ID

functions.what_it_does: Adds a shipping address to a tenant account via the Gemini comfe API. Logs info message with tenant ID. Checks if _tenant_address is a list, if so extracts first element, otherwise uses it directly. Creates address_payload dictionary by filtering out any keys starting with 'fullState' from address_data. Logs address payload at debug level. Sends POST request to instantink comfe shipping addresses endpoint with tenantId query parameter, JSON payload, and Bearer token authorization. Logs response status and text at debug level. If response status is not 201, raises RuntimeError with status code. Logs success message. This function adds the shipping address required for subscription enrollment and cartridge shipments.

Orchestrates the complete flow of creating and claiming a virtual printer. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org token and tenant ID, logs success. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer. Unpacks the returned PrinterData tuple into entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint. Logs printer details with truncated postcard and fingerprint (first 8 chars). Calls claim_virtual_printer() with all required parameters. Logs success message. Sleeps 600 seconds (10 minutes) as temporary fix for printer availability issues due to known bugs III-2131 / IIGAR-2774. Returns the PrinterData object. This is a high-level convenience function that combines printer creation and claiming into a single operation with a workaround delay for backend synchronization issues.

Orchestrates the complete flow of creating and claiming a virtual printer plus adding shipping address. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org token and tenant ID, logs success. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer. Unpacks the returned PrinterData tuple into entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint. Logs printer details with truncated postcard and fingerprint. Calls claim_virtual_printer() with all required parameters. Logs printer claimed success. Calls add_address_to_tenant() with org token and tenant ID. Logs address added success. Logs info message about 10-minute wait for printer availability. Sleeps 600 seconds as temporary fix for backend synchronization issues. Returns the PrinterData object. This is the most complete setup function that prepares everything needed for enrollment testing including printer and address.

parameters.name: payment_type
parameters.description: Payment method identifier, defaults to global _payment_method or 'credit_card_master'

functions.what_it_does: Loads payment method test data from JSON file and processes dynamic expiration year. If payment_type is None, uses global _payment_method or defaults to 'credit_card_master'. Constructs path to payment_method_data.json file in same directory. Calls load_json_file() to load the JSON data. Checks if payment_type exists in data, if not raises ValueError with available types list. Creates a copy of the payment data for the specified type. Checks if 'expiration_year' key exists in payment data. If expiration_year value is a string starting with '+', extracts the numeric offset, gets current year from datetime, adds the offset, and replaces expiration_year with the calculated year as a string. Returns the processed payment data dictionary. This function provides dynamic expiration year calculation so test credit cards always have valid future expiration dates.

functions.what_it_does: Creates an OOBE (out-of-box experience) registration record in Agena for a printer. Constructs URL to Agena oobe_registrations endpoint. Creates headers with Version HTTP/1.0, Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=2, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL and payload. Sends POST request with headers and JSON payload. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Returns the response object. This creates the initial OOBE registration record that tracks the printer setup flow.

Locates the '.card-body' element within that parent. Returns the card body locator. This is a helper for extracting information from card-based UI layouts commonly used in admin interfaces.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts the last numeric value from the current page URL. Gets the current URL from page.url. Uses re.findall() with pattern r'\d+' to find all sequences of digits in the URL. Returns the last number from the matches list if any numbers found, otherwise returns None. This is useful for extracting IDs or other numeric identifiers from URLs like '/subscriptions/12345/details'.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text string to extract numbers from

functions.what_it_does: Extracts all numeric values (integers and decimals) from a text string. Returns empty list if text is None or empty. Strips whitespace from text. Uses re.findall() with pattern r'\d+\.\d+|\d+' to match decimal numbers (digits.digits) or integers (digits). Returns the list of matched number strings if any found, otherwise returns empty list. This is useful for parsing numeric values from UI text that may contain mixed content.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Printer serial number

parameters.name: cloud_id
parameters.description: Cloud ID

parameters.name: device_uuid
parameters.description: Device UUID

functions.what_it_does: Sends an RTP (Real-Time Printing) device status message to the G2 simulator for a virtual printer. This is an extremely complex function that constructs and sends printer telemetry data. Initializes counter values: bizmodelFlag=1409286144, lastValidConfigSeqNum=1, lastReceivedConfigSeqNum=1, lastValidCredentialSeqNum=1, supplyInsertionCount=1, totalImpressionCount=0, bizlogicImpressionCount=0, internalReportImpressionCount=0, snapshotCountersSum=0. Gets current UTC timestamp in ISO format. Defines crsSessionData_template as base64-encoded binary template. Defines field_map dictionary mapping field names to (offset, length, format) tuples for binary packing. Creates mutable bytearray from decoded template. Defines set_field() helper that packs values into the bytearray at specified offsets using struct.pack() for numeric fields or ASCII encoding for string fields. Calls set_field() to update totalImpressionCount, bizlogicImpressionCount, internalReportImpressionCount, snapshotCountersSum, and manufacturingDeviceUuid in the binary data. Base64 encodes the modified bytearray to create crsSessionData string. Defines decode_crssession() helper that unpacks the binary data to verify values. Calls decode_crssession() and logs the decoded values at debug level. Logs truncated crsSessionData at debug level. Creates replacements dictionary with all placeholder values including cloud_id, uuid, crsSessionData, counter values, timestamps, etc. Logs replacements at debug level. Constructs path to RTPDS-PayLoad-{_supplyvariant}.json template file based on supply variant (IIC or IPH). Raises FileNotFoundError if template doesn't exist. Reads template file content. Performs string replacement for all placeholders in template using {{key}} format. Parses the replaced template as JSON. Defines numeric_keys set containing field names that should be integers. Defines coerce_numerics() recursive function that converts string values to integers for numeric fields. Calls coerce_numerics() on the payload to ensure proper types. Logs the complete payload at debug level. Constructs URL to G2 simulator rtpdevicestatustriggers endpoint. Logs info message with entity_id and supplyvariant. Sends PUT request with JSON payload. Logs raw response at debug level. Logs response status and text at info level. Raises exception if response is not successful. Returns the response object. This function simulates printer telemetry reporting which triggers backend processing for supply levels, page counts, and subscription billing.

parameters.name: identifier_code
parameters.description: Offer identifier code from offers.json

functions.what_it_does: Generates an offer code from an offer identifier via Agena API. Calls get_org_aware_token() to get org token (only uses token, discards tenant_id). Constructs URL to Agena codes endpoint with identifier_code. Creates headers with Version HTTP/1.0, Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=1, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends POST request with headers. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Extracts code from posaCodes[0]['code'] path. Logs info message with generated code. Returns the code string. This converts an offer identifier into an actual offer code that can be used during enrollment.
functions.usage_example: code = offer_request('10232')  # Returns actual offer code like 'ABC123XYZ'

functions.method_signature: def get_offer(region: str, type: str = 'free', amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> dict

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Country code (e.g., 'US', 'DE', 'CA')

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Offer type - 'free' or 'prepaid'

parameters.name: amount_equal
parameters.description: Filter for exact amount in cents

parameters.name: amount_greater
parameters.description: Filter for amount greater than this value

parameters.name: amount_less
parameters.description: Filter for amount less than this value

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves an offer from OFFERS_DATA that matches the specified criteria. Raises ValueError if region is not provided. Raises ValueError if region is not found in OFFERS_DATA. Gets the offers array for the region. Iterates through each offer in the array. Checks if offer type matches the specified type, skips if not. If no amount filters are provided, returns the first matching offer immediately. Gets amountCents from offer, skips if not present. Converts amount to integer, logs warning and skips if conversion fails. Checks amount filters: skips if amount_equal specified and amount doesn't match, skips if amount_greater specified and amount is not greater, skips if amount_less specified and amount is not less. If all filters pass, returns the offer. If loop completes without finding a match, builds error message listing which filters were applied. Raises ValueError with descriptive message. This function provides flexible offer selection with multiple filtering options for test data.
functions.usage_example: offer = get_offer('US', type='prepaid', amount_equal=6000); offer_guid = offer['guid']

functions.method_signature: def get_offer_code(offer_type: str = 'free', amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> str

parameters.description: Filter for amount greater than this value

functions.what_it_does: Generates an offer code for the current test country based on offer criteria. Gets region from GlobalState.country_code. Raises ValueError if country_code is not set. Calls get_offer() with region, offer_type, and amount filters. Extracts identifier from the returned offer. Raises ValueError if identifier is not found in offer. Calls offer_request() with the identifier to generate the actual code. Logs info message with offer type, code, and region. Returns the generated code string. This is a high-level convenience function that combines offer selection and code generation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Captures user information by intercepting the /v3/usermgtsvc/users/me API request from the dashboard. Logs info message about waiting for the request. Uses page.expect_response() context manager with URL pattern '**/v3/usermgtsvc/users/me' and 120-second timeout. Inside the context, reloads the page to trigger the API request. Captures the response from the context manager. Gets the request object from the response. Attempts to parse response as JSON, if parsing fails logs warning and creates error dict. Builds captured_data dictionary with 'request' section containing method, url, headers dict, and body (post_data), and 'response' section containing status, status_text, headers dict, and data (parsed JSON or error). Prints the captured data. Returns the captured_data dictionary. If any exception occurs, logs warning and returns None. This function intercepts network traffic to extract authentication tokens and user data from API responses.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Makes an API request to /api/users/me using token captured from dashboard. Calls user_info_from_dashboard() to capture user data and token. Prints the user data. Extracts authorization header from request headers and removes 'Bearer ' prefix to get token. Constructs URL to instantink /api/users/me endpoint. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/json, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL and headers. Sends GET request with headers. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data dictionary. This function uses the captured token to make additional API calls for user information.

parameters.name: id

Constructs URL to instantink /api/users/subscriptions/{id} endpoint. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/json, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends GET request with headers. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data dictionary. This provides direct API access to subscription data using service-level authentication.

parameters.name: id

functions.what_it_does: Extracts subscription state from Gemini subscription data. Calls subscription_data_from_gemini() with the subscription ID. Attempts to navigate the nested dictionary path userSubInfo -> subscriptionInfo -> minSubInfo -> subscriptionState. Returns the subscriptionState value if found. If KeyError occurs (path doesn't exist), returns None. This provides a safe way to extract subscription state with error handling.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Extracts subscription state from dashboard /api/users/me response. Calls request_me_by_dashboard() to get user data. Attempts to navigate the nested dictionary path userMinSubInfo -> minSubInfo[0] -> subscriptionState. Returns the subscriptionState value if found. If KeyError occurs, returns None. This provides subscription state extraction from the user-level API response.

parameters.name: sub_id
parameters.description: Subscription ID

parameters.name: expected_state

functions.what_it_does: Validates that subscription reaches expected state within 60 seconds. Records start time. Enters loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. Calls get_subscription_state() with subscription ID. If state matches expected_state, logs info message and breaks out of loop. Sleeps 1 second before next check. After loop, asserts that state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError with descriptive message if not. This provides polling-based state validation with timeout.
functions.usage_example: validate_subscription_state('12345', 'ACTIVE')  # Polls until ACTIVE or 60 seconds

Calls get_subscription_state_by_me_from_dashboard() with page. If state matches expected_state, logs info message and breaks out of loop. Sleeps 1 second before next check. After loop, asserts that state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError if not. This provides polling-based state validation using the dashboard API.

parameters.name: entity_id

functions.what_it_does: Removes web services from a virtual printer via G2 simulator API. Logs info message with entity_id. Constructs URL to G2 simulator webservices endpoint for the printer. Sends DELETE request. Logs debug message with response status and text. If response status is not 200, raises RuntimeError with status code. Logs success message. This simulates disabling web services on a printer, which can be used to test offline scenarios.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Printer serial number

functions.what_it_does: Enables web services on a virtual printer via G2 simulator API. Logs info message with entity_id. Constructs URL to G2 simulator webservices endpoint for the printer. Sends PUT request. Logs debug message with response status and text. If response status is not 200, raises RuntimeError with status code. Logs success message. This simulates enabling web services on a printer after they were disabled.

Returns the list of ink colors for the current printer based on supply variant. If global _supplyvariant is 'IPH', returns ['CMY', 'K'] (Integrated Print Head with combined color cartridge and separate black). If _supplyvariant is 'IIC', returns ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] (Individual Ink Cartridges with separate cartridges for each color). Returns the colors list. This is used to determine the correct color array for supply level reporting and RTP payloads.

parameters.name: date_string
parameters.description: Date string in various formats

functions.what_it_does: Parses date strings in multiple formats into datetime objects. Defines list of date format strings including '%b %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM), '%Y/%m/%d %I:%M:%S %p' (2025/09/14 02:41:13 PM), '%d %b %Y, %H:%M:%S' (14 Sep 2025, 14:41:13), '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S' (14/09/2025 14:41:13), '%m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p' (09/14/2025 2:41:11 PM), '%B %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (September 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM). Iterates through each format. Attempts to parse date_string using datetime.strptime() with the format. If successful, returns the datetime object. If ValueError occurs, continues to next format. If all formats fail, raises ValueError with the original date string. This provides robust date parsing for various international date formats.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text containing a date string

functions.what_it_does: Extracts and parses a date from text containing mixed content. Cleans text by replacing multiple whitespace with single space and stripping. Defines list of regex patterns to match various date formats. Iterates through each pattern. Uses re.search() to find pattern in cleaned text. If match found, extracts the date string from group 1. Logs info message with extracted date string. Calls parse_flexible_date() to parse the date string. Returns the datetime object. If no pattern matches, raises ValueError with the cleaned text. This combines pattern matching and date parsing to extract dates from UI text.

Retrieves all Instant Ink plans data from Agena API for the current country. Constructs URL to Agena plans endpoint with country=_tenant_country_short, program=i_ink, and obsolete=false query parameters. Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get service token. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=3, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends GET request with headers. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data (array of plan objects). This retrieves the complete catalog of available plans for the test country.
functions.usage_example: plans = get_plans_data(); plan_100 = [p for p in plans if p['pages'] == 100][0]

Filters plans array to find plans where plan[key] equals value. If no matching plans found, raises ValueError with key and value. Returns the list of matching plan objects. This provides filtered plan selection for test data.

This retrieves yearly subscription plans which have different pricing and page allotments.

Filters plans array to find plans where plan[key] equals value. If no matching plans found, raises ValueError. Returns the list of matching plan objects. This provides filtered yearly plan selection.

Returns the expected billing status code based on payment method and gateway. Initializes status to None. If 'credit_card' in _payment_method, sets status to 'CPT-100'. If _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides status to 'STRATUS-PAYMENT-COMPLETE'. If 'direct_debit' in _payment_method, sets status to 'DIRECT-DEBIT-SUCCESS'. If 'paypal' in _payment_method, sets status to 'PAY-PAL-SUCCESS'. If 'prepaid' in _payment_method, sets status to 'PEGASUS-SUCCESS'. Returns the status string. This provides the expected billing status code for validation based on the payment method configuration.
functions.usage_example: expected_status = billing_status_code()  # Returns 'CPT-100' for credit card with PGS

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Whether this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund event response status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines refund_status_code dictionary mapping payment method types to full/partial refund status codes. For credit_card: full='PAYMENT-GATEWAY-REFUNDED', partial='PAYMENT-GATEWAY-PARTIAL-REFUNDED'. For direct_debit: full='DIRECT-DEBIT-REFUND-SUCCESS', partial='DIRECT-DEBIT-PARTIAL-REFUND-SUCCESS'. For paypal: full='PAY-PAL-REFUNDED', partial='PAY-PAL-REFUND-PARTIAL'. For prepaid_only: full='PEGASUS-REFUNDED', partial='PEGASUS-PARTIAL-REFUNDED'. Normalizes _payment_method to base type (credit_card, direct_debit, paypal, prepaid_only). Gets the refund codes for the payment method. Returns full or partial code based on full parameter. This provides expected refund event status codes for validation.

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Whether this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines refund_status_code dictionary mapping payment method types to full/partial refund statuses. For credit_card: full='FULL-REFUND', partial='PARTIAL-REFUND'. For direct_debit: full='DIRECT-DEBIT-REFUND', partial='PARTIAL-REFUNDED'. For paypal: full='PAY-PAL-REFUND', partial='PARTIAL-REFUND'. For prepaid_only: full='PEGASUS-REFUNDED', partial='PARTIAL-REFUND'. Normalizes _payment_method to base type. Gets the refund codes for the payment method. Returns full or partial code based on full parameter. This provides expected refund status codes for validation.

Returns the payment engine name based on payment method and gateway. Initializes payment_engine to None. If 'credit_card' in _payment_method, sets to 'payment_gateway'. If _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides to 'stratus'. If 'direct_debit' in _payment_method, sets to 'pgs_direct_debit'. If 'paypal' in _payment_method, sets to 'pgs_pay_pal'. If 'prepaid_only' in _payment_method, sets to 'pegasus'. Returns the payment_engine string. This identifies which payment processing system is used for the current payment method.
functions.usage_example: engine = payment_engine()  # Returns 'payment_gateway' or 'stratus' for credit cards

functions.what_it_does: Returns a list of countries that do not require state/province selection in address forms. Returns hardcoded list containing Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Luxembourg, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Singapore, Sweden, Switzerland. This is used to determine whether state field should be required in address validation.

Returns a list of countries that support direct debit payment method. Returns hardcoded list containing Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Spain. This is used to determine whether direct debit payment option should be available for the test country.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: locator
parameters.description: Playwright Locator object

functions.what_it_does: Checks if an element is actionable by verifying it is the topmost element at its center point. Wraps logic in try-except to return False on any exception. Gets bounding box of the locator. Returns False if bounding box is None (element not visible). Calculates center point coordinates: center_x = x + width/2, center_y = y + height/2. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that calls document.elementFromPoint(x, y) to get the topmost element at the center point. Checks if the element at that point is either the target element itself or contains the target element. Returns the boolean result. This is useful for checking if an element is actually clickable or if it's obscured by another element.

parameters.name: json_string

functions.what_it_does: Parses a JSON string into a Python object with error handling. Wraps json.loads() in try-except. If parsing succeeds, returns the parsed object. If exception occurs, logs error message with exception details and re-raises the exception. This provides JSON parsing with logging for debugging.

This retrieves printer configuration data for the specified model and country.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts comprehensive font and text styling details from an element. Calls element.evaluate() with JavaScript function that gets computed styles. Extracts font-family, font-size, font-weight, font-style, line-height, letter-spacing, text-decoration, text-transform, color, font-variant, text-align, background-color, and fill CSS properties. functions.usage_example: font_details = get_font_details(heading_element); assert font_details['font-family'] == 'Forma DJR UI'

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes font family values for comparison by extracting the first font name. Returns value unchanged if it's None or empty. Splits value by comma to handle font stacks. Takes the first font name. Removes double quotes and single quotes. Strips whitespace. Returns the normalized font name. This handles font family values like '"Forma DJR UI", Arial, sans-serif' and returns just 'Forma DJR UI'.
functions.usage_example: normalized = _normalize_font_family('"Forma DJR UI", Arial')  # Returns 'Forma DJR UI'

parameters.name: landing_page
parameters.description: Landing page object with footer element attributes

functions.what_it_does: Validates footer element fonts and styling against expected values. Defines checks list with tuples of (attribute_name, expected_styles_dict, label). For each check: gets element from landing_page using getattr, asserts element is not None, calls get_font_details() to get actual styles, iterates through expected properties, normalizes font-family values using _normalize_font_family(), asserts actual equals expected with descriptive error message, logs success message. Checks include footer_section background color, footer_hp_link font, footer_privacy_statement font, footer_terms_of_use font, footer_cookies font, footer_privacy_choice font, footer_iitos font, and footer_copyright font. All footer links expect Forma DJR UI font family, 16px size, and 20px or 24px line height. This provides comprehensive footer styling validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: new_account_auth_option
parameters.description: Authentication option for new accounts - 'use_password' or 'send_sign_in_code'

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account with support for both password and OTP authentication flows. This is an enhanced version of create_ii_v2_account with more robust handling of different authentication paths. Tests browser connectivity with about:blank. Navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Accepts privacy banner if present. Locates and clicks sign-up button. Clicks create-account button. Checks if password screen appears first (sometimes happens), if so navigates back to email screen using back button. Fills email field with _tenant_email. Clicks next button. Detects whether this is new email flow (firstName field appears) or existing email flow. For new email flow: fills firstName and lastName, validates new_account_auth_option parameter, if 'send_sign_in_code' clicks that button, if 'use_password' clicks use password button and fills password then clicks create button, waits for OTP code field, fetches verification code with 3 retries, fills code and submits, waits for stepper element. For existing email flow: clicks use password button, fills password, clicks sign-in button, waits 5 seconds for stabilization. Navigates to portalshell URL. Waits 60 seconds. Clicks side menu toggle if present. Clicks HP Instant Ink menu item. Returns page object. This function handles multiple authentication flows including new account with password, new account with OTP, and existing account login.

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: HTML content of verification email

functions.what_it_does:
Extracts 6-digit verification code from email HTML using multiple fallback strategies. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse HTML. First tries to find

element with class 'code' and returns its text if found. If not found, joins all stripped text strings from the HTML. Tries multiple regex patterns in order: 'one-time passcode (OTP): (\d{6})', 'one-time passcode.*?\b(\d{6})\b', 'The OTP will expire.*?\b(\d{6})\b', '\b(\d{6})\b' (any 6-digit number). For each pattern, uses re.search() with DOTALL flag. If match found, returns the captured 6-digit code. If no pattern matches, raises RuntimeError. This is more robust than extract_verification_code as it handles multiple email formats and has fallback patterns.

classes.class_purpose: Manages multiple browser instances with support for remote debugging, incognito mode, and normal browser sessions. Provides comprehensive window management including switching between browsers, finding windows by URL, waiting for new windows, bringing windows to foreground, and handling cross-platform window focus operations. Maintains a registry of all managed browsers with their metadata and provides utilities for listing, switching, and closing browsers.

classes.docstring:
A flexible test step executor that can handle various action types
    and dynamic step configurations from YAML files.
classes.class_purpose: Core test execution engine that interprets and executes YAML-based test definitions. Loads JSON metadata files containing UI element definitions, manages test variables with substitution support, executes various action types (click, set, verify, navigate, scroll, etc.), handles iframe switching, performs AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision models, manages printer selection workflows, tracks execution logs, and generates CSV result reports. Supports multiple metadata sources (auth_controls, hpsmart, instantink, oss_emulator) and provides comprehensive error handling and logging.

Custom exception class for flow configuration errors. Raised when there are issues with flow setup such as missing flow folders, invalid locale configurations, missing addresses, or printer profile resolution failures. Extends the built-in Exception class to provide specific error handling for flow configuration problems.

Manages the complete lifecycle of Playwright browser automation including browser launch, context creation, page initialization, and cleanup. Handles browser type selection (chromium, firefox, webkit) based on target platform configuration. Configures browser context with locale, viewport dimensions, device scale factor, HAR logging, video recording, and proxy settings. Implements context manager protocol (__enter__ and __exit__) for automatic resource cleanup. Provides centralized browser management for all test flows.

Data class representing a single test step in the execution report. Stores all information about a step including its ID, source file location, expected pattern, execution status, logged message, timestamp, duration, execution order, and occurrence count. Used to track both expected steps (extracted from source code) and dynamically discovered steps (logged during execution but not pre-scanned).
classes.how_to_instantiate: step = ReportStep(id=1, file='test.py', line=10, pattern='Login successful', status='PASS')

classes.class_purpose: Custom logging handler that intercepts INFO-level log messages from framework_logger and forwards them to ReportsManager for step execution tracking. Filters out non-INFO messages and calls reporter.record_step_execution() for each INFO message. This is the mechanism that connects logging statements in test code to the step tracking system.

Main reporting engine that orchestrates the entire test reporting lifecycle. Scans test flow source code using AST parsing to extract expected framework_logger.info() calls as potential steps. Installs a custom log handler to intercept actual log messages during test execution. Matches logged messages to expected steps using pattern matching and source location tracking. Tracks step execution order, status, duration, and retry attempts. Captures complete console output by redirecting stdout and stderr. Generates comprehensive HTML reports with color-coded step status, execution timeline, duration metrics, and embedded console logs. Also generates JSON companion reports for programmatic analysis.

Inner utility class that implements a tee-like stream wrapper to duplicate output to both the original stream (stdout/stderr) and a StringIO buffer. Used to capture console output while still displaying it in real-time. Implements write() and flush() methods to mirror the file-like interface.
classes.how_to_import: Not intended for external import - inner class of ReportsManager

classes.inner_class_purpose: Implements a tee-like stream wrapper to duplicate output to both the original stream and a capture buffer

classes.class_purpose: Container class for framework-wide constants including directory paths, file paths, Excel column headers, color codes for Excel cell styling, timeout values, retry counts, and sets of HTML tags to ignore during scraping. All members are class-level constants that are accessed throughout the framework without instantiation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: Constants is not instantiated - access constants directly like Constants.ROOT_DIR

classes.class_purpose: Singleton-like class that stores runtime configuration state for the current test execution. Holds all configuration parameters including stack, locale, language, printer profile, easy enroll flag, business model, target platform, region, country, country code, language code, address payload, selected printer SKU, tenant email, flow name, timestamp, mode, run directory, headless flag, payment method, simulator platform, log collection settings, and requirements dictionary. Provides a reset() class method to clear all state between test runs.
classes.how_to_instantiate: GlobalState is not instantiated - access state directly like GlobalState.locale

classes.class_purpose: Configuration initialization class that populates GlobalState from command-line arguments and loads supporting data from JSON files. Provides the init() class method that takes keyword arguments, resets GlobalState, populates it with provided values, loads locale information from locale_map.json, loads address data from address_data.json, resolves printer SKU from printer_profiles.json based on region and easy_enroll settings, and sets any additional keyword arguments as GlobalState attributes.
classes.how_to_instantiate: Config is not instantiated - call Config.init(**kwargs) to initialize configuration

classes.class_purpose: Custom dictionary subclass that allows attribute-style access to dictionary keys. Enables accessing dictionary values using dot notation (e.g., elements.username_input) instead of bracket notation (e.g., elements['username_input']). Raises AttributeError if a key does not exist when accessed as an attribute.

Static helper class providing utility methods for testing subscription cancellation flows. Contains methods for selecting cancellation feedback options (both ink and paper), verifying cancellation UI elements and sections, validating cancellation timeline steps and dates, handling plan changes during cancellation, managing subscription restoration, testing pause plan functionality, and validating various cancellation-related features. All methods are static and do not require instantiation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: CancellationPlanHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call CancellationPlanHelper.select_cancellation_feedback_option(page, 'I do not print enough') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Comprehensive static helper class providing utility methods for all dashboard-related test operations. Contains methods for dashboard access and navigation, first-time setup with modal handling, status card and plan details verification, progress bar validation, plan management (pause/resume), billing and shipping address operations, payment method handling for multiple payment types (credit cards via PGS and 2CO, PayPal, Google Pay, Apple Pay, direct debit), printer selection and management, footer link validation, tour modal handling, notification verification, savings calculator checks, session management, and various dashboard UI interactions. All methods are static and do not require instantiation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: DashboardHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call DashboardHelper.access(page, 'user@example.com') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for email verification operations in test automation. Contains methods to check if emails with specific subjects have been received, retrieve email messages by subject, and extract detailed email content including subject, pre-header, and HTML body. Uses Gmail API through the common module's authenticate_gmail function to access and search emails.
classes.how_to_instantiate: EmailHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like EmailHelper.sees_email_with_subject(email, subject)

classes.class_purpose: Comprehensive helper class providing static utility methods for automating HP Instant Ink enrollment flows. Contains over 100 methods covering all enrollment stages including terms acceptance, printer selection, plan selection with multiple plan types (ink-only, ink+paper, monthly, yearly, pay-as-you-print), shipping address management with validation and editing, billing information with support for multiple payment methods (PGS credit/debit cards, 2CO cards, PayPal, Google Pay, Direct Debit), promotional code application (prepaid, EK codes, RAF codes, promo codes), enrollment completion with automatic renewal notice handling, and extensive validation methods for UI elements, modals, forms, and data persistence. Supports multiple enrollment variants including standard web flow, OOBE (Out of Box Experience), MOOBE (Mobile OOB), and FLIP enrollment.
classes.how_to_instantiate: EnrollmentHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like EnrollmentHelper.start_enrollment(page)

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with the Fulfillment Service Rails Admin interface in test automation. Contains methods for authenticating and accessing the Rails Admin, searching for and navigating to specific orders by trigger ID, validating order status and shipping fields, sending orders to the fulfillment system, and updating order error codes for testing error scenarios. Extends functionality from RABaseHelper for common Rails Admin operations.
classes.how_to_instantiate: FulfillmentServiceRAHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods like FulfillmentServiceRAHelper.access(page)

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with the Fulfillment Simulator Rails Admin interface in test automation. Contains methods for accessing the simulator Rails Admin, searching for orders by trigger ID, updating order status and tracking information to simulate shipping events, and orchestrating complete order processing workflows including triggering background jobs. Used to simulate fulfillment operations in test environments without actual physical shipping.
classes.how_to_instantiate: FulfillmentSimulatorRAHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods like FulfillmentSimulatorRAHelper.access(page)

classes.class_purpose: Comprehensive helper class providing static utility methods for interacting with the Gemini Rails Admin interface in test automation. Contains over 60 methods covering tenant management (accessing tenant pages, subscriptions), subscription operations (state transitions, obsoleting, RPL updates, free months management), billing cycle operations (accessing cycles, calculating page tallies, submitting charges, refunds, status updates), payment credential management (PGS, PayPal, Direct Debit), printer operations (setting offline, registering devices, page count updates), notification verification, cartridge shipping, event shifting for time-based testing, and extensive validation methods for verifying Rails Admin field values. Extends RABaseHelper functionality for common Rails Admin operations.
classes.how_to_instantiate: GeminiRAHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods like GeminiRAHelper.access(page)

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for HPID authentication operations including dismissing country selector popups, performing sign-in with email and password, completing full sign-in flows, signing up new users with account details, confirming account verification codes, and completing full sign-up flows. All methods are static and work with Playwright page instances and HPIDPage page objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: HPIDHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password)

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for landing page test automation including opening and accessing the landing page with privacy banner handling, verifying RAF (Refer-a-Friend) banners and footnotes, validating section visibility in viewport, testing consumer landing page OOBE and MOBE flows with navigation and section scrolling, validating hyperlinks in footer, testing cookie settings functionality, validating FAQ links and content, selecting countries, and validating emergency banners with different color schemes and icons for various countries. All methods are static and designed for use in test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: LandingPageHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like LandingPageHelper.access(page)

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for OSS emulator operations including navigating to the emulator URL, signing in with HPID, clicking 3-month button, filling printer configuration details with base64 decoding, selecting app types, starting flows, setting up complete OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting connected printing services agreements, declining HP Plus offers, continuing through dynamic security notices and value propositions, selecting printer replacement options, activating HP Plus, selecting countries, and verifying reminder checkboxes and trial text. All methods are static and work with Playwright page instances and OssEmulatorPage page objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OssEmulatorHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OssEmulatorHelper.setup_oss_emulator(page, printer)

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for overview page operations including verifying cartridge installation messages for single or multiple cartridge types, checking the number of printers in status-grouped sections, verifying plan suggestion banners for downgrading or upgrading, validating plan upgrade information, verifying unsubscribed status card elements, testing HP shop ink link navigation, testing enroll printer again functionality with enrollment flow, and downloading past invoices as PDF files. All methods are static and designed for use in overview page test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OverviewHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge)

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for print history page operations including verifying previous billing cycle periods, selecting specific billing cycle options by index, verifying billing table data with page counts, normalizing currency values for comparison, validating currency fields with partial or exact matching, verifying complete billing section details against expected values dictionaries, validating all billing cycles data from a list of expected data rows, validating FAQ questions and links with navigation testing, validating no-pens print history page elements, downloading all invoices with retry logic, verifying notification text in print history table, verifying plan information with value and page counts, and verifying current total values. All methods are static and designed for detailed print history and billing validation scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: PrintHistoryHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like PrintHistoryHelper.verify_billing_table_data(page, total_pages_printed)

classes.class_purpose: Provides a comprehensive set of static utility methods for interacting with Resque job scheduler, managing background job execution, navigating application pages and menus, extracting data from page elements by title, accessing links within titled sections, validating boolean field values, and validating template data with date/time comparisons. This helper class encapsulates common patterns for Resque job orchestration and page element interaction that are reused across multiple test scenarios. All methods are static so no instantiation is required.
classes.how_to_instantiate: RABaseHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call RABaseHelper.wait_page_to_load(page, page_title) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for verifying shipment tracking page elements and cartridge installation messages. Contains methods for validating that specific cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY) are displayed as installed, handling multiple cartridge type scenarios with fallback logic, verifying all printer color cartridges are shown, clicking the view entire history link, and validating the shipment history table contains expected cartridge counts. All methods are static and work with the ShipmentTrackingPage page object to locate and verify cartridge-related elements.
classes.how_to_instantiate: ShipmentTrackingHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call ShipmentTrackingHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, 'k,c') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for managing subscription plan updates, upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations. Contains methods for verifying free trial visibility, validating available plan cards with expected page counts and pricing, verifying current plan selection, selecting plans by page count or position, clicking plan selection buttons, canceling subscriptions with confirmation flow, validating terms of service page navigation, verifying plan change messages on banners, undoing plan changes through modal interactions, and verifying plan information details. All methods are static and work with UpdatePlanPage, CancellationPage, and OverviewPage page objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: UpdatePlanHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call UpdatePlanHelper.verify_available_plans(page, [10, 25, 50]) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Base class for defining element locators. Page object classes create an inner Elements class that extends this base class and defines locator strings as class attributes. This class serves as a marker/base type for the locator pattern and does not contain any methods or attributes itself. Child classes add selector strings as class-level attributes which are then accessed through the BasePageObject's dynamic attribute pattern.
classes.how_to_instantiate: ElementLocators is typically not instantiated directly. Page objects create inner classes that extend it: class Elements(ElementLocators): login_button = '#login-btn'

classes.class_purpose: Proxy class that provides convenient wait operations for page elements. When accessed through BasePageObject's wait attribute, it intercepts attribute access and returns a lambda function that calls page.wait_for_selector with the corresponding selector string from the elements object. This allows page objects to use syntax like 'self.wait.login_button(timeout=5000)' instead of 'self.page.wait_for_selector(self.elements.login_button, timeout=5000)', making wait operations more concise and readable.

Base class for all page objects in the framework providing the core locator management pattern. Stores the Playwright Page instance, creates an ElementLocators instance, and creates a WaitProxy instance. Implements __getattr__ magic method to provide dynamic attribute access - when an attribute is accessed that matches a selector name in the elements object, it automatically returns a Playwright locator object for that selector. Provides get_selector method to retrieve raw selector strings. This architecture allows page objects to define selectors as simple string attributes and access them as Playwright locators without manual locator creation code.

Page object representing the cancellation banner component that displays when a user has scheduled their subscription for cancellation. Provides locators for all interactive elements within the cancellation banner including the see cancellation timeline link, keep subscription button, and the subscription resume confirmation modal with its resume and back buttons. Also includes locator for the subscription resumed success banner that appears after resuming.

Comprehensive page object representing all screens and components in the subscription cancellation flow. Encapsulates locators for printer information display, cancellation summary card with animation, confirm cancellation and keep enrollment buttons, cancellation retention section with alternative options (change plan, pause plan, transfer subscription, contact support, pay-as-you-print), change plan modal with all available plan buttons (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, 1500 pages), cancellation feedback survey with multiple radio button options for cancellation reasons, paper subscription cancellation elements, and various modal and navigation elements. Provides a utility method to dynamically retrieve plan buttons based on page count.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the HP Smart Dashboard page. Provides locators for signup buttons, promotional page elements, footer navigation links, subscription state indicators, printer management options, and enrollment links. This page object is used to interact with the main dashboard interface where users can manage their HP Instant Ink subscriptions, view printer status, and access various account features. Extends BasePageObject to leverage the elements attribute pattern for locator storage.

Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the dashboard side navigation menu. Provides locators for menu toggle button, main menu items (Instant Ink, Overview, Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking, My Account, Printers), and submenu items (Shipping & Billing, Notifications). Contains intelligent click methods that automatically handle parent menu expansion, mobile layout menu expansion, and retry logic for menu items that may be initially hidden. This page object handles the complexity of responsive menu behavior where menu items may be collapsed on mobile or nested under parent menu items.

Encapsulates UI elements for the Gemini Admin page, which appears to be an internal administrative interface for HP Instant Ink support or operations teams. Provides locators for viewing item details (titles and content) and performing administrative actions like processing refunds. The page uses a combination of definition list (dl/dt/dd) elements and card-based layout (.card-header, .card-body) to display information. This page object would be used in admin-focused test scenarios or support workflow automation.

Encapsulates all UI elements for HP ID authentication flows including sign-in, account creation, and email verification. Provides locators for form input fields, submit buttons, validation helper text elements, and page-level containers. This page object handles the HP ID authentication system which is used across HP services. Contains locators for both the sign-in flow (username, password, sign-in button) and the registration flow (first name, last name, email, password, country selector, create account button). Also includes locators for the email verification code entry step and form validation helper text that appears when fields have errors.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the HP Instant Ink public landing page, which is the main marketing and enrollment entry point for the service. Provides extensive locator coverage for all page sections including header with sign-in/sign-up buttons and country picker, hero section with get started button, how it works section with step descriptions, plan section with ink plan cards and navigation arrows, FAQs section with accordion tabs, go green section, refer-a-friend section, paper delivery add-on section, footnotes section, footer with legal links, alert banners, enrollment kit banner, country selector modal, and mobile responsive menu elements. Contains helper methods for dynamically accessing plan cards by index and property decorators for elements that need special handling. This is the most comprehensive page object in the framework covering the primary customer-facing landing page.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Notifications page. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns. Defines locators for the notifications table, table rows, notifications box, notifications icon, and notifications table using data-testid attributes. Provides a property method to access the first matching notifications table element for reading notification data in tests.

Encapsulates all UI elements and controls for the OSS Emulator testing interface. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines 50+ locators organized by functional sections: Dev Tab for login and authentication, Quick Options for preset configurations, Device Tab for printer hardware settings, App Tab for application type selection, Page Elements for flow control, Offers Elements for HP Plus and subscription offers, and Post-OOBE Elements for post-setup configuration. Used extensively in test automation to simulate printer setup flows without requiring physical printer hardware.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the main Overview/Dashboard page of the Instant Ink application. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines 150+ locators organized into functional sections: enrollment and printer replacement, status card elements, printer information and selector, printer details modal, ink cartridge status, plan details and billing, promo codes and special offers, first access and onboarding elements, privacy settings, ink cartridge items, pause/resume plan functionality, tour tooltips, alert messages, footer links, unsubscribed status, support card, PaaS banner, emergency banners, paper subscription elements, and various action buttons. Provides getter methods and properties for accessing frequently used elements like page title, change plan link, printer name, and RAF code. This is the most complex page object in the framework due to the comprehensive nature of the overview page.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the version 3 plan selector page where users choose their Instant Ink subscription plan. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines locators for the plan selector combobox, listbox, plan options, ink-and-paper button, ink-only button, content area, continue button, and header elements including virtual agent link, support phone number, country selector, and account button. Also defines locators for pay-as-you-print and monthly plan cards. Provides a select_plan method that takes a plan value (like '10', '50', '100') and clicks the corresponding plan option using an XPath selector with logging.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Print History page where users view their printing activity and billing information. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines 40+ locators organized into sections: page elements (title, card, links), progress bars (plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, additional pages), cards (plan details, FAQ), FAQ questions and answers (4 questions with expandable answers), FAQ links (terms of service, overview, update plan), table elements (history table, date column, description column, invoice links, billing cycle selector), and various text elements displaying usage statistics and billing amounts. Provides two property methods (faq_update_plan_link and plan_price_text) for convenient access to frequently used elements with .first filtering.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the Printer Replacement page where users can set up a new printer to replace their current printer. Provides locators for the main page container, printer setup buttons (both affirmative and negative options), back navigation button, and card elements displaying current and new printer information. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the self.elements attribute pattern.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the Printer Selection page where users can select a printer from available options or add a new printer. Provides locators for the main page container, printer radio button cards with input elements, continue button, add printer card with its components (image, title, body text, checkbox), printer details (image, serial number, benefits), carousel navigation arrows (back and next), printer offline error message, and connectivity guide link. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the self.elements attribute pattern.

Encapsulates UI elements and user interactions for the Printer Updates page where users can accept or configure automatic printer updates. Provides a locator for the continue button and an action method to accept automatic updates by clicking the continue button. Sets a custom default timeout of 60000 milliseconds (60 seconds) for all page operations to accommodate potentially slow update-related operations. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the self.elements attribute pattern.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the Printers management page where users can view their printers, check printer online status, access printer options, remove printers, enroll in Instant Ink subscription service, and manage subscriptions. Provides locators for printer online status indicator (check circle icon), printer options menu button, remove printer button, enroll in Instant Ink button, printer box containers for both enrolled and non-enrolled printers (using :has() pseudo-class to differentiate), and modal dialog buttons for confirming or closing printer removal action. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the self.elements attribute pattern.

Encapsulates UI elements and user interactions for the Privacy Banner component that appears to request user consent for cookies and privacy policies. Provides a locator for the accept button using OneTrust cookie consent framework ID selector and an action method to accept the privacy banner by clicking the accept button. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the self.elements attribute pattern. This is typically used in test setup to dismiss the privacy banner before proceeding with test steps.

Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipment Tracking page where users can view the status of their ink cartridge and paper shipments. Contains locators for different cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), paper tracking items, FAQ sections with expandable questions and answers, shipment history table with date/description/tracking number columns, and support links for recycling cartridges and reporting missing ink. Provides methods to wait for page load, access specific FAQ questions by index, and retrieve element selectors dynamically. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns.

Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipping and Billing management page where users can add, edit, and manage their shipping addresses and payment methods. Contains locators organized into three sections: shipping section (manage address link, form modal, address fields, validation messages, save/cancel buttons), billing section (manage payment link, billing form modal, payment method fields, PayPal logo, credit card inputs, iframe elements), and page-level elements (page title, notification messages, modal close buttons). Provides a static method to select business account type during billing setup. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure.

Encapsulates all UI elements for the Sign In page where users authenticate to access the application. Contains locators for the email/username input field, the button to proceed to password entry, the password input field, and the final sign-in submit button. This page object represents a two-step sign-in flow where users first enter their email/username, then click to proceed to password entry, then enter their password and submit. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure. Also imports test_flows_common module which likely contains reusable test flow helper functions.

Encapsulates all UI elements for the Thank You confirmation page that appears after users complete important workflows like printer replacement, subscription enrollment, or order completion. Contains locators for the continue button to proceed to the next step, the thank you card container displaying confirmation information, a modal close button for finishing printer replacement workflows, the email address display showing where confirmation was sent, cartridge images showing what was ordered or configured, and subtitle text with additional confirmation details. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure. This page serves as a confirmation and transition point in multi-step workflows.

Encapsulates the UI element for the Terms of Service HP Smart consent page where users must accept terms and conditions before proceeding with HP Smart application setup or usage. Contains a single locator for the continue button that users click to accept the terms of service and move forward in the onboarding or setup flow. This is a simple consent page with minimal interaction - users review the terms and click continue to accept. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and the elements dictionary structure. The page is part of the initial setup or first-time user experience flow.

Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Update Plan page where users can view their current plan details, change to different page count plans (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, 1500 pages), upgrade or downgrade plans, pause or resume plans, cancel Instant Ink subscription, manage paper add-ons, update shipping and billing information, and redeem prepaid codes. Contains extensive locator definitions organized by functional areas including plan details card, plan selection cards, downgrade/upgrade modals, pause plan controls, header links, and page elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page interaction patterns.

Named tuple class for storing virtual printer data returned from printer creation operations. Extends tuple to provide both indexed access and named attribute access to printer information including entity_id (serial number), model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard (claim code), and fingerprint. Used as the return type for create_virtual_printer() function to encapsulate all printer identification data needed for claiming and enrollment operations.
classes.how_to_instantiate: printer = PrinterData(entity_id='ABC123', model_number='1G5L3A', device_uuid='uuid-string', cloud_id='cloud-id-string', postcard='postcard-base64', fingerprint='fingerprint-base64')

Footer link that navigates to the main HP.com website. Located in the footer section of the dashboard page.
Footer link that navigates to the HP Wireless Print Center for printer setup and wireless printing configuration.

Item title elements on the admin page. Uses a composite selector to match either definition list term elements (dl dt) or card header elements (.card-header). These display the titles or labels for various data items shown on the admin interface.

Item content elements on the admin page. Uses a composite selector to match either definition list description elements (dl dd) or card body elements (.card-body). These display the actual content or values for various data items shown on the admin interface.

Refund payment button or link on the admin page that allows administrators to process payment refunds for customer accounts. This is a child anchor element within a container with class refund_payment_member_link.
Helper text element that appears below the first name input field to display validation error messages when the first name is invalid or missing.
Helper text element that appears below the last name input field to display validation error messages when the last name is invalid or missing.
Helper text element that appears below the email input field to display validation error messages when the email format is invalid or the email is already registered.
Helper text element that appears below the password input field to display validation error messages when the password does not meet security requirements or is missing.

Country selector button within the country selector buttons container. This button opens a dropdown or modal to select the user's country during account creation.
Username or email input field on the HP ID sign-in form where users enter their HP ID email address or username.

Username form submit button that advances to the password entry step after the username has been entered. This is used in multi-step sign-in flows where username and password are entered on separate screens.
Password input field on the HP ID sign-in form where users enter their account password. This is a masked input field of type password.
Sign in submit button on the HP ID sign-in form that submits the authentication request with the entered username and password.
